Q: Best way to get 100% Hosidius favour for Tithe Farm? 
A: Do the favour tasks in order: 
Help in the kitchen (cook stews) — gives Hosidius favour 
Do woodland tasks (chop trees, give logs to forester) — gives Hosidius favour 
Do messroom tasks (clean up messhall) — gives Hosidius favour 
Takes about 3-5 hours total for 100% favour.

A: It depends on GE prices (which fluctuate daily). Generally: 
Snapdragon (62 Farming): Highest consistent profit (~500K-800K GP/run) 
Ranarr (32 Farming): Good profit, cheaper seeds (~300K-500K GP/run)

1. Hitpoints Overview & Unique Mechanics 
Hitpoints (HP) is the only skill in OSRS you cannot directly train .
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Guide (2026) – Best Training Methods & XP Rates

text:
ts Overview & Unique Mechanics 
Hitpoints (HP) is the only skill in OSRS you cannot directly train . Every 1 damage you deal to an enemy gives 1.33 Hitpoints XP. This means Hitpoints trains alongside your combat skills — it's the ultimate "passive" skill. 
Why Hitpoints Matters 
Benefit Details 
Survival Every HP level = +1 maximum hitpoints. At 99, you have 99 base HP. 
Boss Viability Higher HP means you can survive longer at bosses without eating, increasing kills per trip.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Guide (2026) – Best Training Methods & XP Rates

text:

PvP Advantage HP level determines your combat level. Higher HP = higher combat = stronger in PvP brackets. 
Quest Requirements HP is not directly required, but many quests require combat levels that need high HP to achieve. 
Hitpoints Cape (99) Doubles natural HP regeneration rate (2 HP/min → 4 HP/min). Excellent for AFK training.

text:
ts Cape (99) Doubles natural HP regeneration rate (2 HP/min → 4 HP/min). Excellent for AFK training. 
The 1.33:1 Ratio 
In OSRS, combat XP is distributed as follows: 
Combat Skill Used Combat XP per Damage Hitpoints XP per Damage Ratio 
Attack, Strength, Defence (Melee) 4 XP 1.33 XP 3:1 
Ranged 4 XP 1.33 XP 3:1 
Ranged (Longrange style) 2 Ranged + 2 Defence 1.33 XP 3:1 
Magic (combat spells) 2 Magic + 1.33 HP 1.33 XP 3:2

text:

Magic (combat spells) 2 Magic + 1.33 HP 1.33 XP 3:2 
Magic (defensive casting) 1.33 Magic + 1.33 Defence + 1.33 HP 1.33 XP 1:1:1 
Key insight: Hitpoints gains XP at 1/3 the rate of your main combat skills. By the time you reach 99 Attack/Strength, you'll have ~13M Hitpoints XP (level 98-99).

2. XP Formula — The Math Behind Hitpoints 
Understanding exactly how Hitpoints XP works helps you optimize your training: 
The Formula 
Hitpoints XP = Damage dealt × 1.33 
This means: 
A max hit of 25 gives 33.25 HP XP (plus 100 combat XP) 
Higher max hits = faster HP training 
Using better weapons and gear directly improves your HP XP/hr
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Guide (2026) – Best Training Methods & XP Rates

text:
igher max hits = faster HP training 
Using better weapons and gear directly improves your HP XP/hr 
Overkill damage (hitting 25 on a 10 HP enemy) still gives full XP 
XP Distribution Settings 
In your combat settings (the crossed-swords icon), you control XP distribution: 
Accurate (Attack XP): 4 Attack XP + 1.33 HP XP per damage 
Aggressive (Strength XP): 4 Strength XP + 1.33 HP XP per damage 
Defensive (Defence XP): 4 Defence XP + 1.33 HP XP per damage
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Guide (2026) – Best Training Methods & XP Rates

text:
Strength XP + 1.33 HP XP per damage 
Defensive (Defence XP): 4 Defence XP + 1.33 HP XP per damage 
Controlled: 1.33 Attack + 1.33 Strength + 1.33 Defence + 1.33 HP per damage 
Note: Controlled gives the same total combat XP (4 per damage) but spreads it across all three melee skills equally.

Unlike other skills, very few quests directly reward Hitpoints XP. Most combat XP from quests goes into Attack, Strength, Defence, Ranged, or Magic — not HP.

The Nightmare Zone (NMZ) is the best AFK Hitpoints training method in OSRS. You can train for 15-20 minutes with zero interaction (using absorption potions) or use Dharok's for max XP rates.

AFK Method (Normal) 
Gear: Your best melee gear (proselyte + whip, or full obsidian + berserker necklace) 
Inventory: 4-6 overload potions, rest absorption potions 
Prayer: None — use absorption potions only

10. Ironman Hitpoints Strategy 
Hitpoints on an Ironman works the same way — it's passive. Because Ironmen can't use NMZ for AFK training (no absorption pots without Herblore), the strategy differs. 
Early Game (1-60) 
Train on Sand Crabs / Ammonite Crabs — no supplies needed, safe death mechanics 
Do all combat quests (Waterfall, Fight Arena, Vampyre Slayer, Grand Tree) for free XP

Train HP Through Slayer: It's the most efficient approach. You need Slayer for PvM anyway — don't waste time at NMZ or crabs unless you need to AFK. 
Don't Splash Magic: Splashing gives zero HP XP.

14. FAQ 
Q: How long does 1-99 Hitpoints take? 
Since HP is passive, it depends on your combat training. Rough estimates: If training combat at 60K-100K XP/hr, expect 13M HP XP after ~150-200 hours of combat.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Guide (2026) – Best Training Methods & XP Rates

gh estimates: If training combat at 60K-100K XP/hr, expect 13M HP XP after ~150-200 hours of combat. Pure HP grinding (chins) can be done in ~65-130 hours. 
Q: Will I hit 99 HP before 99 Attack/Strength/Defence? 
If you train ALL three melee skills to 99, you'll have ~39M HP XP (far past 99). If you train only one combat style to 99 (e.g., 99 Ranged), 99 Ranged = ~13M combat XP = ~13M HP XP = about level 98-99 HP.

Q: What's the fastest way to train Hitpoints? 
Black Chinchompas at Maniacal Monkeys (MM2 tunnels): 100K-200K HP XP/hr. This is the absolute fastest method but costs money (chins are expensive) and requires active play. 
Q: What's the most AFK way to train Hitpoints? 
Nightmare Zone with absorption potions: 20 minutes of zero-click AFK for ~25K HP XP/hr.

OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Training Guide 2026 
Skill Training Hitpoints Combat Skill Updated 2026 
Hitpoints (also called Constitution) is your character's health pool in OSRS. Unlike other skills, it levels up automatically as you deal damage in combat — you cannot directly "train" it like Woodcutting or Fishing.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Training Guide 2026

text:
matically as you deal damage in combat — you cannot directly "train" it like Woodcutting or Fishing. However, understanding exactly how Hitpoints XP works allows you to maximize its gain while training other combat stats, ensuring you reach maximum HP as fast as possible. 

How Hitpoints XP Works — The Complete System 
In OSRS, Hitpoints XP is earned whenever your character deals damage to an NPC (or another player in PvP).
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Training Guide 2026

text:
S, Hitpoints XP is earned whenever your character deals damage to an NPC (or another player in PvP). The key formula is simple: 
The Hitpoints XP Formula 
For every point of damage you deal: You receive 1.33 Hitpoints XP 
This means: If you hit a monster for 20 damage, you get ~26.6 Hitpoints XP 
Important caveat: Damage from familiars, thralls, and poison does NOT give Hitpoints XP 
Cannon damage: Does NOT give Hitpoints XP (or any combat XP)

What DOES Give Hitpoints XP

Every hit you land deals massive damage, which converts into massive Hitpoints XP. A single 5-hour NMZ session can net you 2–3 million Hitpoints XP — equivalent to weeks of regular combat training.

Early Game Hitpoints (Levels 1–50) 
New players don't need any special method. Just follow standard combat training and your Hitpoints will keep pace: 
Levels 1–20: Kill chickens/cows/goblins in Lumbridge area. Your HP will naturally reach ~20 by the time combat stats hit similar levels. 
Levels 20–40: Rock Crabs (Hosidius beach or Rellekka) or Sand Crabs (Hosidius). Very low-risk, decent HP XP (~60k–80k/hr), extremely AFK. 
Levels 40–60: Ammonite Crabs (Fossil Island).

Levels 60–70: Start Slayer (complete first Slayer Master quests), fight ogres/dwarves, or begin early NMZ (if you've completed required quests).

Tips to Maximize Hitpoints Gain 
Avoid multicannon for XP training — cannons kill faster but give zero Hitpoints XP. Only use them when you don't care about HP (slayer tasks for speed). 
Use weapons with high max hit — Higher damage per hit = more HP XP per click.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Training Guide 2026

text:
yer tasks for speed). 
Use weapons with high max hit — Higher damage per hit = more HP XP per click. Dharok's, Vesta's blighted longbow, and Dragon knife are excellent choices. 
Train on high-HP monsters — Monsters with more HP let you deal more total damage before they die, which means more XP per monster. 
Piety/Rigour/Augury increase your accuracy and damage — which indirectly increases HP XP by letting you hit higher/more consistently.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hitpoints Training Guide 2026

text:
accuracy and damage — which indirectly increases HP XP by letting you hit higher/more consistently. 
Don't "safe-spot" if you care about HP XP — Safe spots reduce damage taken but also slow down kills. For pure XP efficiency, tank and DPS aggressively. 
Use the Bonecrusher (Slayer reward) — While not direct HP XP, it saves you inventory space and keeps your prayer up, letting you use offensive prayers longer. 
Consider HP as a "free" stat — You never need to specifically grind Hitpoints.

3. Levels 47–80: Salamanders to Maniacal Monkeys 
Orange Salamanders (47–59) 
Head to the desert south of Uzer with 3-4 small fishing nets and 3-4 rope. Set up traps at the 3 young trees. Orange salamanders are fast XP at 30K-45K/hr. This takes 2-3 hours to reach level 59. 
Red Salamanders (59–67) — The XP Jumps 
Move to the Ourania Altar area (ZMI) west of the Observatory.

In the Wilderness (level 20-24), black salamanders offer 80K-100K XP/hr. Only worth it if you're comfortable with low-risk Wilderness; otherwise stick with red salamanders until 80.

7. Common Mistakes & How to Avoid Them 
Skipping birdhouse runs entirely. Birdhouses are free passive XP. Not doing them means you need 3M-5M more active XP — that's 20+ extra hours of active training. Do them.

Using wrong tier birdhouses. Always use the highest tier birdhouse your level allows. The XP difference between regular and yew birdhouses is roughly 4x. Pay the extra 200 GP per log — it's worth it. 
Not bringing enough traps. Salamander spots have 3 trees, so bring at least 3 traps. For Maniacal monkeys, bring 4-5. One less trap = 25% less XP/hr. 
Forgetting Digsite Pendants.

Training Hunter without Graceful. Almost every Hunter method involves running between trap spots or tracking objects. Graceful saves 30% run energy drain, essentially doubling your effective uptime.

Q: Can I get 99 Hunter with birdhouses alone? 
A: Yes, technically. Using redwood birdhouses (90+), you'd gain ~20K XP per run.

Q: When should I switch from salamanders to Herbiboar? 
A: At level 80 Hunter exactly. Herbiboar at level 80 provides comparable XP to black salamanders (~100K-120K XP/hr initially) but also makes 300K-600K GP/hr from herbs. As your Hunter level increases, Herbiboar XP scales up to 180K/hr at 95+. Switch at 80 and never look back.

Q: How profitable is 1-99 Hunter? 
A: 5M-20M GP, depending on methods. Birdhouse runs generate 5M-10M GP in bird nests and seeds. Herbiboar from 80-99 generates 8M-15M GP in herbs (higher Herblore = higher profit). Active salamander training generates zero profit. Maniacal monkeys cost supplies (prayer potions if using Protect from Melee).

🐾 You Can AFK Your Way to 99 Hunter

text:
Q: How long does 1-99 Hunter take in OSRS?
A: Using birdhouse runs as passive XP alongside active training: 30-50 hours of active play spread over weeks. Pure active training (salamanders then Herbiboar): 40-60 hours to 99. Pure birdhouse runs only: roughly 4-6 months logging in every 50 minutes, yielding 40K-60K XP per run. The most efficient approach combines daily birdhouse runs with 1-2 hours of active Maniacal monkey or Herbiboar training.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hunter Guide AFK Method 2026 — Birdhouses, Maniacal Monkeys & Herbiboar

text:
Q: What is the most AFK Hunter training method?
A: Maniacal monkeys (level 60+ after Monkey Madness II) are the most AFK Hunter method in OSRS. Place box traps in Kruk's Dungeon and the monkeys continuously destroy and re-trigger traps. You only need to click every 1-2 minutes to reset traps, making this true AFK training at 60K-110K XP/hr. Birdhouse runs are also passive but require logging in every 50 minutes to collect.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hunter Guide AFK Method 2026 — Birdhouses, Maniacal Monkeys & Herbiboar

text:
Q: Are birdhouse runs worth doing for 99 Hunter?
A: Absolutely. Birdhouse runs provide 4,000-18,000 XP per run (depending on birdhouse tier) and take only 2-3 minutes. Over the journey from 1-99, birdhouse runs alone can contribute 3-5 million passive Hunter XP. They also generate profit — bird nests contain valuable seeds (ranarr, snapdragon, spirit) and jewelry. Do a birdhouse run every time you log in or between other activities.
title: OSRS 1-99 Hunter Guide AFK Method 2026 — Birdhouses, Maniacal Monkeys & Herbiboar

text:
Q: Which quests are required for efficient Hunter training?
A: Bone Voyage is the most important quest — it unlocks Fossil Island (birdhouses and Herbiboar). Monkey Madness II unlocks Maniacal monkeys (best AFK method). Eagles' Peak is optional but useful for unlocking box traps earlier. Natural History Quiz at Varrock Museum provides free Hunter XP and contributes to the 100 kudos needed for Bone Voyage.

Why Train Magic Cheaply? 
Magic is one of the most punishing skills in OSRS if you train it the "standard" way.

agic Cheaply? 
Magic is one of the most punishing skills in OSRS if you train it the "standard" way. High Alchemy from level 55 to 99 costs approximately 150–180M GP depending on nature rune prices. The Splashing Guide: Complete AFK Method 
Splashing is the ultimate budget Magic training method. It's slow, but it's completely AFK and costs a fraction of other methods. If you have time but not GP, this is your best option.
title: OSRS 1-99 Magic Training Cheap Methods 2026

text:
ly AFK and costs a fraction of other methods. If you have time but not GP, this is your best option. 
How Splashing Works 
In OSRS, if you cast a combat spell on a target that has 100% magic defense (or is in a safe area where damage is impossible), the spell will always "splash" — dealing 0 damage. You still gain the full Magic XP for the cast, but you don't need to worry about the monster fighting back or dying. 
The 20-minute logout timer: OSRS logs you out after 20 minutes of inactivity.
title: OSRS 1-99 Magic Training Cheap Methods 2026

text:
ghting back or dying. 
The 20-minute logout timer: OSRS logs you out after 20 minutes of inactivity. When splashing, "inactivity" means not interacting with the game. Simply having a spell auto-cast at a target does not reset the timer. You need to perform an action (move camera, click a tile, open inventory) at least once every ~18–19 minutes to stay logged in.
title: OSRS 1-99 Magic Training Cheap Methods 2026

text:
on (move camera, click a tile, open inventory) at least once every ~18–19 minutes to stay logged in.

OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide for Beginners (2026) 
Skill Training Mining Updated 2026 
Mining is one of the most accessible skills in Old School RuneScape, perfect for beginners who want a relaxed, AFK-friendly training experience. This comprehensive guide walks you through every level from 1 to 99, covering the most efficient methods, money-making opportunities, and essential tips that will save you countless hours.

fficient methods, money-making opportunities, and essential tips that will save you countless hours. Whether you're a brand-new player just leaving Tutorial Island or an experienced account looking to finally get that Mining pet, you'll find everything you need right here. 

Why Train Mining? (Benefits & Uses) 
Mining might seem like a simple click-and-wait skill, but it offers several compelling reasons to train it seriously.

like a simple click-and-wait skill, but it offers several compelling reasons to train it seriously. Understanding these benefits will help you stay motivated through the grind to 99. 
🏗️ Essential for Smithing 
Mining and Smithing go hand-in-hand like no other skills in OSRS. If you plan to train Smithing (which lets you craft armor, weapons, and useful items like cannonballs), you'll need a steady supply of ores.

📜 Quest Requirements 
Several important quests have Mining level requirements that can block your progression if you neglect this skill. Here are the most significant ones: 
Heroes' Quest: Requires 60 Mining (along with other stats). This quest is essential for accessing the Heroes' Guild and starting the Legend's Quest. 
Lunar Diplomacy: Requires 70 Mining.

💰 Money Making Potential 
While Mining isn't the most profitable skill at low levels, it becomes increasingly lucrative as you progress.

😌 AFK-Friendly Training 
Mining is arguably the most AFK-friendly skill in OSRS. The Motherlode Mine (available at level 30) allows you to train completely afk for hours at a time. You can watch Netflix, do homework, or work on other tasks while your character mines away.

Consistent, predictable XP rates without competition from other players 
AFK gameplay with minimal attention required 
Passive income from the ores you receive in pay dirt

Levels 1–15: Tin and Copper Rocks (The Very Beginning) 
The first 15 levels of Mining are quick and straightforward.

The first 15 levels of Mining are quick and straightforward. Your goal here is to reach level 15 as fast as possible so you can start mining iron ore, which is much faster XP. 
📍 Best Locations for Levels 1-15 
You have two excellent options for mining tin and copper: 
Southeast Lumbridge Swamp: This is the closest mine to a bank for free-to-play players. It has 3 tin rocks and 5 copper rocks. The nearby Lumbridge swamp is safe and rarely crowded.

Recommendation: Start at Lumbridge Swamp mine since it's right after Tutorial Island. Only switch to Rimmington if Lumbridge is crowded.

⚡ Power-Mining (Drop Mining) for Speed 
Here's the most important tip for early Mining: DO NOT BANK YOUR ORE. Instead, use the "power-mining" method: 
Mine a full inventory of tin/copper ore 
Drop all the ore (don't walk to a bank) 
Repeat immediately 
This is much faster than banking because you eliminate travel time.

to a bank)

This is much faster than banking because you eliminate travel time. Yes, you get zero profit, but you'll reach level 15 in about 15 minutes instead of an hour. The time saved is worth far more than the ~2,000 GP worth of ores you'd get from banking. 
How to drop quickly: With a full inventory, right-click each ore and select "Drop." Even faster: enable "Shift-click to drop" in your game settings (right-click any inventory item → "Toggle shift-click drop").

⚒️ Pickaxe Progression (Levels 1-15) 
You start Tutorial Island with a bronze pickaxe, which works fine for levels 1-15. However, as soon as you can afford better pickaxes, upgrade immediately. Here's the progression: 
Bronze Pickaxe: Automatically given at Tutorial Island. Works until level 1. 
Iron Pickaxe: Buy from the Grand Exchange for ~100 GP. Drastically faster than bronze.

thod for Beginners 
Congratulations! You've reached level 30 Mining and completed the Digsite Quest. Now begins the most AFK, relaxed training method in OSRS: the Motherlode Mine (MLM). 
This section will explain everything you need to know about MLM, from how it works to maximizing your XP rates and profits. 
📍 Where is the Motherlode Mine? 
The Motherlode Mine is located in the Digsite area, north of the Digsite (east of Varrock).

e Mine? 
The Motherlode Mine is located in the Digsite area, north of the Digsite (east of Varrock). Here's how to get there: 
Fastest method: Use a Digsite teleport (unlocked after Digsite Quest) or a teleport to Varrock, then run east. 
Alternative: Use the Mine cart system from Keldagrim (requires started Dwarf Cannon Quest) or from the Grand Exchange (after completing the Digsite Quest). 
F2P note: Motherlode Mine is members-only. Free-to-play players cannot access it.

⛏️ How Motherlode Mine Works (Step-by-Step) 
The Motherlode Mine is not like regular mining. Instead of mining ore rocks and getting ores directly, you mine "ore veins" and collect "pay dirt," which you then process for random ores. Here's the full process: 
Mine Ore Veins: In the MLM cavern, you'll see special ore veins (they look different from normal rocks). Click on them to mine.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
cavern, you'll see special ore veins (they look different from normal rocks). Click on them to mine. You'll get pay dirt in your inventory instead of actual ores. 
Fill the Hopper: When your inventory is full of pay dirt, go to the hopper (a large machine in the center of the cavern) and deposit your pay dirt into it. 
Operate the Hopper Controls: Pull the lever next to the hopper to send your pay dirt through the washing machine.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
opper Controls: Pull the lever next to the hopper to send your pay dirt through the washing machine. 
Collect from the Sack: Walk to the sack (large cloth bag) and withdraw your cleaned ores. The pay dirt "washes" into various ores depending on your Mining level. 
What ores do you get from pay dirt? 
The ores you receive depend on your Mining level.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

ining level. 
What ores do you get from pay dirt? 
The ores you receive depend on your Mining level. At higher levels, your pay dirt yields better ores: 
Mining Level Ores from Pay Dirt 
30-39 Clay, Copper, Tin, Iron 
40-54 + Silver, Gold 
55-69 + Mithril 
70-84 + Adamantite 
85+ + Runite (very rare but valuable!) 
This means MLM gets more profitable as you level up, since higher-level ores are worth more GP.

📊 XP Rates: Upper Level vs. Lower Level 
MLM has two mining areas: the Upper Level (available at level 30) and the Lower Level (unlocked at level 72). Here's the difference: 
Upper Level (Levels 30-71) 
XP Rate: ~35,000–45,000 XP per hour 
AFK Level: Very high. You can easily go 3-4 minutes between clicks. 
Pros: Extremely relaxed, almost fully AFK, perfect for watching videos or doing other tasks.

licks. 
Pros: Extremely relaxed, almost fully AFK, perfect for watching videos or doing other tasks. 
Cons: Slower XP than lower level; you'll be here for a long time (levels 30-72). 
Lower Level (Levels 72-99) 
XP Rate: ~50,000–55,000 XP per hour 
AFK Level: Moderate. You need to pay more attention because lower-level veins deplete faster. 
Pros: Faster XP, better profit per hour, more engaging gameplay. 
Cons: Less AFK than upper level; requires more active playing.

Cons: Less AFK than upper level; requires more active playing. 
My recommendation: Stay on the upper level until level 72. The difference in XP (10,000-15,000 per hour) isn't worth the loss of AFK comfort for most players. Once you unlock the lower level at 72, switch down there for the rest of your journey to 99.

🎽 Essential Gear for MLM 
While MLM is low-requirement, having the right gear makes a significant difference: 
Pickaxe 
Always use the best pickaxe you can afford. Here's the progression: 
Rune Pickaxe (level 41): Huge upgrade over adamant. Buy for ~25,000 GP or get one as a drop from the Heroes' Guild minigame. 
Dragon Pickaxe (level 61): 5% faster than rune.

Dragon Pickaxe (level 61): 5% faster than rune. Very expensive (~5-6 million GP), but the time saved might be worth it for you. Alternative: Use a Dragon pickaxe special attack from the Ork statue in God Wars Dungeon (requires 70 Strength). 
Crystal Pickaxe (level 71): Best pickaxe in the game. Requires 71 Mining and completion of Song of the Elves quest. Very expensive (multiple millions), but the ultimate goal for efficiency.

Full Graceful Outfit 
Wearing the full Graceful outfit reduces your run energy depletion by 30% and increases run energy restoration by 30%.

💰 Sack Upgrades: Bigger Capacity = Less Clicking 
Your sack (where you collect ores from pay dirt) has a limited capacity. At level 30, it holds 81 pay dirt. But at level 81, you can upgrade it to hold 162 pay dirt! 
Why this matters: A bigger sack means you can AFK longer without emptying it.

⚡ 3-Tick Granite Mining (Levels 50-99) — Fastest XP 
If you want to get 99 Mining as fast as possible and don't mind intense clicking, 3-tick granite mining is the best method in the game. 
What is 3-Ticking? 
"3-ticking" is a game mechanic where you perform an action every 3 game ticks (1.8 seconds) instead of the normal 4 ticks (2.4 seconds).
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
you perform an action every 3 game ticks (1.8 seconds) instead of the normal 4 ticks (2.4 seconds). This makes your character mine faster because you're starting the next mining action before the current one finishes (if the rock depletes). 
How to 3-Tick Granite Mine 
Location: Desert Quarry (south of the Shanty Pass in the Kharid Desert). Requires level 50 Mining. 
What you mine: Granite rocks (they give 500g, 2kg, or 5kg granite blocks).
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
equires level 50 Mining. 
What you mine: Granite rocks (they give 500g, 2kg, or 5kg granite blocks). 
The 3-tick cycle: Click the granite rock 
Immediately drop the granite (or fill your inventory and drop everything) 
Click the rock again 
Repeat in a rhythm 

Food/Water: Bring waterskins (4-dose) and fill them at the desert well. The desert heat will damage you if you don't stay hydrated.

text:
-dose) and fill them at the desert well. The desert heat will damage you if you don't stay hydrated. Alternatively, wear the Desert amulet 4 (after completion of Desert Treasure II) for infinite desert heat protection. 
XP Rates and Requirements 
XP Rate: ~65,000–75,000 XP per hour (much faster than MLM!) 
Profit: Negative (you spend money on waterskins, though they're cheap) 
Intensity: High. This is NOT AFK. You must maintain the 3-tick rhythm constantly.

Is 3-Tick Granite Worth It? 
Yes, if: You want 99 Mining quickly and don't mind intense gameplay. It's also great if you're a streamer or content creator who needs to show progress fast. 
No, if: You prefer relaxed, AFK training. 3-tick granite is exhausting and not enjoyable for long sessions.

3-Tick Timing Guide 
Mastering the 3-tick rhythm takes practice. Here's a simple way to learn: 
Turn on "F-key behaves as hotkey" in settings (this lets you use F1-F5 to open tabs quickly) 
Mine a rock, then immediately click an empty inventory slot (drop the ore) 
Click the rock again 
The rhythm is: Click rock → drop → click rock → drop 
After some practice, it becomes muscle memory

🎯 3-Tick Granite Mining: Detailed Timing Guide 
Since 3-tick granite is the fastest Mining method but also the most confusing for beginners, here's a more detailed explanation: 
The Theory Behind 3-Ticking
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
the most confusing for beginners, here's a more detailed explanation: 
The Theory Behind 3-Ticking 
OSRS runs on a "tick system" where everything happens in 0.6-second intervals. Normally, mining a rock takes 4 ticks (2.4 seconds). But if you perform an action on the 3rd tick (like dropping an ore), you can start mining the next rock 25% faster. 
Step-by-Step 3-Tick Granite Method 
Go to the Desert Quarry with a pickup and a full inventory of waterskins (4-dose).
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
k Granite Method 
Go to the Desert Quarry with a pickup and a full inventory of waterskins (4-dose). 
Find a granite rock that's not being mined by someone else. 
Click the granite rock. 
The moment your character starts swinging the pickaxe, RIGHT-CLICK an inventory slot with an ore and select "Drop." (Or shift-click if you enabled shift-click drop.) 
As soon as the ore is dropped, click the granite rock again. 
Repeat steps 3-5 in a continuous rhythm.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
soon as the ore is dropped, click the granite rock again. 
Repeat steps 3-5 in a continuous rhythm. 
Common Mistakes 
Clicking too early: If you click the rock before your character finishes the current mining action, it cancels the action. Wait for the "ore obtained" message. 
Not dropping fast enough: If you don't drop by the 3rd tick, you lose the speed bonus. Practice until it's muscle memory. 
Running out of water: Desert heat will damage you.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

text:
speed bonus. Practice until it's muscle memory. 
Running out of water: Desert heat will damage you. Check your waterskins regularly and refill at the desert well. 
Mining the wrong size granite: Granite comes in 500g, 2kg, and 5kg sizes. It doesn't matter which you mine; they all give the same XP. Just mine whichever respawns first. 
YouTube is your friend: Search "OSRS 3-tick granite mining guide" and watch a video. It's much easier to understand the timing when you see it in action.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 
Here are the most common questions beginners have about Mining, answered in detail. 
❓ Q: Should I bank ores or power-mine (drop them)? 
A: Below level 30, always power-mine (drop ores). The time saved is worth far more than the ~2,000 GP you'd get from banking tin/copper/iron ores.
title: OSRS 1-99 Mining Guide Beginner 2026

s). The time saved is worth far more than the ~2,000 GP you'd get from banking tin/copper/iron ores. At level 30+, if you're at MLM, you're not banking ores anyway — you're collecting pay dirt and withdrawing the cleaned ores later. 
If you need GP desperately (e.g., for a bond), you can bank iron ore from level 15-30 and sell it on the GE. But for pure XP, power-mining is always faster.

❓ Q: Is Motherlode Mine better than 3-tick granite? 
A: It depends on your playstyle and goals: 
Choose MLM if: You want to relax, watch Netflix, work on other tasks, or play casually. It's much less intense and almost fully AFK. 
Choose 3-tick granite if: You want 99 Mining as fast as possible and don't mind (or enjoy) high-intensity clicking. It's 70-80 hours to 99 vs. 150+ hours at MLM.

F2P Mining progression: 
Levels 1-15: Power-mine tin/copper in Lumbridge Swamp or Rimmington. 
Levels 15-30: Power-mine iron ore in Al Kharid. 
Levels 30-40: Mine iron ore and BANK it (sell on GE for profit). F2P doesn't have MLM, so you can't AFK train effectively.

title: OSRS 1-99 Thieving Guide for Ironman 2026 — Fastest XP, Pyramid Plunder & Master Farmers

text: man Optimized — Updated June 2026 Thieving is one of the most important skills for ironman accounts. It provides raw coins for daily expenses, seeds for Herblore training, gems for Crafting, and the Pharaoh's Sceptre for efficient teleportation. This guide covers the optimal ironman path from level 1 to 99 Thieving in 2026.

Pyramid Plunder Run Strategy 
Enter the pyramid — you have 5 minutes per run. 
Run straight to the highest room available — do NOT stop in lower rooms. 
Loot urns in your highest room first, then work down through rooms 5-8. 
Always loot golden chests in every room — they have the Pharaoh's Sceptre drop (1/1,000 per chest). 
When time is almost up, rush to the exit or let the timer expire.

text:
Sceptre drop (1/1,000 per chest). 
When time is almost up, rush to the exit or let the timer expire. 
Bank, reset, repeat. Each run is self-contained — there's no penalty for failing. 
🏺 Pharaoh's Sceptre — Keep It: The sceptre has a 1/1,000 drop rate from golden chests and sarcophagi. Ironmen should KEEP the first sceptre — it teleports directly to the Pyramid (replacing the need for the long desert walk) and to the Ancient Pyramid for switching spellbooks.

You'll never need to move or chase the NPC. Check the "OSRS Knight Splash" worlds on community trackers — this is the #1 AFK Thieving method for ironmen.

Q: What is the fastest Thieving XP for ironmen? 
A: Pyramid Plunder from level 71 onwards, reaching 280,000 XP/hr at 91+. From 91-99, Ardougne Knights are also ~250,000 XP/hr but much more AFK and provide 12-15M GP.

title: OSRS 1-99 Thieving Guide for Ironman 2026 — Fastest XP, Pyramid Plunder & Master Farmers

text:
Q: What is the fastest Thieving XP for ironmen in OSRS?
A: Pyramid Plunder is the fastest Thieving XP for ironmen from level 71 onwards, reaching up to 280,000 XP/hr at level 91+. From level 91+, pickpocketing Ardougne Knights with the Medium Ardougne Diary complete and full Rogues' outfit reaches 250,000+ XP/hr and is more AFK-friendly.
title: OSRS 1-99 Thieving Guide for Ironman 2026 — Fastest XP, Pyramid Plunder & Master Farmers

text:
Q: What level should ironmen start pickpocketing Master Farmers?
A: You can start at level 38 Thieving, but success rates are low. The ideal time to start is level 70+ with full Rogues' outfit — at this level you'll rarely fail and can farm ranarr/snapdragon seeds efficiently for Herblore training. At level 94+ with the Hard Ardougne Diary complete, you'll never fail Master Farmers.
title: OSRS 1-99 Thieving Guide for Ironman 2026 — Fastest XP, Pyramid Plunder & Master Farmers

text:
Q: Do ironmen need Rogues' outfit for Thieving?
A: Absolutely yes. Full Rogues' outfit (from Rogues' Den minigame) doubles all coin drops from pickpocketing. For ironmen, this doubles your raw GP income from Ardougne Knights and other NPCs. It typically takes 2-4 hours to obtain the full set at level 50+ Thieving.
title: OSRS 1-99 Thieving Guide for Ironman 2026 — Fastest XP, Pyramid Plunder & Master Farmers

text:
Q: How many coins can ironmen make from 1-99 Thieving?
A: From 55-99 Thieving pickpocketing Ardougne Knights with full Rogues' outfit, an ironman earns approximately 13-15M raw GP. Combined with Pyramid Plunder for the earlier levels, expect 10-15M total GP from 1-99 Thieving, plus valuable seeds and gems from stalls and Master Farmers.

title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text: 2026 Woodcutting is one of the most approachable skills in OSRS, suitable for both F2P and members. Whether you are just starting out or pushing for the cape, this guide covers every stage from level 1 to 99, including the F2P route, the fastest members path, and the beloved AFK Redwoods method for the final stretch.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
the F2P route, the fastest members path, and the beloved AFK Redwoods method for the final stretch. 
🌳 Woodcutting 1-99 -- Quick Reference 
XP at 99: 13,034,431 XP Cape Perk: +10% bird nest drops 
Fastest XP (members): 170K-200K/hr (tick manipulation) Best AFK: Redwoods (60K-80K/hr, 5-min AFK) 
F2P Max XP/hr: ~35K (Willows) to ~30K (Yews) Time to 99: 120-350 hrs depending on method 

1. Axe Upgrades -- Always Use the Best Available 
Your axe determines how quickly you chop logs.

1. Axe Upgrades -- Always Use the Best Available 
Your axe determines how quickly you chop logs. Always upgrade to the best axe your level allows. Wielding the axe in your weapon slot provides approximately 10% faster chopping than holding it in your inventory.

Adamant Axe 31 30 ~1,500 GP

Infernal Axe 61 60 ~3M GP (Dragon Axe + Smouldering Stone) 
Crystal Axe 71 70 Gauntlet reward 
The Dragon Axe special attack temporarily boosts your Woodcutting level by 3, which can help you access higher-level trees a few levels early.

boosts your Woodcutting level by 3, which can help you access higher-level trees a few levels early. The Infernal Axe burns logs as you cut them (1/3 chance), providing passive Firemaking XP at the cost of losing the logs. The Crystal Axe has a faster chop speed than Dragon and is the best non-Infernal axe. 
💡 Lumberjack Outfit: Obtained from the Temple Trekking minigame, the Lumberjack outfit provides up to 2.5% bonus Woodcutting XP (full 4-piece set).

rekking minigame, the Lumberjack outfit provides up to 2.5% bonus Woodcutting XP (full 4-piece set). It takes about 2-4 hours to obtain and is worth getting. The Forestry outfit is an alternative from the Forestry activity. 

2. F2P Woodcutting Route (1-99) 
Free-to-play players have fewer tree options but can still reach 99 Woodcutting efficiently.

(1-99) 
Free-to-play players have fewer tree options but can still reach 99 Woodcutting efficiently. Here is the optimal F2P path:

Key F2P locations: Draynor Village has willow trees right next to the bank -- this is the best overall F2P XP spot for levels 30-60. For Yews, Edgeville has 3 yew trees south of the bank, and Varrock Palace has 2 yew trees. F2P players should note that willows remain competitive XP/hr even at higher levels due to Yews being slow to chop.

uld note that willows remain competitive XP/hr even at higher levels due to Yews being slow to chop. 
💵 F2P Profit Note: Yew logs sell for ~250-300 GP each on the Grand Exchange. From level 60-99, you'll cut approximately 75,000 Yew logs, yielding 18-22M GP in profit. This makes Woodcutting one of the better F2P money-making skills alongside the XP you earn.

4. Redwood Trees -- Best AFK Method (90-99) 
Redwood trees in the Woodcutting Guild are the most AFK Woodcutting method in OSRS. With enormous tree HP pools that rarely deplete, you can AFK for up to 5 minutes without interaction. This makes Redwoods the #1 choice for players who want 99 with minimal attention.

6. Advanced Tips for Maximum Efficiency 
2-Tick Teaks: The absolute fastest Woodcutting XP in the game.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
Advanced Tips for Maximum Efficiency 
2-Tick Teaks: The absolute fastest Woodcutting XP in the game. Requires clicking the tree, moving one tile, then clicking the tree again -- repeating every 2 game ticks (1.2 seconds). Hard to learn but yields 170K-200K XP/hr. Watch a YouTube guide for the rhythm. 
Woodcutting Guild passive +7 boost: While inside the Woodcutting Guild, you receive an invisible +7 Woodcutting boost. This stacks with your Dragon Axe spec and visible boosts.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
receive an invisible +7 Woodcutting boost. This stacks with your Dragon Axe spec and visible boosts. Always train inside the guild when possible. 
Infernal Axe passive Firemaking XP: The Infernal Axe burns 1/3 of logs automatically. Over the course of 1-99 Woodcutting, this can give you 1-3M+ passive Firemaking XP. Well worth the 3M GP investment if you don't need the logs. 
Bird Nests: Randomly drop from all trees. Empty nests sell for ~6,000 GP each.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
don't need the logs. 
Bird Nests: Randomly drop from all trees. Empty nests sell for ~6,000 GP each. Nests with seeds can contain Spirit seeds (rare), Magic seeds (~100K GP), or other valuable tree seeds. Always pick up nests -- they can be worth millions over the course of 99. 
Ring of Endurance: If you own this ring (from Hallowed Sepulchre), the stamina potion effect doubling is excellent for long run distances like Ape Atoll teaks to the bank.

stamina potion effect doubling is excellent for long run distances like Ape Atoll teaks to the bank. 
The Forestry update: Forestry added group Woodcutting events that give bonus XP and the Forestry outfit. Participating in Forestry events at popular tree spots adds variety and extra XP. The Forestry kit provides a passive +2 level boost when carried in your inventory. 

7. Common Mistakes 
Using Yews for XP. Yews are slow and terrible XP compared to Teaks or Mahogany.

text:
7. Common Mistakes 
Using Yews for XP. Yews are slow and terrible XP compared to Teaks or Mahogany. Only cut Yews if you're F2P or specifically want profit, not XP. 
Banking low-value logs. Banking Normal, Oak, or Willow logs wastes enormous time for minimal GP. Drop them and save the banking time for higher-value trees (Mahogany, Yew, Magic). 
Not using the Woodcutting Guild.

ve the banking time for higher-value trees (Mahogany, Yew, Magic). 
Not using the Woodcutting Guild. The +7 invisible boost inside the guild makes it the best place to train at any level where you have access to the trees inside. Get 75% Hosidius favour as early as possible. 
Ignoring bird nests. Over 1-99, bird nests can generate 5-20M+ GP in seeds. The 10 seconds to pick one up is always worth it. With the Woodcutting Cape, you get 10% more nests.

Using an axe from inventory instead of wielding it. Wielding your axe in the weapon slot gives approximately 10% faster chopping speed than holding it in your inventory. Always equip it. 
Not pre-potting with Dragon Axe spec. The +3 boost from the Dragon Axe special attack lasts about 1 minute. Use it whenever possible for faster chopping and earlier access to higher trees.

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I reach 99 Woodcutting in F2P? 
A: Yes! Many players have achieved 99 Woodcutting in F2P. The optimal F2P path is Willows from 30-99 (35K XP/hr, ~350 hours) or Yews from 60-99 (30K XP/hr, ~300 hours + 18-22M GP profit). It takes longer than members but is achievable. The key is consistency -- a few hours per week over several months.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
than members but is achievable. The key is consistency -- a few hours per week over several months. 

Q: Should I use the Infernal Axe or keep my Dragon Axe? 
A: It depends on your goals. If you want maximum Woodcutting XP and don't care about logs, use the Infernal Axe -- the passive Firemaking XP is a bonus and you'll never have to drop logs. If you want profit or are an Ironman who needs logs for Construction/Fletching, keep using the Dragon or Crystal Axe.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
t or are an Ironman who needs logs for Construction/Fletching, keep using the Dragon or Crystal Axe. The Infernal Axe destroys 1/3 of logs permanently. 

Q: What's the best way to get 75% Hosidius favour for the WC Guild? 
A: Three fast methods: (1) Plow the fields in Hosidius (0-5% favour, ~5 min), (2) Make Sulphurous Fertiliser from saltpetre and compost (5-75% favour, ~45 min), (3) Complete Client of Kourend quest for 20% favour in all houses.

compost (5-75% favour, ~45 min), (3) Complete Client of Kourend quest for 20% favour in all houses. The fertiliser method costs ~100K GP but takes under an hour from 0-75%. 

Q: How much GP can I make from 1-99 Woodcutting? 
A: It varies dramatically by method. Cutting and banking Magic logs (75-99) can yield 50-80M GP but takes 250+ hours. Cutting and banking Yews (60-99) yields 18-22M GP. Teaks/Mahogany to 99 yields 10-15M GP if banked. Redwoods 90-99 yields ~5M GP.

text:
Q: How long does 1-99 Woodcutting take in OSRS?
A: The time varies by method: F2P-only (Yews/Willows) takes 250-350 hours. Members using Teaks at Ape Atoll takes 150-200 hours. Members using tick manipulation at Teaks takes 100-130 hours. AFK Redwoods from 90-99 takes about 100 hours for that stretch alone but with minimal attention. Overall with efficient training expect 120-180 hours 1-99 on a members account.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
Q: What is the fastest Woodcutting training method in OSRS?
A: Tick manipulation Teak cutting (2-tick Woodcutting) is the absolute fastest at 170,000-200,000 XP/hr when performed perfectly. For more relaxed training, regular Teaks at Ape Atoll or Fossil Island give 65,000-95,000 XP/hr. Mahogany trees on Ape Atoll (3 trees in a triangle) give 80,000-115,000 XP/hr at high levels. Redwoods at level 90+ give 60,000-80,000 XP/hr and are extremely AFK.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
Q: Do I need to bank logs for Woodcutting XP?
A: No. Dropping logs is always faster than banking them for pure XP. If you want profit, bank Yew or Magic logs. For Ironmen, banking Teak/Mahogany logs is worthwhile for Construction training. But if your goal is 99 as fast as possible, drop the logs -- or use an Infernal Axe which automatically burns logs for Firemaking XP.
title: OSRS 1-99 Woodcutting Guide for Early Game 2026 -- F2P to Redwoods

text:
Q: What is the Woodcutting Guild boost and how do I access it?
A: The Woodcutting Guild in Great Kourend provides a passive +7 invisible Woodcutting boost to all players inside it. To enter you need: 60 Woodcutting and 75% Hosidius favour. The guild contains regular trees, oaks, willows, yews, magic trees, and two redwood trees. It's the best location for Redwoods and one of the best for Yews/Magics due to the proximity to a bank.

Sailing Skill Deep Dive 
Sailing is the first new skill added to OSRS since its 2013 release. Developed through extensive community polls and beta testing, Sailing introduces a whole new dimension to Gielinor: the seas. 
How Sailing Works 
Sailing is trained by building and upgrading ships, navigating waters, exploring islands, engaging in naval combat, and establishing trade routes. It's a gathering, PvM, and profit skill all rolled into one.

Q: Will Sailing be members-only?

1. Why Train Agility? 
Agility is one of the most important skills in OSRS because it directly affects your run energy recovery rate . At higher levels, you regenerate run energy so fast that you essentially never stop running — which saves time on literally everything you do in the game. 
Beyond run energy, Agility unlocks over 50 shortcuts across Gielinor that drastically cut travel time.

Wear cheap gear only — Graceful + Amulet of glory + Royal seed pod

Marks of Grace are exclusive drops from rooftop Agility courses. They spawn on the ground near obstacles and appear as red arrow markers on your screen. In 2026, Marks now also spawn at 7 additional non-rooftop courses including the Gnome Stronghold basic course.

11. FAQ 
What is the fastest way to train Agility in OSRS? 
The Hallowed Sepulchre (level 62+) offers the highest XP rates in the game: up to 100,000–105,000 XP/hr at level 92+. For rooftop courses, Ardougne at level 90 gives ~70,000 XP/hr.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
o 100,000–105,000 XP/hr at level 92+. For rooftop courses, Ardougne at level 90 gives ~70,000 XP/hr. The Wilderness Agility Course gives ~50,000 XP/hr at level 47 but carries PK risk. 

How long does it take to get 99 Agility in OSRS? 
Using the fastest methods (Hallowed Sepulchre from level 62), reaching 99 Agility takes approximately 180–200 hours. Using rooftop courses exclusively, expect around 230 hours. Brimhaven Arena spike-trap method can speed up the early levels significantly.

ect around 230 hours. Brimhaven Arena spike-trap method can speed up the early levels significantly. 

How much money can you make training Agility? 
From 1–99 using rooftop courses, Marks of Grace converted to Amylase crystals earn roughly 15,000,000–20,000,000 GP total. The Wilderness Agility Course can earn up to 2,000,000 GP/hr from loot. The Hallowed Sepulchre earns 1,000,000+ GP/hr from Grand Coffin loot, with the rare Ring of Endurance worth ~6,000,000 GP.

Where should I farm Marks of Grace? 
Canifis rooftop course (level 40) has the highest Marks of Grace spawn rate for most players. With the elite Ardougne Diary completed, the Ardougne course (level 90) gives the most Marks per hour. With the hard Kandarin Diary, Rellekka (level 80) is also excellent.

Is the Graceful outfit required for Agility training? 
The Graceful outfit is highly recommended but not strictly required. It reduces weight to near zero, which slows run energy drain significantly. Purchase it with Marks of Grace from Grace in the Rogues' Den. Most players collect the full set at Canifis (level 40) before moving to higher courses.

Should I use Summer Pies to boost Agility? 
Yes, Summer Pies (+5 boost) are very useful for accessing courses 3–5 levels early. They're cheap (~1,800 GP each) and the time saved from training at a faster course is worth far more than the pie cost. Use them when you're within 5 levels of a much better course. 

Is the Hallowed Sepulchre worth learning? 
Absolutely.

Is the Hallowed Sepulchre worth learning? 
Absolutely. The learning curve is steep (expect 5–10 hours of practice before you're efficient), but the payoff is enormous: 85K–105K XP/hr versus 52K–70K from rooftops. Plus you earn 1M+ GP/hr and have a chance at the 6M GP Ring of Endurance. It's the best Agility method from 62–99 by a wide margin. 

12. Final Tips 
Always use Stamina potions — this is non-negotiable. The XP difference is 20–40%.

title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
Q: What is the fastest way to train Agility in OSRS?
A: The Hallowed Sepulchre (level 62+) offers the highest XP rates in the game: up to 100,000–105,000 XP/hr at level 92+. For rooftop courses, Ardougne at level 90 gives ~70,000 XP/hr. The Wilderness Agility Course gives ~50,000 XP/hr at level 47 but carries PK risk.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
Q: How long does it take to get 99 Agility in OSRS?
A: Using the fastest methods (Hallowed Sepulchre from level 62), reaching 99 Agility takes approximately 180–200 hours. Using rooftop courses exclusively, expect around 230 hours. Brimhaven Arena spike-trap method can speed up the early levels significantly.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
Q: How much money can you make training Agility?
A: From 1–99 using rooftop courses, Marks of Grace converted to Amylase crystals earn roughly 15,000,000–20,000,000 GP total. The Wilderness Agility Course can earn up to 2,000,000 GP/hr from loot. The Hallowed Sepulchre earns 1,000,000+ GP/hr from Grand Coffin loot, with the rare Ring of Endurance worth ~6,000,000 GP.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
Q: Where should I farm Marks of Grace?
A: Canifis rooftop course (level 40) has the highest Marks of Grace spawn rate for most players. With the elite Ardougne Diary completed, the Ardougne course (level 90) gives the most Marks per hour. With the hard Kandarin Diary, Rellekka (level 80) is also excellent.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

text:
Q: Is the Graceful outfit required for Agility training?
A: The Graceful outfit is highly recommended but not strictly required. It reduces weight to near zero, which slows run energy drain significantly. Purchase it with Marks of Grace from Grace in the Rogues' Den. Most players collect the full set at Canifis (level 40) before moving to higher courses.
title: OSRS Agility Training Guide 2026 — 1-99 Fastest XP & Rooftop Courses

OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss, Venom Arena & Noxious Halberd

👹 Boss Guide — Updated June 2026 Araxxor is one of the most profitable Slayer bosses in OSRS — 92 Slayer required, a unique venom arena mechanic, and the chance at the 80M GP Noxious Halberd.
title: OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss Strategy, Gear Setups & Noxious Halberd

text:
S — 92 Slayer required, a unique venom arena mechanic, and the chance at the 80M GP Noxious Halberd. This guide covers the poison management system, all 3 phases, gear from budget to max, and detailed kill strategies for consistent 5.5M+ GP/hr. 
🕷️ Araxxor — Quick Reference 
Slayer Level: 92 Location: Araxxor Lair, Morytania Swamp 
Best Drop: Noxious Halberd (~80M) GP/hr: 5.5M+ (on-task) 

1. The Venom Arena — Unique Mechanic 
Araxxor's lair slowly fills with venom gas.
title: OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss Strategy, Gear Setups & Noxious Halberd

text:
5.5M+ (on-task) 

1. The Venom Arena — Unique Mechanic 
Araxxor's lair slowly fills with venom gas. The poison level rises over time — if it maxes out, you take rapid 10+ damage per tick. Clear vents are scattered around the room. Step on a vent to clear the poison back to zero. Managing the poison WHILE fighting Araxxor is the core skill check. Strategy: Clear vents only when poison reaches 60%+ — this minimizes time off DPS.
title: OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss Strategy, Gear Setups & Noxious Halberd

text:
core skill check. Strategy: Clear vents only when poison reaches 60%+ — this minimizes time off DPS. Each vent clear takes 2 ticks and has a 30-second cooldown per vent. 

2. Three-Phase Fight 
Phase Attacks Strategy 
🕸️ Web Ranged + Web snare Protect from Missiles. Avoid webs on floor — they root you for 3 seconds. Clear vents at 60% poison. 
🦵 Melee Melee + Stomp AOE Protect from Melee. Run 3 tiles away from Stomp indicator. Kite if low HP.
title: OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss Strategy, Gear Setups & Noxious Halberd

text:
🦵 Melee Melee + Stomp AOE Protect from Melee. Run 3 tiles away from Stomp indicator. Kite if low HP. 
🕷️ Minion Summons 5-8 spiderlings Kill minions FAST with Blowpipe switch. Each spiderling stacks venom on you — cumulatively deadly. Anti-venom+ is essential here. 

3. Gear & Inventory 
Primary: Osmumten's Fang (Stab is BIS) or Abyssal Tentacle. Switch: Toxic Blowpipe for minion phase.
title: OSRS Araxxor Guide 2026 — Slayer Boss Strategy, Gear Setups & Noxious Halberd

text:
Primary: Osmumten's Fang (Stab is BIS) or Abyssal Tentacle. Switch: Toxic Blowpipe for minion phase. Essential supplies: 2 Anti-venom+ potions (venom from spiderlings is brutal), 3-4 Prayer Potions, 6-8 Saradomin Brews, 2 Super Restores, 1 Super Combat Potion, and a House Teleport tab for emergency escape. Armor: Blood Moon/Bandos for Melee phases, Karil's/Black D'hide switch if you need extra Ranged defence during Web phase.

4. Advanced Techniques 
Vent timing optimization: Clear vents during Araxxor's attack animation — you lose zero DPS ticks. The perfect vent clear happens in the 3-tick window after Araxxor's attack lands. 
Minion stacking: In Minion phase, run to a corner so spiderlings cluster up. Blowpipe hits multiple targets — you can clear all 8 spawns in 4-5 shots when they're stacked.

Always kill on-task. The Slayer Helm bonus (+15% damage/accuracy) is the difference between 4M and 5.5M GP/hr.

Q: Off-task or on-task? 
A: Always on-task. Slayer Helm bonus makes a ~30% DPS difference. Off-task kills take 25-30% longer and are significantly less profitable.

Q: Fang or Whip at Araxxor?

Araxxor combines Slayer, boss mechanics, and environmental management into one of the most engaging fights in OSRS. The Noxious Halberd is a genuinely exciting drop — a 2-tile melee weapon that opens up new strategies at many bosses. Manage your vents, respect the venom, and that 80M drop will come.

OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 
You've been playing for a few hours. Your inventory is full. You have no idea where to put your stuff. You walk into a bank, talk to the banker, and suddenly see a 20-tab interface that makes no sense. This guide fixes ALL of that.

1. What Is the Bank? (And Why You Need It) 
Your Inventory holds 28 items. That's it. After 20 minutes of gameplay, your inventory will be full. You need a place to store everything else — that's what the Bank is for.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
our inventory will be full. You need a place to store everything else — that's what the Bank is for. 
🏦 What the Bank Does 
Stores UNLIMITED items (well, 2,304 slots — effectively unlimited for most players) 
Accessible from any bank booth / banker NPC / bank chest in the game 
Lets you withdraw exactly the items you need for a specific activity 
Lets you deposit loot from a long skilling or bossing session 

How to Find a Bank 
Every major town has a bank.

How to Find a Bank 
Every major town has a bank. The closest one to Tutorial Island is: 
Lumbridge: Bank chest on 2nd floor of Lumbridge Castle (easiest for new players!) 
Varrock: West and East banks (very popular) 
Falador: South of town square 
Edgeville: Just north of the Wilderness border (convenient for high-level content) 
💡 Mark Your First Bank on the World Map
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
the Wilderness border (convenient for high-level content) 
💡 Mark Your First Bank on the World Map 
As soon as you visit Lumbridge Castle, open your world map, find the bank chest on the 2nd floor, right-click → "Add marker." Now you'll always have a green arrow pointing you home. 

2. How to Bank — Step by Step 
Here's exactly how to use a bank: 
📥 Depositing Items (Putting Stuff IN the Bank) 
Walk to a bank booth / banker NPC / bank chest
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
k: 
📥 Depositing Items (Putting Stuff IN the Bank) 
Walk to a bank booth / banker NPC / bank chest 
Right-click the banker → "Bank" (or left-click if it says "Bank") 
The bank interface opens (large window with your stored items) 
To deposit: Click an item in your inventory → it goes into the bank. OR click "Deposit inventory" (bottom-right) to deposit EVERYTHING at once.

→ it goes into the bank. OR click "Deposit inventory" (bottom-right) to deposit EVERYTHING at once. 
Close the bank when done (Esc key or click X) 

📤 Withdrawing Items (Taking Stuff OUT of the Bank) 
Open the bank (same as above) 
Find the item you want in the bank interface 
Left-click: Withdraws 1 item 
Right-click: Shows options: Withdraw-1, Withdraw-5, Withdraw-10, Withdraw-All, Withdraw-All-but-1 
Click the amount you want → item appears in your inventory 
Close bank

Withdraw Mode: Note vs Item 
This is a setting in the bank interface that confuses many beginners: 
Mode What Happens When to Use 
Item (default) Withdraws actual items (each takes 1 inventory slot) Withdrawing gear, food, potions 
Note Withdraws items as "bank notes" (stackable, 1 slot for up to 2,147,483,647 items) Selling large quantities to the GE
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
"bank notes" (stackable, 1 slot for up to 2,147,483,647 items) Selling large quantities to the GE 
⚠️ Beginner Mistake: Withdrawing in Note Mode by Accident 
You click "Withdraw-All" on your gear and suddenly your armor is a "note" that you can't wear. To fix: right-click the note in your inventory → "Withdraw as item." Always check the "Note/Item" toggle at the bottom of the bank interface before withdrawing! 

3.

m." Always check the "Note/Item" toggle at the bottom of the bank interface before withdrawing! 

3. Bank Tabs — Organize Your Life 
The bank has 8 tabs (or more with the "Bank Organiser" feature unlocked later). You can rename tabs and assign items to them. This is CRITICAL for staying organized. 
How to Set Up Bank Tabs 
Open the bank 
Click the tab name (e.g., "Tab 1") at the top of the bank interface 
Type a new name (e.g., "Combat Gear") 
Drag items into that tab to assign them
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

Recommended Tab Setup for Beginners (F2P) 
Tab # Name What to Put Inside 
1 ⚔️ Combat Gear Your best armor set, weapon, shield, amulet 
2 🍳 Food Cooked fish, bread, any food items 
3 💰 Valuables Rune items, gems, expensive drops 
4 🎣 Skilling Gear Fishing rod, bait, axe, pickaxe 
5 📦 Materials Ore, logs, raw fish, uncooked food

6 📜 Drops / Loot Unsorted drops from monsters (sort later) 
7 🎒 Spare Gear Lower-tier armor (keep as backup) 
8 🔮 Quest Items Quest-related items you might need later 

4. Inventory Management Tips 
Your inventory is only 28 slots. Good inventory management means less running back to the bank.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
ips 
Your inventory is only 28 slots. Good inventory management means less running back to the bank. Here's how to maximize it: 
Stackable vs Non-Stackable Items 
Type Examples Slots Used 
Stackable Coins, runes, arrows, bolts, feathers 1 slot (holds up to 2.1B of that item) 
Non-stackable Food, weapons, armor, raw materials 1 slot per item 
📦 Inventory Management Rules (Memorize These) 
Always fill your inventory before banking. Don't run to the bank with 10 empty slots.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
morize These) 
Always fill your inventory before banking. Don't run to the bank with 10 empty slots. Kill 10 more monsters or gather 10 more resources first. 
Drop low-value items. If an item is worth less than 100 GP and you have full inventory, drop it. Your time is worth more than the GP you'd get from selling it. 
Use the "Shift-click drop" setting. Enabled in Settings → Gameplay → Controls. Makes dropping items 10x faster. 
Carry teleport runes.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
. Enabled in Settings → Gameplay → Controls. Makes dropping items 10x faster. 
Carry teleport runes. Keep 3-5 teleport runes (Law + Air + Earth) in your inventory for quick banking trips. 

5. What Items to Keep vs Drop/Sell 
As a beginner, you'll constantly ask: "Is this item worth keeping or can I drop it?" Here's a decision framework: 
Item Value (GE Price) Action Examples 
<100 GP Drop it (not worth inventory space) Bronze items, bones (unless training Prayer), low-level herbs
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
Drop it (not worth inventory space) Bronze items, bones (unless training Prayer), low-level herbs 
100-1,000 GP Sell to GE or drop if inventory is full Iron items, cowhides, low-level fish 
1,000-10,000 GP Always keep (bank it or sell at GE) Steel/Mithril items, uncut gems, herbs 
>10,000 GP ALWAYS bank or sell (these add up fast!) Rune items, clue scrolls, dragon items, herbs (Grimy Ranarr, etc.) 
💰 The "Cowhide Trick" — Your First Income Source 
Kill cows near Lumbridge.

text:
bs (Grimy Ranarr, etc.) 
💰 The "Cowhide Trick" — Your First Income Source 
Kill cows near Lumbridge. Collect cowhides (each worth ~100-200 GP). Fill inventory (28 hides). Bank them. Once you have 50-100 hides, sell them at the Grand Exchange. This is the classic F2P beginner money method. Easy, no stats required, and you train combat while doing it! 

6. Bank Space — When You Run Out of Room 
The bank has 2,304 item slots (8 tabs × 288 items per tab).
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
. Bank Space — When You Run Out of Room 
The bank has 2,304 item slots (8 tabs × 288 items per tab). Most players never fill it, but if you're a hoarder, here's what to do: 
How to Free Up Bank Space 
Convert items to bank notes: Withdraw items as notes (they stack in your bank too if noted). Wait — actually, items ALREADY stack in the bank. Notes are only for withdrawal. 
Drop/sell low-value items: Be honest — are you really going to use those 500 Bronze arrows? No? Drop them.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
ell low-value items: Be honest — are you really going to use those 500 Bronze arrows? No? Drop them. 
Use the "Placeholders" feature: In the bank settings, enable "Placeholders" — this keeps an empty slot with the item name visible, so you know you own it but it's not taking space. 
Get a member's "Bank Organiser": Members can unlock additional bank tabs (up to 8+ with certain unlocks). More tabs = more organization options. 

7.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
ck additional bank tabs (up to 8+ with certain unlocks). More tabs = more organization options. 

7. Useful Bank Features You Should Know 
Search Bar (Top of Bank Interface) 
Type an item name → the bank shows only matching items. Saves you from scrolling through 500 items looking for your Rune scimitar. 
Deposit Inventory / Deposit Worn Items Buttons 
At the bottom of the bank interface: 
"Deposit inventory": Deposits EVERYTHING in your inventory. One click.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
ottom of the bank interface: 
"Deposit inventory": Deposits EVERYTHING in your inventory. One click. 
"Deposit worn items": Takes off all your equipped gear and deposits it. Perfect after a combat session. 
Bank PIN (Security) 
Set a Bank PIN so that even if someone hacks your account, they can't steal your bank items immediately. Set this up in: Settings → Display & Sound → Bank PIN .
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
hey can't steal your bank items immediately. Set this up in: Settings → Display & Sound → Bank PIN . 
⚠️ Don't Forget Your Bank PIN 
If you set a Bank PIN and forget it, you'll need to go through Jagex account recovery. It's a huge hassle. Write it down somewhere safe (but NOT in a file on your computer). 

8. Beginner Bank Tab Setup (Step-by-Step) 
Let's set up your bank tabs properly.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
n your computer). 

8. Beginner Bank Tab Setup (Step-by-Step) 
Let's set up your bank tabs properly. Follow these steps: 
Go to any bank and open it 
Click "Tab 1" → rename to "⚔️ Gear" 
Click "Tab 2" → rename to "🍳 Food" 
Click "Tab 3" → rename to "💰 Sell/GP" 
Click "Tab 4" → rename to "🎣 Tools" 
Click "Tab 5" → rename to "📦 Raw Materials" 
Click "Tab 6" → rename to "🔮 Quest Items" 
Click "Tab 7" → rename to "📚 Spare Gear" 
Click "Tab 8" → rename to "❓ Unsorted"

text:
"🔮 Quest Items" 
Click "Tab 7" → rename to "📚 Spare Gear" 
Click "Tab 8" → rename to "❓ Unsorted" 
Now drag each item from your bank into the appropriate tab 
Done! Your bank is now organized. 

9. Common Bank Mistakes to Avoid 
⚠️ Top 5 Bank Mistakes 
Not using bank tabs: You have 100+ items in Tab 1 with no organization. You spend 5 minutes looking for your teleport runes. Set up tabs! 
Accidentally withdrawing notes: You withdraw your armor as notes and can't equip it.
title: OSRS Bank & Inventory Management Guide 2026 — Organize Your Items Like a Pro

text:
. Set up tabs! 
Accidentally withdrawing notes: You withdraw your armor as notes and can't equip it. Check the Note/Item toggle! 
Not banking often enough: You fill your inventory, keep playing, then die and lose everything. Bank every 15-20 minutes when training. 
Hoarding worthless items: You have 500 Bronze arrows from 2024. They're worth 5 GP each. Just drop them. 
Forgetting to set a Bank PIN: Someone hacks your account and cleans out your bank in 30 seconds.

You now understand banking and inventory management! Here's what to do next: 
✅ Your Bank Action Plan 
Go to Lumbridge Castle bank chest right now 
Set up your 8 bank tabs using the naming scheme above

The Twisted Bow alone is worth over 1.1 billion GP, and even the common loot provides steady income. This guide breaks down the complete CoX loot table, the points system that determines your rewards, and exactly how much GP/hr solo and team CoX generates in 2026.

2. How the CoX Points & Loot System Works 
CoX uses a personal points system that directly determines your rewards. Points are earned by dealing damage in each puzzle room and to the Great Olm (final boss).

Unique Drop Chance: Your personal points divided by the team's total points determines your share of the unique roll.

6. CoX Profit Tips 
Maximize personal points. Deal damage in every room. Olm is the biggest point source — learn efficient Olm cycles (4:1 melee hand, 3:0 mage hand) to maximize your contribution. 
Don't die. One death reduces your personal points by 30-40%, essentially wiping out a third of your potential rewards. Prioritize survival over speed until you're experienced. 
Scout for fast layouts.

potential rewards. Prioritize survival over speed until you're experienced. 
Scout for fast layouts. Not all room layouts are equal. Scout for raids without Vespula, Vanguards, or Tekton if you're pushing for speed. Faster raids = more unique rolls per hour. 
Make overloads. If your team has 78+ Herblore, gather the supplies inside the raid to make overloads. They boost your combat stats by 5-19 levels for 5 minutes each and dramatically increase DPS. 
Avoid masses.

Use the Raids helper plugin on RuneLite. It tracks your personal points in real-time, shows Olm attack cycles, and helps you optimize your damage output.

7. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Is solo CoX worth it for GP? 
A: Yes, but it requires significant practice. Solo CoX with deathless runs and 30K+ points averages 2M-3M GP/hr including uniques. However, learning to solo efficiently takes 50-100 raids. Start in teams to learn the mechanics, then transition to solos once you're comfortable with all rooms and Olm.

ams to learn the mechanics, then transition to solos once you're comfortable with all rooms and Olm. 

Q: How long does it take to see a Twisted Bow drop? 
A: On average, 750 solo raids at 30K+ points. However, this is an average — some players go 2,000+ raids dry, while others see a bow in their first 100. The expected value is what matters: even without the bow, the other uniques (Kodai, Ancestral, Claws) provide significant income that sustains you between big drops.
title: OSRS Chambers of Xeric Loot & Profit Guide 2026 — Twisted Bow, Unique Drops & GP/hr

text:
er uniques (Kodai, Ancestral, Claws) provide significant income that sustains you between big drops. 

Q: What's better for GP — CoX or ToB? 
A: CoX is more accessible; ToB has higher potential GP/hr. Theatre of Blood (Raids 2) averages 4M-6M+ GP/hr with uniques, but requires higher stats, better gear, and a reliable team. CoX is more beginner-friendly, has lower entry requirements, and solo CoX is viable while solo ToB is extremely difficult. Start with CoX, then graduate to ToB.

Chambers of Xeric is the quintessential OSRS endgame activity. Every raid is a lottery ticket, and the Twisted Bow is the billion-GP jackpot. Even without hitting the bow, the consistent common loot and other uniques make CoX one of the best long-term money makers in the game. Learn the mechanics, find a team, and start raiding.

text:
Q: How does the Chambers of Xeric loot system work?
A: CoX loot is based on personal points earned during the raid. Points are accumulated by dealing damage in each room and to the Great Olm (final boss). At the end of the raid, your personal points determine both your common loot value and your chance at a unique drop. More points = better common loot AND higher unique chance. Dying reduces your points by 30-40%, dramatically lowering your loot.
title: OSRS Chambers of Xeric Loot & Profit Guide 2026 — Twisted Bow, Unique Drops & GP/hr

text:
Q: What is the drop rate for the Twisted Bow in CoX?
A: The Twisted Bow has an approximate drop rate of 1/750 from solo raids at 30,000+ personal points. In teams, the overall unique drop rate scales with total team points — a team of 3 with 90,000 total points has roughly a 1/10-1/15 chance of seeing ANY unique per raid. When a unique drops, it's randomly assigned to one player weighted by their personal point contribution.
title: OSRS Chambers of Xeric Loot & Profit Guide 2026 — Twisted Bow, Unique Drops & GP/hr

text:
Q: How much GP per hour is Chambers of Xeric in 2026?
A: Solo CoX averages 1.5M-3M GP/hr when factoring in unique drop probability over many raids. Common loot alone (without uniques) yields 400K-800K GP/hr. In efficient 3-4 player teams, GP/hr can reach 3M-5M+ with unique drops averaged over time. The profit is heavily swingy — you may go 50+ raids without a unique, then hit a Twisted Bow (1.1B+ GP) or Kodai Wand (30-40M) in a single raid.
title: OSRS Chambers of Xeric Loot & Profit Guide 2026 — Twisted Bow, Unique Drops & GP/hr

text:
Q: What stats and gear do I need for Chambers of Xeric?
A: Recommended minimum stats: 80+ Attack/Strength/Defence, 80+ Ranged (85+ ideal), 80+ Magic, 70+ Prayer (Rigour and Augury recommended), 78+ Herblore (to make overloads inside the raid). Gear: Ranged setup (Toxic Blowpipe or Bow of Faerdhinen), Magic setup (Trident of the Swamp or Sang Staff), Melee setup (Abyssal Whip/Dragon Hunter Lance). Budget entry gear runs ~30-50M GP total.

1. Why Flipping Works for New Players 
Flipping exploits the Grand Exchange's supply-and-demand mechanics. Players who need items immediately pay a premium (the instabuy price ). Players who want to sell quickly accept a discount (the instasell price ).
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
um (the instabuy price ). Players who want to sell quickly accept a discount (the instasell price ). The gap between these two prices is the margin — and that's where flippers profit. 
Here's the beauty for new players: flipping requires no stats. No Slayer level. No quest completions. No combat gear. All you need is a small amount of gold, some patience, and the knowledge of which items have the best margins.
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
ed is a small amount of gold, some patience, and the knowledge of which items have the best margins. You can even flip while training other skills — set your offers, go train, come back and collect profit. 
💡 The Golden Rule of Flipping: Buy at the instasell price (what impatient sellers accept) and sell at the instabuy price (what impatient buyers pay). Your profit is the spread. The wider the spread and the higher the volume, the better the flip. 

2.

Your profit is the spread. The wider the spread and the higher the volume, the better the flip. 

2. Getting Started — The Margin Check Process 
Before committing your entire cash stack to an item, you must check its current margin. Here is the exact 5-step process: 
Buy 1 item at 20% above market price. This is an instant buy. Note the exact price it buys at — this is the current instabuy price . 
Sell that 1 item at 20% below market price. This is an instant sell.
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
s the current instabuy price . 
Sell that 1 item at 20% below market price. This is an instant sell. Note the exact price it sells at — this is the current instasell price . 
Calculate your margin: Subtract the sell price from the buy price. This is your profit per item. 
Evaluate the margin: If the margin is at least 1% of the item's value, it's worth flipping. For a 200 GP item, you want at least a 2 GP margin.

text:
ast 1% of the item's value, it's worth flipping. For a 200 GP item, you want at least a 2 GP margin. 
Place bulk offers: Now place a buy offer for your target quantity at the instasell price, and when it fills, list it at the instabuy price. 
This test costs you the spread on exactly 1 item (typically 5–30 GP) but reveals exactly how much profit you can expect per flip cycle.

6. Advanced Flipping Tips 
Spread your capital across 2–3 items. Never put all your GP into one item — if the margin collapses, you're stuck. Diversifying across cannonballs, nature runes, and coal keeps your flips moving. 
Use the GE's overnight window. Place buy offers before you log off for the night. When you log back in, they're often filled at great prices because fewer players are online.

A: You can start flipping with as little as 10K-50K GP. Items like fire runes, feathers, and eye of newt have buy limits in the thousands and margins of 1-5 GP each.

📈 Start Small, Scale Up

text:
Q: How much GP do I need to start flipping on the Grand Exchange?
A: You can start flipping with as little as 10K-50K GP. Items like fire runes, feathers, and eye of newt have buy limits in the thousands and margins of 1-5 GP each. With 100K capital, you can flip cannonballs and nature runes for 50K-200K GP profit per hour of passive flipping.
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
Q: What is margin checking and how do I do it?
A: Margin checking tests an item's buy-sell spread. Buy 1 of the item at 20% above market price (instant buy), note the price. Then sell that 1 item at 20% below market price (instant sell), note the price. The difference is your margin. If the margin is at least 1% of the item's value, it's worth flipping. This test costs you the spread on 1 item but reveals exact profit potential.
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
Q: What are the best items for new players to flip with low GP?
A: The best low-capital flip items are: Cannonballs (3-8 GP margin, 10K buy limit, ~30K capital needed), Nature Runes (5-15 GP margin, 25K buy limit, ~40K capital), Fire Runes (1-3 GP margin, 25K buy limit, ~10K capital), Coal (10-30 GP margin, 25K buy limit, ~50K capital), and Iron Ore (5-15 GP margin, 25K buy limit, ~25K capital). These all have very high trade volumes and stable margins.
title: OSRS Cheap Flipping Methods for New Players 2026 — 100K Capital Guide

text:
Q: How fast do GE offers fill when flipping?
A: Buy offers at the instasell price typically fill within 30 minutes to 2 hours for high-volume items like cannonballs and nature runes. Selling at the instabuy price is similar. During peak hours (evenings and weekends US time), offers fill faster due to more players online. Avoid flipping 30 minutes before or after a game update, as prices can be volatile.

This is the best choice for budget accounts and Ironmen.

Crafting lava runes without Magic Imbue. Without Magic Imbue, you need earth runes in your inventory -- losing one essence slot per trip.

Q: Can I get 99 Runecrafting completely free? 
A: Yes! Guardians of the Rift (GOTR) costs zero GP and even provides passive profit from the rune rewards. The XP rates are competitive with paid methods (30K-70K XP/hr depending on level). Many Ironmen get 99 Runecrafting exclusively through GOTR.

Q: What is the absolute cheapest method for 99 Runecrafting?
A: Guardians of the Rift (GOTR) is the completely free method -- it costs zero GP and even provides passive profit from rune rewards. For direct runecrafting, Lava Runes are the cheapest at ~8-9M GP total to 99 (after selling the lava runes crafted). ZMI costs ~20M GP total. Blood runes at 77+ can be close to break-even or slight profit depending on blood rune prices. GOTR is the best choice for budget accounts and Ironmen.

Q: How much XP per hour do lava runes give?
A: Lava runes give 55,000-72,000 XP per hour depending on your efficiency and Runecrafting level. At lower levels (23-50), expect 35,000-45,000 XP/hr. At 50-75 with all pouches, expect 50,000-60,000 XP/hr. At 75+ with the Giant Pouch (and Colossal Pouch from GOTR), expect 65,000-72,000 XP/hr. Tick-perfect lava rune crafting can reach 80,000+ XP/hr but requires intense focus.

A: Lunar Diplomacy is highly recommended for lava runes because it unlocks the Magic Imbue spell, which removes the need to carry earth runes in your inventory -- freeing one slot for an extra pure essence per trip. Without Magic Imbue, you need an earth talisman or earth runes, reducing efficiency. For ZMI and GOTR, Lunar Diplomacy is not required. For maximum efficiency at lava runes, also complete Dream Mentor for Spellbook Swap.

A: The Colossal Pouch is obtained from the Guardians of the Rift minigame and holds 40 pure essence (compared to 12 for the Giant Pouch). It requires 85 Runecrafting to use and costs 1,000 Abyssal Pearls from the GOTR reward shop. It dramatically increases trips per hour for lava runes and ZMI crafting because you carry more essence per inventory. The pouch degrades and must be repaired with abyssal pearls or by the Dark Mage.
title: OSRS Cheapest 99 Runecrafting Guide 2026 -- Lava Runes, ZMI & GOTR
