1. Combat Basics — Understanding the Combat Triangle 
OSRS uses a combat triangle system: Melee beats Ranged, Ranged beats Magic, Magic beats Melee. Each style has strengths and weaknesses against the others.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
, Ranged beats Magic, Magic beats Melee. Each style has strengths and weaknesses against the others. As a beginner, understanding this early saves you hours of frustration. 
Style Strong Against Weak Against Best For

🏹 Ranged Magic Melee Safe spotting, multi-combat, range-based bosses

How Combat Level Is Calculated

text:
agic Melee Ranged Magic-based bosses, utility (teleports, alchemy) 
How Combat Level Is Calculated 
Your Combat Level is calculated from: Attack + Strength + Defence + (Hitpoints ÷ 2) + (Prayer ÷ 2) + (Ranged ÷ 2) + (Magic ÷ 2) , all divided by 4. This means Ranged and Magic contribute half as much per level compared to melee stats. Plan your build accordingly.

🎯 Recommended Beginner Combat Path (Priority Order) 
Melee first (Levels 1–60/70) — Cheapest, simplest, prepares you for all quests 
Ranged (Levels 1–60) — Safe, powerful for Slayer and bosses 
Magic (Levels 1–55+) — High Alchemy for GP, teleports for mobility, required for quests 
Slayer (Combat 60+) — Combines all combat styles, best long-term training method

⚔️ The Combat Style Tab — This Changes Everything 
When you have a weapon equipped, open your Combat Options tab (sword icon in the game interface). You can choose your attack style: 
Aggressive — Trains Strength (hit harder, higher max hit) 
Accurate — Trains Attack (more accurate, unlocks better weapons) 
Defensive — Trains Defence (take less damage, wear better armor)
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
ccurate, unlocks better weapons) 
Defensive — Trains Defence (take less damage, wear better armor) 
Controlled — Trains Attack + Strength + Defence slowly (NOT recommended for beginners) 
Rule of thumb: Set to Aggressive until Strength is 1 level above Attack, then switch to Accurate to catch up. Never use Controlled early on. 

Understanding Attack Speed (Ticks) 
Every weapon in OSRS has an attack speed measured in game ticks (0.6 seconds per tick).

Every weapon in OSRS has an attack speed measured in game ticks (0.6 seconds per tick). Faster weapons = more XP per hour but lower damage per hit. Slower weapons = higher damage but slower XP.

2. Core Training Principles Every Beginner Must Know 
Principle 1: Attack > Strength > Defence Priority 
For melee training, the most efficient order is: Attack to 60 → Strength to 70+ → Defence to 40–70 . Why? Attack 60 unlocks the Dragon Scimitar (best melee weapon until 70 Attack). Strength gives you higher max hit (more damage per swing).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
itar (best melee weapon until 70 Attack). Strength gives you higher max hit (more damage per swing). Defence is least impactful for DPS — only raise it when you need to wear specific armor. 
Principle 2: Safe-Spotting = Free Training 
Many monsters can be killed without taking any damage by standing behind a tree, rock, or fence that the monster can't path around. This is called "safe-spotting" and it means you don't need food. Read our Safe Spots Guide for exact locations.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
led "safe-spotting" and it means you don't need food. Read our Safe Spots Guide for exact locations. 
Principle 3: Aggressive Monsters Save you Time 
Aggressive monsters (marked with a yellow skull icon in the combat interface) will automatically attack you when you're nearby. This means you can AFK (away from keyboard) while they come to you. Non-aggressive monsters require you to click on them every few seconds.

💡 XP Multipliers & Boosted XP 
Some activities give bonus XP : Fighting in Multi-combat areas (marked by a blue skull icon) lets you hit multiple monsters at once with Area-of-Effect attacks (Magic Burst/Barrage, or Chinchompas for Ranged). This can triple your XP rates.

3. Melee Training: Complete 1–70 Step-by-Step Route 
This is the most detailed section. Follow this exact route and you'll go from Level 1 to 70 Melee in the fastest, cheapest way possible. 
Step 1: Levels 1–10 (Chickens & Cows) 
Where: Chicken coop north of Lumbridge (north exit, cross the bridge, go north). 
What to do: Equip your Bronze sword (from Tutorial Island). Set combat mode to Aggressive . Kill chickens. They die in 1–2 hits.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
e sword (from Tutorial Island). Set combat mode to Aggressive . Kill chickens. They die in 1–2 hits. Pick up feathers — they sell for ~5 GP each at the Grand Exchange. 
When you reach Combat Level 5: Cows east of Lumbridge become available. Cows have more HP and drop cowhides (200 GP each at GE). Switch to cows at Level 10. 
Time: ~15 minutes to reach Level 10 in all melee stats. 
Loot to pick up: Feathers, cowhides (bank them or sell at GE), bones (bury for Prayer XP or save for later).

Step 2: Levels 10–20 (Al-Kharid Warriors — F2P Option) 
Where: Al-Kharid Palace (south of Al-Kharid bank, go through the palace gates). 
What to do: Equip Iron/Steel armor (buy from Grand Exchange for ~5k GP total). Bring 10–15 Salmon (buy from GE ~100 GP each). Attack Al-Kharid Warriors. They have low Defence and decent HP.

Time: ~30 minutes from Level 10 to 20. 
Step 3: Levels 20–40 (Barbarians or Stronghold of Security) 
Where: Barbarian Village (use Varrock teleport, walk west). 
What to do: Kill Barbarians.

What to do: Kill Barbarians. They're aggressive (will come to you), have low Defence, and drop herbs (valuable). Stronghold of Security is in the same area — complete all 4 floors for free Armor, GP, and Emote rewards . The Stronghold alone can get you from Level 20 to 30. 
Gear at Level 20: Mithril armor (buy from GE, ~10k GP set). Weapon: Steel scimitar or better.

Step 4: Levels 40–60 (Flesh Crawlers or Hill Giants) 
Option A — Flesh Crawlers (F2P/P2P): Stronghold of Security Floor 2. They are aggressive, cluster in groups, and have low Defence. Great AFK spot. Bring 5–10 Lobsters just in case. 
Option B — Hill Giants (Members): Edgeville Dungeon (requires 22 GP to enter, or use a Brass Key). They drop Big Bones (burry for Prayer XP) and have decent HP.

Time: ~2–3 hours from Level 40 to 60. 
Step 5: Levels 60–70 (Experiments or Rock Crabs) 
Option A — Experiments (Members, HIGHLY RECOMMENDED): Near Canifis (after Creature of Fenkenstrain quest, which has no requirements).

How to get there: Teleport to Canifis (Fairy Ring code: CKS, or walk from Port Phasmatys after Ghosts Ahoy quest). Talk to Dr. Fenkenstrain to start the quest (can be completed in 5 minutes). Then go to the Experiment cave south of the castle.

4. Melee Training: 70–99 Efficient Routes 
Once you reach Level 70, you have two choices: continue manual training, or start Slayer (recommended).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
e you reach Level 70, you have two choices: continue manual training, or start Slayer (recommended). Slayer gives combat XP while also training the Slayer skill, which unlocks boss access and unique drops. 
Method A: Slayer (Best Overall, 70–99) 
Assign a Slayer master (Turael for easy tasks, Mazchna for medium, Konar for harder). Each task gives a specific monster to kill. This is the most engaging way to reach 99 because you're always killing different monsters and making GP from drops.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
engaging way to reach 99 because you're always killing different monsters and making GP from drops. 
Best Slayer monsters for Melee XP (70–99): Gargoyles (75 Slayer), Nechryaels (80 Slayer), Abyssal Demons (85 Slayer), Armarx (95 Slayer). 
Method B: Nightmare Zone (NMZ) — AFK 99 
NMZ is a P2P mini-game where you fight bosses from quests in a dream world. With Dragon Scimitar + Absorbtion potions , you can AFK for 20-minute intervals. XP rate: 60,000–80,000 XP/hr.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

n Scimitar + Absorbtion potions , you can AFK for 20-minute intervals. XP rate: 60,000–80,000 XP/hr. Requires completion of at least 5 quests that have bosses (Fight Arena, Tree Gnome Village, etc.). 
Method C: Manual Grinding (Fastest XP) 
If you just want 99 fast and don't care about GP or variety: 
Levels 70–80: Ammonite Crabs (Fossil Island) — 50,000–70,000 XP/hr, fully AFK 
Levels 80–90: Sand Crabs (Hosidius) or Monkey Guards (Ape Atoll, 65+ Defence) — 60,000–80,000 XP/hr

Levels 90–99: NMZ or Slayer — Slayer is more profitable 
Total time to 99 Melee from 70: ~150–200 hours of active play (or ~300 hours AFK at crabs).

Levels 30–60: Rock Crabs (Members) or Ogress Warriors (F2P)

Levels 70–99: Slayer with Ranged 
Most Slayer tasks are best completed with Ranged because you can safe-spot and use Prayer protection. Tasks like Black Dragons, Demons, and Bosses are all Ranged-friendly.

🏹 Essential: Get Ava's Device (Saves Thousands in Ammo) 
Complete Animal Magnetism quest (requires 35 Woodcutting, 30 Crafting, 18 Slayer, 19 Ranged). This unlocks Ava's Attractor (Ranged 30+) or Ava's Accumulator (Ranged 50+) .

18 Slayer, 19 Ranged). This unlocks Ava's Attractor (Ranged 30+) or Ava's Accumulator (Ranged 50+) . These devices automatically collect 80% of your spent arrows/bolts . Without Ava's, you'll spend 500k+ GP on ammo per hour of training. With Ava's, that drops to ~50k GP per hour. 
How to start Animal Magnetism: Talk to Ava in Draynor Manor (south of Draynor Village). The quest takes ~30 minutes and has low requirements.

6. Magic Training: Complete 1–99 Route 
Magic is unique because it's useful for combat, utility (teleports), and money-making (High Alchemy) . Train it alongside your other combat styles. 
Levels 1–7: Imp Catcher Quest (Instant XP) 
Complete Imp Catcher quest (talk to Wizard Mizgog on the top floor of the Wizards' Tower, south of Draynor).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
Imp Catcher quest (talk to Wizard Mizgog on the top floor of the Wizards' Tower, south of Draynor). Rewards: 875 Magic XP (instant Level 7) + a sapphire amulet. Takes 5 minutes. 
Levels 7–25: Splashing (AFK Method) 
What is Splashing? Equip cursed gear (cursed gorilla armor or just wear no armor) to reduce your Magic accuracy to -64 or lower. Then cast the lowest level spell (Wind Strike) on a monster with 0% magic defence (chickens, crabs).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
Then cast the lowest level spell (Wind Strike) on a monster with 0% magic defence (chickens, crabs). Because your accuracy is negative, you'll never hit, but you'll still get full Magic XP per cast. This is 100% AFK. 
Cost: ~100k GP for runes to reach Level 25. XP rate: 5,000–8,000 XP/hr (fully AFK, you can watch a video while doing this). 
Where: Rock Crabs (they have 0 Magic defence). Stand next to a crab and cast continuously.

g this).

Levels 25–55: Superheat Item (Smithing XP + Money) 
Superheat Item (Level 25 Magic) lets you smelt bars from ore without a furnace. Requirements: 1 Nature rune + 1 Fire rune per cast. Use on Iron ore → Iron bars (profit ~100 GP per bar after rune costs). 
Why this is amazing: You get Magic XP + Smithing XP + small profit simultaneously.

ter rune costs). 
Why this is amazing: You get Magic XP + Smithing XP + small profit simultaneously. 53 Magic lets you superheat Gold bars (requires 40 Smithing). Gold bars give 56.2 XP per cast and can be profitable with the Goldsmith's Gauntlets (unlocked from Family Crest quest). 
XP rate: 20,000–30,000 XP/hr. 
Level 55+: High Level Alchemy (Passive Income) 
High Alchemy (Level 55 Magic) converts items into GP at 60% of their GE value.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
emy (Passive Income) 
High Alchemy (Level 55 Magic) converts items into GP at 60% of their GE value. This is the single most important money-making spell in the game. 
What to alch: Yew Longbows (buy uncut yew logs, fletch into longbows, alch them), Maple Longbows, or any item you craft/buy below 60% GE value. 
Pro tip: Buy a Magic Shortbow (unstrung) from the GE for ~1,500 GP, string it (10 GP), alch it for ~3,900 GP. Profit + Magic XP.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
w (unstrung) from the GE for ~1,500 GP, string it (10 GP), alch it for ~3,900 GP. Profit + Magic XP. 
XP rate: 60,000–80,000 XP/hr (if you have the GP to buy items to alch). In practice, most players alch while doing other activities (Slayer, questing) for passive XP. 
Levels 55–99: Combat Magic (Bursts/Barrages) 
Once you have 70+ Magic, the fastest way to 99 is bursting/barraging Slayer tasks in multi-combat areas:
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
ou have 70+ Magic, the fastest way to 99 is bursting/barraging Slayer tasks in multi-combat areas: 
Levels 55–70: Ice Burst (Slayer 65) at Dust Devils or Nechryaels — 50,000–80,000 XP/hr 
Levels 70–94: Ice Barrage (Slayer 85) at Maniacal Monkeys or NMZ — 100,000–200,000 XP/hr 
Levels 94–99: Ice Barrage at Zulrah (boss) or raids — 150,000–300,000 XP/hr 
Cost warning: Bursting/Barraging costs 3–10M GP per 99. Most players combine this with Slayer so the drops offset the rune costs.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

ing costs 3–10M GP per 99. Most players combine this with Slayer so the drops offset the rune costs. 
✨ Magic 55 Is the Biggest Magic Milestone 
High Alchemy unlocks at Level 55. From this point on, you can pay for all your other skills just by alching items you get from Slayer or skilling. Rush to Magic 55 as fast as possible — it's a total game-changer for your economy.

10. When & How to Start Slayer — The Best Combat Training Method 
Slayer is a skill where you kill specific monsters assigned by an NPC called a Slayer Master . It's the #1 best way to train combat past Level 60 because: 
You get combat XP + Slayer XP simultaneously (two skills at once) 
Unique monsters drop valuable loot (Dragon items, herbs, seeds, resources)

Unique monsters drop valuable loot (Dragon items, herbs, seeds, resources) 
It's never boring — every task is a different monster in a different location 
Unlocks boss access (KBD, Kalphite Queen, Zulrah, etc.) through higher-level tasks

11. Advanced Combat Training Methods (70+ Combat) 
Once you have 70+ in your chosen combat style, these methods become available and offer much higher XP rates. 
Nightmare Zone (NMZ) — AFK Melee 60k–80k XP/hr 
NMZ is a P2P mini-game. You fight quest bosses in a dream world. Requirements: Complete 5+ quests with bosses (Fight Arena, Tree Gnome Village, Vampyre Slayer, etc.).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
equirements: Complete 5+ quests with bosses (Fight Arena, Tree Gnome Village, Vampyre Slayer, etc.). Setup: Dragon Scimitar + Absorbtion potions (4) + Overload potions (optional). Method: Drink Absorbtion, stand in a corner, let monsters hit you (Absorbtion absorbs all damage), and AFK for 20 minutes. XP rate: 60,000–80,000 XP/hr. 
Bursting/Barraging — Magic 100k–300k XP/hr 
Use Ice Burst (Level 70 Magic, 65 Slayer) or Ice Barrage (Level 94 Magic, 85 Slayer) on monsters in multi-combat areas.

However, cannonballs cost ~7 GP each, and the cannon uses 1 cannonball every 2 seconds. That's ~12,600 cannonballs per hour = ~90k GP/hour in ammo costs. Only use a cannon if you have GP to burn.

12. Combat Training FAQs — 15 Questions Every Beginner Asks 
Should I train Attack, Strength, or Defence first? Train Attack to 60 first (unlocks Dragon Scimitar, the best melee weapon until 70 Attack). Then Strength to 70+ (higher max hit = more damage per swing). Then Defence to 40–70 as needed for armor unlocks. Defence is the least impactful for DPS — prioritize Attack and Strength.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
s needed for armor unlocks. Defence is the least impactful for DPS — prioritize Attack and Strength. A pure Strength build (1 Attack, 99 Strength, 1 Defence) is viable for PVP, but for PVE (PvM), you want balanced stats. 
How much food should I bring to training sessions? At Levels 1–40: Bring 5–10 pieces of food (Salmon/Trout, ~50 GP each). You can bank frequently. At Levels 40–70: Bring 10–20 pieces (Lobster, ~150 GP each).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
~50 GP each). You can bank frequently. At Levels 40–70: Bring 10–20 pieces (Lobster, ~150 GP each). At Level 70+: With good armor and Defence, you may not need food at all (especially at crabs/Experiments with proper safe-spotting). Pro tip: Always carry a Ring of Recoil (475 GP) — it reflects 10% of damage dealt back to the monster. This effectively gives you free damage without using food. 
Is it worth buying a Dwarf Multicannon for early training? Not until you have 500k+ GP to spare.

d. 
Is it worth buying a Dwarf Multicannon for early training? Not until you have 500k+ GP to spare. The cannon costs 750k GP to buy, and cannonballs cost ~90k GP per hour of use. For early training (Levels 1–60), manual grinding is faster and cheaper. Save your GP for Rune Scimitar (20k), Dragon Scimitar (60k), and Ava's Device (free with quest) first. Only buy a cannon if you're doing Slayer tasks where cannons are effective (e.g., Dagannoths, Kalphite).

What's the fastest way to 99 combat? For Melee: Slayer (70–99) is the most efficient because you get Slayer XP too. For pure speed (ignoring cost): NMZ (Nightmare Zone) for AFK, or Sand/Ammonite Crabs for semi-AFK. For Ranged: Chinning (Red Chinchompas) is fastest but costs 5M+ GP. For Magic: Ice Barrage bursting Slayer tasks .

inning (Red Chinchompas) is fastest but costs 5M+ GP. For Magic: Ice Barrage bursting Slayer tasks . But as a beginner, just enjoy the content — the levels come naturally if you do quests and Slayer. 
Do I need to train all three combat styles (Melee, Ranged, Magic)? Yes, eventually. Most endgame content requires all three styles. Melee for general bossing, Ranged for safe DPS and specific bosses (Zulrah, Vorkath), Magic for teleports, High Alchemy, and Magic-based bosses ( Chambers of Xeric).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
s (Zulrah, Vorkath), Magic for teleports, High Alchemy, and Magic-based bosses ( Chambers of Xeric). However, as a beginner, focus on one style first (usually Melee) to get to 60+ combat, then branch out. Trying to train all three at once spreads your XP too thin. 
What's the best free-to-play (F2P) combat training route? F2P route: Chickens (1–10) → Cows (10–20) → Al-Kharid Warriors (20–40) → Moss Giants in Varrock Sewers (40–60) → Ogress Warriors in Corsair Cove (60+, after Dragon Slayer I) .

ss Giants in Varrock Sewers (40–60) → Ogress Warriors in Corsair Cove (60+, after Dragon Slayer I) . F2P has fewer options, but Ogress Warriors are surprisingly good (high HP, good drops). Consider becoming a member at Level 40+ for access to Rock Crabs and Slayer — it's 10x more efficient. 
How does the "Attack Speed" of weapons affect my XP rates? Faster weapons (4 ticks = 2.4 seconds per hit) give more XP per hour because you hit more often.
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text: es? Faster weapons (4 ticks = 2.4 seconds per hit) give more XP per hour because you hit more often. Slower weapons (6 ticks = 3.6 seconds) hit harder but give less XP per hour. For training, always use the fastest weapon available for your level: Dragon Scimitar (4 ticks) > Dragon Longsword (5 ticks) > Dragon 2h Sword (6 ticks). The only exception is if you're bursting/barraging with Magic — then tick timing matters for multi-hitting.

Why do some players say " train Defence last"? Defence only affects how often you get hit and how much damage you take . It doesn't increase your DPS (damage per second). Attack increases accuracy (you hit more often), Strength increases max hit (you hit harder). For pure DPS efficiency, train Defence last.

How do I safe-spot monsters? (Step-by-step) Safe-spotting means attacking a monster without it being able to hit you back. How: Stand behind an obstacle (tree, rock, fence, or pillar) that the monster can't walk through.

What's the best way to train Hitpoints (HP) to 99? Hitpoints XP is gained automatically when you take damage or deal damage. You don't need to "train" HP separately. It will level up naturally as you train Attack/Strength/Defence. However, if you want to boost HP faster: Eat food during combat (each bite heals you and gives small HP XP), or get hit more often (train with lower Defence gear).

(each bite heals you and gives small HP XP), or get hit more often (train with lower Defence gear). Most players reach 99 HP naturally by the time they have 90+ in other combat stats. 
Should I use a special attack weapon for training? Special attacks consume Special Attack energy (shown as a bar under your weapon). Most special attacks are not worth using for training because they drain energy you could use on bosses later.

For general training, just use normal attacks.

Levels 1–40: ~10k GP (food + basic armor) 
Levels 40–60: ~50k GP (Rune Scimitar + Rune armor + food) 
Levels 60–70: ~200k GP (Dragon Scimitar + Dragon armor + food)

What's the difference between "Controlled" and "Aggressive" combat styles? Controlled distributes XP equally across Attack, Strength, and Defence (33% each). It's slow and inefficient. Aggressive gives 100% XP to Strength (hit harder). Accurate gives 100% XP to Attack (more accurate).
title: OSRS Combat Training Guide for Beginners 2026 — Levels 1 to 99

text:
Aggressive gives 100% XP to Strength (hit harder). Accurate gives 100% XP to Attack (more accurate). Defensive gives 100% XP to Defence (take less damage). Rule: Never use Controlled. Switch between Aggressive and Accurate depending on which stat you want to train. Use Defensive only if you need Defence levels for a specific armor unlock.

Complete Waterfall Quest before you grind. It gives you Attack 30 + Strength 30 for free.

OSRS combat isn't just 'click monster, deal damage.' There's a rock-paper-scissors system called the Combat Triangle that determines which style beats which. Understanding this before you train will save you from making expensive mistakes.

1. What Is the Combat Triangle? 
The Combat Triangle is the core system that determines which combat style has the advantage over others.
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

text:
Combat Triangle is the core system that determines which combat style has the advantage over others. It works like Rock-Paper-Scissors: 
⚔️ The Three-Way Relationship 
🗡️ Melee beats 🏹 Ranged (Melee has an advantage against ranged users) 
🏹 Ranged beats ✨ Magic (Ranged has an advantage against mages) 
✨ Magic beats 🗡️ Melee (Magic has an advantage against melee users) 

What "advantage" means in practice: If you use a style that has the advantage, you'll hit more often and take less damage.
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

text:

💡 Important: The Triangle Only Matters in PvP and Some PvM 
Against most NPCs (monsters), the combat triangle doesn't matter much. NPCs have fixed weaknesses. A general rule: Melee for most things, Magic for monsters with high defence, Ranged for safety (attack from distance).
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

lee for most things, Magic for monsters with high defence, Ranged for safety (attack from distance). The triangle matters most when fighting other players. 

2. Melee — The Starting Point for Everyone 
Melee is fighting up close with swords, axes, maces, or fists. Every new player starts with melee because it's the simplest and requires no ammunition (unlike ranged) or runes (unlike magic).

3. Ranged — Attack From a Distance 
Ranged lets you attack monsters from a distance using bows, crossbows, or thrown weapons. The big advantage: you can often attack without taking damage (the monster can't reach you).
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

text:
eapons. The big advantage: you can often attack without taking damage (the monster can't reach you). 
The 2 Ranged Stats 
Skill What It Does 
Ranged Determines your accuracy and what bows/crossbows you can use 
Defence (Same as melee) Helps you take less damage 
Ranged Ammunition — You Need These! 
Unlike melee (which needs no ammo), ranged requires arrows (for bows) or bolts (for crossbows). If you run out, you can't attack.

Bows: Shortbow (fast, less accurate) / Longbow (slower, more accurate) / Composite bow (members) 
Crossbows: Slower but hit harder. Need bolts. 
Ammunition costs: Bronze arrows are cheap. Higher-tier arrows get expensive fast. 
💰 Ranged Is More Expensive Than Melee 
You'll constantly spend money on arrows/bolts. As a beginner with little gold, melee is cheaper.

4. Magic — Spells, Runes, and Ancient Magicks 
Magic uses spells that consume runes (consumable items). Magic is extremely powerful for certain tasks (high-damage spells, teleportation, utility spells) but requires constant rune purchases.

Important Magic Concepts 
Spellbook: You start with the Standard spellbook. Later you can unlock Ancient Magicks (powerful multi-target spells) and Lunar spellbook (utility spells). 
Runes: Each spell consumes specific runes.

Magic weapons: Staves (like the Air Staff) provide infinite runes of one type. Save you money!

No ammo cost: You never run out of "ammunition" with melee 
Simple: Walk up to monster, click it, done 
High HP XP: Melee training also gives you Hitpoints XP (you take damage while training)

7. Understanding Your Combat Stats (The Combat Level Formula) 
Your Combat Level determines which monsters you can fight and which players you get matched with in PvP.
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

text:
Combat Level determines which monsters you can fight and which players you get matched with in PvP. Here's the formula (simplified): 
⚔️ Combat Level Formula 
Combat Level = 
Base: (Defence + Hitpoints + Prayer/2 + Magic/2 + Range/2) / 4 
Plus: Max(Attack+Strength, 2×Magic, 2×Range) / 4 
(Don't worry — you don't need to calculate this manually. The game shows your combat level in the skill tab.) 

What Combat Level Means in Practice

9. Common Combat Mistakes to Avoid 
⚠️ Top 5 Combat Mistakes Beginners Make 
Never changing attack style: You've been training "Accurate" (Attack XP) for 20 levels and wonder why your Strength is still level 1.
title: OSRS Combat Triangle Explained 2026 — Melee, Ranged & Magic for Beginners

text:
ve been training "Accurate" (Attack XP) for 20 levels and wonder why your Strength is still level 1. Check your combat tab! 
Not bringing food: You run out of food in the middle of a fight and die, losing all your items. Always bring more food than you think you need. 
Not checking NPC weakness: Hitting a monster with the wrong style (e.g., slashing a stab-weak monster) means you miss 50% of your hits.

Spending all gold on gear too early: You buy Rune armor at level 35 Defence (can't wear it!) or buy gear before having a money-making method. Build income first! 
AFK combat without checking: OSRS has an AFK logout timer (about 5 minutes). You walk away and come back to a logged-out character. Set a timer!

Quest: Song of the Elves Supply Cost: ZERO GP

1. How the Gauntlet Works 
Enter with nothing . You have 7:30 minutes to gather, craft, and prepare. Craft Tier 1/2 armor + two Tier 3 weapons + 20-25 paddlefish. Fight Hunllef. Win = loot. Die = lose nothing but time.

4. Hunllef Boss Mechanics 
Mechanic Strategy 
🔄 Prayer Switch Hunllef switches every 6 attacks. Count: 1-2-3-4-5-6 → switch prayer. 
🌪️ Tornadoes Run, don't attack. Straight lines along edges, diagonal cuts to escape. #1 killer. 
🟦 Floor Hazards Move off blue tiles immediately. Movement > DPS. 
🛡️ Protection Prayer Hunllef protects from your last style. Switch weapons every 6 attacks. 

5. Tornado Survival Formula 
STOP attacking. DPS doesn't matter if dead.

Use true tile indicator (RuneLite). 
Eat to full HP when tornadoes spawn. 
Final phase (<25% HP): 4 tornadoes + floor. Attack only in gaps.

7. FAQ 
Q: How many deaths before first kill? 
A: 20-50 deaths. Nearly everyone dies 20+ times. After 50 KC you'll rarely die. This is normal. 

Q: Regular or Corrupted? 
A: Regular first (5-10 KC). 10-min timer, softer boss. Switch to CG when consistent. 

Q: Bow + Staff or Halberd? 
A: Bow + Staff for learners. Range distance during tornadoes is safer.

👹 Boss Guide — Updated June 2026 Dagannoth Kings are the original OSRS boss trio — Prime (Magic), Supreme (Ranged), and Rex (Melee).
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text: Dagannoth Kings are the original OSRS boss trio — Prime (Magic), Supreme (Ranged), and Rex (Melee). They drop the Berserker Ring, one of the best value upgrades in the game, and the iconic Dragon Axe. This guide covers solo tribrid rotation, Rex-only safespot farming for mid-level accounts, duo strategies, full gear setups, and how to consistently make 1M+ GP/hr.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:

🦀 Dagannoth Kings — Quick Reference 
Location: Waterbirth Island Dungeon (deepest level) Quest: Fremennik Trials (for pet rock access) 
Best Drop: Berserker Ring (~2M) Ring Rate: ~1/128 per King 
Solo GP/hr: 800K-1.5M Rex-Only GP/hr: 500K-1M (mid-level safe method)

1. The Three Kings — Know Your Enemy

🏹 Supreme 303 Ranged (Protect from Missiles) Melee 30 Archers Ring (~2M)

2. Getting There — The Dungeon Run 
Start at the Waterbirth Island dock (boat from Rellekka). Navigate through the dungeon: bring a Pet Rock and Rune Thrownaxe to pass the gate mechanism (stand on one pressure pad, drop pet rock on the other). The DK lair is on the deepest level. 85 Agility shortcut skips most of the dungeon.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
k on the other). The DK lair is on the deepest level. 85 Agility shortcut skips most of the dungeon. Wear a Fremennik shield or helm to avoid aggro from Dagannoth spawns in the tunnels. Pro tip: Pray Protect from Magic when descending the ladder into the DK room — Prime is usually the first King to aggro. 

3. Rex-Only Safespot — Mid-Level Ring Farm 
This is the safest and most accessible method for farming Dagannoth Kings.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
ot — Mid-Level Ring Farm 
This is the safest and most accessible method for farming Dagannoth Kings. Rex can be permanently safespotted along the southeastern wall of the DK lair. Stand 3 tiles from the southernmost point, pressed against the eastern wall — Rex will never reach you. Cast Magic (Iban's Blast for budget, Trident of the Seas/Swamp for efficiency) from complete safety. 
Entry setup: Pray Protect from Magic before descending the ladder.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
fficiency) from complete safety. 
Entry setup: Pray Protect from Magic before descending the ladder. Immediately run to the southeastern safespot along the east wall. Stay against the wall — Prime and Supreme patrol the northern and western sides and won't aggro if you stay south. Wait for Rex to spawn — he spawns from the southern pool. Kill him → wait ~90 seconds for respawn → repeat. Kill times: ~30-45 seconds with Iban's, ~20-30 seconds with Trident. ~30-40 kills/hr = 500K-1M GP/hr.

4. Solo Tribrid — Full Rotation (Max Profit) 
Soloing all three Kings requires precise timing and gear switches. The correct kill order is: Supreme → Prime → Rex → repeat.

Timing is everything. At max combat, Supreme takes ~30 seconds, Prime ~25 seconds, Rex ~20 seconds. The rotation takes ~90 seconds — perfectly aligning with the 90-second respawn timer. If you're too slow, two Kings will be active simultaneously = heavy damage.

6. Advanced Techniques & Common Mistakes 
Gear switch order matters. Always swap to the NEXT style before finishing the current kill. Example: As Supreme dies, already switch to Ranged gear so you can attack Prime immediately. 
Blood Barrage for sustain. Bring Blood Barrage runes. Cast on Rex (from safespot) for free healing — saves inventory slots on long trips.

Spinolyps priority. The small Spinolyps drain your prayer. Kill the ones nearest your safespot with Ranged before starting each rotation.

Q: Solo tribrid or duo DKs? 
A: Solo tribrid for max GP/hr per person. Duo for easier trips. In a duo, one player handles Supreme + Prime while the other handles Rex.

text:
Q: Can I solo Dagannoth Kings?
A: Yes — solo DKs is very popular and profitable at ~1M+ GP/hr. The key is the kill rotation: Supreme (Ranged) → Prime (Magic) → Rex (Melee from safespot) → repeat. With max combat, each rotation takes ~90 seconds. If two Kings attack you simultaneously, you'll take heavy damage — maintaining the correct kill order is essential.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
Q: What is the best ring from Dagannoth Kings?
A: Berserker Ring (~2M) is the most valuable — +8 Strength bonus when imbued at Nightmare Zone, making it one of the best melee upgrades in the game. Archers Ring (~2M) for Ranged. Seers Ring (~500K) for Magic. All three should be imbued for double stats. The Dragon Axe (~50K) is also a common DK drop.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
Q: How do I safespot Dagannoth Rex?
A: Rex can be safespotted along the southeastern wall of the DK lair. Stand on the marked safespot tile (3 tiles from the southernmost point, against the eastern wall) and Rex cannot reach you. Cast Magic (Trident/Iban's Blast) for free, safe kills. This is how low-level accounts and Ironmen farm the Berserker Ring. While safespotting, stay against the wall to avoid Prime and Supreme's patrol paths.
title: OSRS Dagannoth Kings Guide 2026 — DKs Solo, Duo & Tribrid Strategy, Ring Farming

text:
Q: How do I get to Dagannoth Kings?
A: Fastest route: Use a Pet Rock + Rune Thrownaxe to pass through the Waterbirth Island dungeon gates (requires Fremennik Trials). 85 Agility shortcut saves significant time. Fairy Ring code D•J•P is a slower alternative. Fremennik Hard Diary boots provide a direct teleport to Rellekka. Bring a Fremennik shield or helm to avoid being attacked by Dagannoth spawns in the tunnels.

Not bringing enough prayer potions. This is the #1 cause of death. Bring at least 12-16 (4) dose potions for the full quest. You can always sell unused ones back.

You don't lose items permanently. The exception: Kamil's ice path has aggressive wolves — if you die on the path (not at the boss), you might struggle to reach your gravestone. Use protect item prayer and bring minimal risk.

If you're a 1-Defence pure, you cannot complete this quest without gaining Defence levels.

text:
Q: What is the minimum combat level for Desert Treasure?
A: Level 60 combat is the realistic minimum, though the quest has been completed at level 40 by skilled players. At 60 combat with 50 Magic, 43 Prayer, and access to Iban's Blast or fire spells, all 4 bosses are manageable. Below 60 combat, you'll need significant bossing experience, tick-perfect prayer flicking, and a willingness to die multiple times. 75+ combat makes the quest comfortable.
title: OSRS Desert Treasure Quest Guide Low Level 2026 — Bosses, Requirements & Ancient Magicks

text:
Q: Which boss is the hardest in Desert Treasure?
A: Damis (Shadow Hound) is the hardest for low-level players. He has two forms, drains Prayer rapidly, and hits accurately through melee. The fight can deplete 8+ prayer potions on a low-level account. Kamil (Ice Troll King) is second hardest — his ice attacks freeze you and drain stats. Dessous (Vampire) and Fareed (Fire Warrior) are easier if you use their elemental weaknesses (air and water magic respectively).
title: OSRS Desert Treasure Quest Guide Low Level 2026 — Bosses, Requirements & Ancient Magicks

text:
Q: Can I safe-spot the Desert Treasure bosses?
A: Yes, three of the four bosses have safe-spots. Dessous can be safe-spotted behind the gravestones in his arena — stand on the opposite side and use magic/range. Damis can be trapped behind the rock formations in the Shadow Dungeon (though his second form is faster and may escape). Fareed can be safe-spotted behind the fire pit in the Smoke Dungeon. Kamil has no reliable safe-spot — you must fight him directly while managing freeze and stat drain.
title: OSRS Desert Treasure Quest Guide Low Level 2026 — Bosses, Requirements & Ancient Magicks

text:
Q: What is the best spellbook to use for Desert Treasure bosses?
A: Use the standard spellbook with elemental weaknesses: Air spells for Dessous (Wind Blast/Wave), Fire spells for Kamil (Fire Blast recommended), Water spells for Fareed (Water Blast/Wave). For Damis, use your highest-hitting spell regardless of element (Iban's Blast is excellent if you have Underground Pass completed). Bring entangle/snare runes for Dessous and Damis — freezing them and attacking from distance is the safest strategy.
title: OSRS Desert Treasure Quest Guide Low Level 2026 — Bosses, Requirements & Ancient Magicks

Don't worry — this comprehensive guide will take you from a level 3 noob to a combat level 30+ warrior entirely in F2P, setting you up perfectly for when you decide to become a member. We'll cover every aspect of F2P combat training: melee progression, ranged training, prayer grinding, quest rewards that boost your stats for free, and a day-by-day training schedule that actually works.

Section 1: Understanding F2P Combat Limits 
Before we dive into the training methods, it's crucial to understand exactly what free-to-play players can and cannot do. Many new players assume F2P is hopelessly limited — it's not. You can actually achieve quite respectable levels without ever paying a cent.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
ly limited — it's not. You can actually achieve quite respectable levels without ever paying a cent. 
What's Possible in F2P 
Free-to-play mode gives you access to a surprising amount of content. You can train Attack up to level 60 , Strength up to 60 , Defence up to 60 , Ranged up to 50+ , Magic up to 59 (with high alchemy), and Prayer up to 43 (Protect from Melee).
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
0 , Ranged up to 50+ , Magic up to 59 (with high alchemy), and Prayer up to 43 (Protect from Melee). The absolute maximum combat level for a pure F2P account is 126, though reaching that without membership would take an absurd amount of time. 
For the purposes of this guide, our goal is more modest and practical: get you to combat level 30-40+ entirely in F2P.

Combat Level Formula (Important!) 
Your combat level is calculated using this formula:

Combat Level Formula (Important!) 
Your combat level is calculated using this formula: 
Combat Level = 0.25 × (Attack + Strength) + 0.25 × Defence + 0.25 × Constitution + 0.125 × Prayer + 0.125 × (max of Ranged/Magic) 
This means your base combat stats (Attack, Strength, Defence, Hitpoints) contribute the most to your combat level. Prayer and your highest non-melee attack style (Ranged or Magic) contribute half as much.

Pro tip: Don't neglect Defence entirely. While some players make "pures" (keeping Defence at 1), as a F2P player you'll want at least 20-30 Defence to wear decent armor. It makes training much less painful because you'll take less damage and use less food.

Section 2: F2P Gear Progression (Free Player Gear Tiers) 
One of the most satisfying parts of OSRS is watching your character transform from a bronze-geared noob to a rune-wearing warrior. In F2P, gear progression is straightforward but important — using the best gear you can afford and wield makes a massive difference to your XP rates and survivability.

Melee Gear Progression (Attack/Strength/Defence) 
The golden rule of F2P melee training: always use a scimitar . Scimitars have the fastest attack speed in the game for slash weapons (same speed as a whip, which is the best member weapon), making them far superior to swords, longswords, or 2-handed swords for training.

Section 3: Levels 1–10 Attack/Strength (Chickens & Cows) 
Every OSRS journey starts the same way: in Lumbridge, surrounded by chickens. Don't skip this phase! The first 10 levels go by incredibly fast, and you can make some decent starting cash by collecting drops.

Best Monster: Chickens (Lumbridge & Falador) 
Chicken Stats: Hitpoints: 3 | Defence: Very low | Max Hit: 1 
Chickens are the classic starting monster for a reason. They have only 3 HP, which means they die in 1-2 hits even with bronze weapons. More importantly, they drop feathers — and feathers are stackable items that sell for about 5-6 GP each at the Grand Exchange.

For the Level 40 Attack Goal: You NEED the Rune Scimitar 
The Rune Scimitar is the single biggest DPS upgrade available to F2P players. It requires 40 Attack to wield and has a +43 slash bonus (compared to +35 for adamant scimitar). That's not just a small upgrade — it's a massive increase in DPS. 
Cost: Buy from the Grand Exchange for about 15,000-20,000 GP.

Section 7: F2P Defence Training 
Defence is often neglected by new players, but it's actually one of the most important stats in F2P. Higher Defence means you can wear better armor (Rune armor requires 40 Defence!), take less damage, and train for longer without needing food.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
rmor (Rune armor requires 40 Defence!), take less damage, and train for longer without needing food. 
Why Raise Defence? 
Wear better armor: Rune armor (the best F2P armor) requires 40 Defence. Without Defence training, you're stuck with adamant or lower. 
Take less damage: Every Defence level reduces the max hit monsters can land on you. At 40 Defence, most low-level monsters will hit 0-2 instead of 4-6. 
Less food needed: If you're taking less damage, you don't need to bring as much food.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
stead of 4-6. 
Less food needed: If you're taking less damage, you don't need to bring as much food. That means longer training sessions. 
Higher combat level: Defence contributes to your combat level, making you eligible for more content. 

Defence Training Method 
Defence training follows the same monster progression as Attack/Strength training, but with one key difference: set your combat style to "Defensive" or "Controlled".
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
trength training, but with one key difference: set your combat style to "Defensive" or "Controlled". 
When you have a scimitar or any melee weapon equipped, you'll see three attack style options: "Accurate" (trains Attack), "Aggressive" (trains Strength), and "Defensive" (trains Defence). There's also "Controlled" (trains Attack, Strength, and Defence evenly — available on some weapons like the scimitar). 
Recommended approach: Don't train Defence in isolation.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
ailable on some weapons like the scimitar). 
Recommended approach: Don't train Defence in isolation. Instead, use the "Controlled" attack style (available on scimitars) to train all three melee stats simultaneously. This is much more efficient than grinding Defence separately. 
Goal for F2P: Reach 30-40 Defence so you can wear Mithril, Adamant, and ultimately Rune armor sets. Rune armor (requires 40 Defence) gives a massive armour bonus and makes you much tankier.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
rmor sets. Rune armor (requires 40 Defence) gives a massive armour bonus and makes you much tankier. 
Training tip: Alternate between Attack/Strength training and Defence training rather than "pure" levelling. Balanced accounts progress through content much smoother, especially in F2P where you don't have access to prayer potions or high-level food. 

Section 8: F2P Ranged Training (Levels 1-40) 
Ranged is incredibly useful in F2P because it allows you to safespot monsters.

Ranged is incredibly useful in F2P because it allows you to safespot monsters. A safespot is a location where you can attack a monster but they can't reach you, meaning you take zero damage. This makes Ranged excellent for low-level players who want to train safely. 
Ranged Training Progression 
Levels 1-10: Chickens & Cows 
Same as melee, but with a shortbow and bronze/iron arrows. Stand at a distance and click the monster.

Levels 10-20: Minotaurs & Goblins (safespotted) 
In the Stronghold of Security, there are safespots where you can stand behind a barrier and range monsters while they can't reach you. This is safe and AFK-able.

There's a safespot in Edgeville Dungeon where Hill Giants can't reach you. Look for a rock formation or a corner where you can stand and attack with arrows. This is excellent ranged training because Hill Giants have 28 HP (more HP = more Ranged XP per kill) and they drop Big Bones.

Section 9: F2P Prayer Training (Levels 1-43) 
Prayer is one of the most underrated skills in F2P. At level 43 Prayer, you unlock Protect from Melee — a prayer that makes you immune to melee damage from monsters. This is absolutely game-changing, especially when you start facing tougher monsters or doing boss fights.

Section 12: Tips & FAQ 
Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Should I train Attack or Strength first? 
A: Train Attack first (to 40) for accuracy. Higher Attack means you hit more often. Once you have 40 Attack and a Rune scimitar, train Strength for damage (higher max hits).
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
ore often. Once you have 40 Attack and a Rune scimitar, train Strength for damage (higher max hits). Keep Defence slightly lower initially if you want to be a "semi-pure", but for general F2P play, balanced stats are fine. 
Q: Is F2P combat training boring? 
A: Early levels, yes, a bit. Killing chickens isn't exactly thrilling. But it goes FAST. The first 20 combat levels take only a few hours. Once you're killing Hill Giants and Hobgoblins, it gets more interesting (and more profitable).

urs. Once you're killing Hill Giants and Hobgoblins, it gets more interesting (and more profitable). 
Q: How do I afford Rune equipment? 
A: Cowhides! Seriously. Kill cows near Lumbridge, collect 100-200 cowhides, sell them at GE. That's 10,000-20,000 GP right there — enough for a full Rune set. Alternatively, collect Limpwurt Roots from Hobgoblins (1,500-2,000 GP each) or sell the nature runes and herbs that monsters drop. 
Q: Should I get membership as soon as possible? 
A: YES.

Q: Should I get membership as soon as possible? 
A: YES. Membership opens up 10x more content: better training methods, more quests, bosses, mini-games, the entire game basically. Use this guide to get to combat level 30+ quickly, THEN buy a bond (with GP) or pay for membership. Don't rush to membership at combat level 5 — you'll struggle to do member content.

Q: What about Magic in F2P? 
A: Train Magic alongside combat! Use Curse (level 1 Magic) on the monsters you're meleeing. It lowers their defence and gives you Magic XP at the same time. Once you have 13 Magic, you can use the "Confuse" spell for even better defence reduction. At 19 Magic, "Weaken" reduces their strength.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
the "Confuse" spell for even better defence reduction. At 19 Magic, "Weaken" reduces their strength. Magic in F2P is mostly for supporting melee, not for primary combat (though you CAN train Magic as a combat style — it's just less efficient than melee for training). 
Q: How long does it take to get 99 Attack/Strength in F2P? 
A: Don't. Just don't. 99 Attack in F2P would take hundreds of hours and massive amounts of GP for food and gear repairs. Get membership, then train to 99.

A: Cowhides (as mentioned), collecting Limpwurt Roots from Hobgoblins, mining Iron ore (level 15 Mining) and selling it, or doing the "Fly Fishing" money-making method (catch trout/salmon at the Shilo Village fishing spot, cook them, sell the cooked fish).

king method (catch trout/salmon at the Shilo Village fishing spot, cook them, sell the cooked fish). With 50,000-100,000 GP, you can buy all the gear you need for F2P combat. 

Final Tips for F2P Combat Training Success 
Join a Clan: OSRS clans are friendly and often help new players with advice, gear, or GP. Look for F2P-friendly clans in the clan chat "Join Clan" interface. 
Use the Wiki: The OSRS Wiki is your best friend. Every monster, item, quest, and skill has a detailed page.
title: OSRS F2P Combat Training Guide 2026

text:
Wiki: The OSRS Wiki is your best friend. Every monster, item, quest, and skill has a detailed page. 
Don't be afraid to die: In F2P, if you die in a safe area (like the Stronghold or most dungeons), you keep your items. Die = respawn at Lumbridge with full inventory. It's not the end of the world. 
Take breaks: Combat training can be grindy. Do a quest, train a different skill (Cooking, Mining, Fishing), then come back to combat. Variety keeps the game fun.

Have fun: OSRS is a game, not a job. If you're not enjoying F2P combat training, switch to something else. The beauty of OSRS is there's always something else to do.

1. The F2P Ironman Challenge 
As an F2P Ironman, you cannot buy or sell items on the Grand Exchange. Every gold piece enters your pouch through one of two channels: selling items to NPC shops (at heavily discounted rates) or using High Alchemy (at fixed alch values).

lling items to NPC shops (at heavily discounted rates) or using High Alchemy (at fixed alch values). This fundamentally changes how you approach money making — you must prioritize items with high alch values and process raw materials yourself. 
The key difference between a regular F2P player and an F2P Ironman is value-added processing . A regular player can sell raw cowhides on the GE for profit.

text:
F2P Ironman is value-added processing . A regular player can sell raw cowhides on the GE for profit. An Ironman must tan the hides, craft them into items, and then alch those items — each processing step multiplies the final GP value. Understanding this chain is the secret to Ironman wealth.

Crafting Training Overview 
Crafting is trained primarily by creating items at workbenches, furnaces, spinning wheels, and looms. Most methods are "buyable" — meaning you purchase raw materials from the Grand Exchange or gather them yourself (for ironmen). The skill unlocks useful items like dragonhide armor for ranged combat, amulets for combat stats, and teleport tablets for convenience.

Levels 57–71: Blue & Red Dragonhide 
This range is where Crafting becomes expensive but also very efficient. Blue dragonhide vambraces and bodies are your best bet for pure XP throughput. 
Optimal Path: Blue Vambraces (57–68) → Red Bodies (68–71+) 
Levels 57–68: Craft blue dragonhide vambraces. They give 95 XP each, require only 1 hide, and are cheaper than blue bodies. Expect ~85,000–90,000 XP/hr.

Start every account with Waterfall Quest — it requires zero combat, takes 30 minutes, and instantly boosts you from level 1 to 30 Attack and Strength. Follow up with Fight Arena, Tree Gnome Village, and The Grand Tree for easy Attack XP.

and Strength. Follow up with Fight Arena, Tree Gnome Village, and The Grand Tree for easy Attack XP. You'll hit 50+ Attack before killing a single monster. 
Sand Crabs (Levels 30–60) 
Head to Hosidius Beach (south of Kourend Castle) with your best weapon. Sand crabs have 60 HP and are aggressive for 10 minutes. Find a 3-crab spot (southern beach has multiple), click to attack, and AFK for 10 minutes. After 10 minutes, run 15+ tiles away and run back to reset aggression.

tack, and AFK for 10 minutes. After 10 minutes, run 15+ tiles away and run back to reset aggression. At 40K-60K XP/hr with a rune scimitar, you'll reach 60 in about 4-6 hours of total AFKing. 

3. Levels 60–85: Mid Game — NMZ & Ammonite Crabs 
Ammonite Crabs (60–70) 
After Bone Voyage, head to the Ammonite Crab spots on Fossil Island (north-central beach). Ammonite crabs are superior to sand crabs: 100 HP, same aggression mechanics, 60K-80K XP/hr with a dragon scimitar.

Nightmare Zone Setup (Unlock at 60+) 
🛡️ NMZ Boss Selection for Max AFK: Choose only these 5 bosses for minimal attention: Count Draynor, King Roald, Tree Spirit, Kendal, and Khazard Warlord.

Avoid bosses that require special mechanics: Elvarg (dragonfire), Moss Giant (prayer drain), Slagilith (pickaxe required), Dad (knockback), Arrg (ranged). These 5 have zero special mechanics and never hit above 3 damage at 1 HP. 
NMZ Normal Mode — Obsidian AFK (80K–100K XP/hr) 
Requirements: 60 Defence, ~3M GP for Obsidian armor set.

ormal Mode — Obsidian AFK (80K–100K XP/hr) 
Requirements: 60 Defence, ~3M GP for Obsidian armor set. Wear full Obsidian armor + Berserker Necklace + Obsidian Sword (Toktz-xil-ak). The set effect provides +10% melee accuracy and +10% melee strength. Enter custom rumble (easy bosses), drink Absorption potions to 1,000 HP, rock cake to 1 HP, and AFK for 15-20 minutes. Re-pot absorptions and rock cake when you check back. This is the best balance of AFK-ness and XP in OSRS.

7. Common Melee Training Mistakes 
Training Defence first. Higher Defence doesn't speed up training. Higher Strength does (bigger hits = more XP per attack). Always train Strength first, then Attack, then Defence. 
Using the wrong weapon for Strength training. The Abyssal Whip has a "controlled" option but no "strength-only" option.
title: OSRS Fastest 99 Attack, Strength & Defence Guide 2026 — NMZ, Crabs & Best XP Methods

text:
pon for Strength training. The Abyssal Whip has a "controlled" option but no "strength-only" option. For pure Strength training, use a Saradomin Sword, Abyssal Dagger, or Dragon Scimitar (budget). The Whip is for Attack and Defence training only. 
Not using Super Combat Potions. Super Combat Potions boost all melee stats by ~15% and cost 5K-8K per dose. That's 1-2K GP per kill of extra DPS. Over a 100-hour grind to 99, the boost from Super Combats saves 10+ hours. Don't cheap out on potions.
title: OSRS Fastest 99 Attack, Strength & Defence Guide 2026 — NMZ, Crabs & Best XP Methods

text:
er a 100-hour grind to 99, the boost from Super Combats saves 10+ hours. Don't cheap out on potions. 
Ignoring Piety/Rigour. Piety provides +25% Defence, +23% Strength, and +20% Attack. At 70 Prayer, you should use Piety for ALL melee training. The prayer potion cost (50K-80K/hr) is worth the 23% DPS increase. Without Piety, you're training at 77% efficiency. 
Training at over-crowded crab spots. Sand crab and ammonite crab spots are competitive.

Training at over-crowded crab spots. Sand crab and ammonite crab spots are competitive. If you're sharing a 3-crab spot with 2 other players, your XP drops by 60%+. World hop until you find an empty spot. Worth the 2 minutes of hopping.

Q: Is Slayer the best way to train melee? 
A: For overall account progression, yes. For pure melee XP speed, no. Slayer averages 40K-60K melee XP/hr depending on tasks, which is 50%+ slower than NMZ or Nagua.

text:
Q: How long does 1-99 in Attack, Strength, and Defence take in OSRS?
A: Each skill from 1-99 requires approximately 13,034,431 XP. Using NMZ Dharok method (100K-130K XP/hr): roughly 100 hours per skill, or 300 hours for all three. With Sulphur Nagua (130K-150K XP/hr active): about 87 hours per skill. AFK methods (Sand/Ammonite crabs, normal NMZ) at 60K-80K XP/hr: 165-215 hours per skill. Total time to max all three melee skills: 250-500 hours depending on method and AFK preference.
title: OSRS Fastest 99 Attack, Strength & Defence Guide 2026 — NMZ, Crabs & Best XP Methods

text:
Q: What is the best AFK method for melee training?
A: Nightmare Zone (NMZ) normal mode with Absorption potions is the best pure AFK melee training. Customize a rumble with 5 weak bosses (Count Draynor, King Roald, Tree Spirit, Kendal, Khazard Warlord), drink Absorption potions to stack 1,000 HP of damage absorption, and use a rock cake to stay at 1 HP (bosses can only hit 1 damage at a time). Click once every 15-20 minutes to re-pot and re-rock-cake. This yields 80K-100K XP/hr with full Guthan's or Obsidian gear.
title: OSRS Fastest 99 Attack, Strength & Defence Guide 2026 — NMZ, Crabs & Best XP Methods

text:
Q: Should I train Attack, Strength, or Defence first to 99?
A: Train Strength first. Higher Strength = higher max hits = faster XP for everything else. Then train Attack (more accuracy = less splashing = slightly faster XP). Train Defence last — it provides the least DPS benefit and you'll have higher Strength/Attack by then, making Defence training faster. Optimal order: Strength → Attack → Defence. If you plan to do PvM before maxing, get 85+ in all three first, then push Strength to 99.
title: OSRS Fastest 99 Attack, Strength & Defence Guide 2026 — NMZ, Crabs & Best XP Methods

text:
Q: What gear should I use for the fastest melee XP?
A: Best DPS gear by budget: Budget (5M): Obsidian armor set + Berserker Necklace + Obsidian Sword (Toktz-xil-ak). Mid (30M): Full Bandos, Amulet of Torture, Abyssal Whip (Attack) / Abyssal Dagger (Strength), Dragon Defender, Berserker Ring (i), Fire Cape. Max (500M+): Torva set, Soulreaper Axe (Strength) / Ghrazi Rapier (Attack/Defence), Avernic Defender, Ultor Ring, Infernal Cape. Always prioritize Strength bonus over Defence — you want to maximize DPS, not survivability.

SRS — same Strength bonus as a 25M Bandos Chestplate, earned through 4-6 hours of Barbarian Assault. This guide covers every role, call system, Queen strategy, and exactly how to minimize your time in the minigame. 
🛡️ Fighter Torso — Quick Reference

1. The 4 Roles — What You Must Know

⚔️ Attacker ⭐⭐ Easy Use the CORRECT attack style shown by the horn. Call IMMEDIATELY after each kill so the Collector knows what eggs to pick. 
🥚 Collector ⭐⭐ Easy Pick up eggs from the ground. Load them into the egg launcher. Wait for the Attacker's call before picking up the matching color. Easiest role — good for beginners.

2. The Call System — MOST Important Mechanic 
Your horn displays the current "call" — what action your teammates need. Every 30 seconds, the call changes. You MUST update your call after every action. If the Attacker doesn't call, the Collector can't pick the right eggs. If the Healer doesn't call, the Defender can't kill runners. The call system is the #1 reason pub teams fail.
title: OSRS Fighter Torso & Barbarian Assault Guide 2026 — Fast Queen Runs, Role Strategies

text:
aler doesn't call, the Defender can't kill runners. The call system is the #1 reason pub teams fail. Right-click horn → select the correct option immediately after every kill/heal/egg pickup. 

3. Wave-by-Wave Breakdown 
Each role completes 10 waves (1-10). Wave 10 is the Queen. After completing all 10 waves in one role, you earn points. Swap roles and repeat 3 more times for full Torso. 
Waves Focus Time 
1-4 Easy warmup waves. Practice calls. Learn your role rhythm.

4. Queen Strategy — Wave 10 
Phase 1: Attackers kill the Omega eggs while Collector loads the egg launcher with the called egg type. 
Phase 2: Everyone fires eggs at the Queen using the launcher. Each player grabs eggs from the launcher and fires. 
Queen attacks: The Queen fires a damaging projectile. Dodge by moving when you hear the audio cue. Healer keeps everyone alive.

4. Wave-by-Wave Strategy (Waves 1–62) 
Waves Enemies Strategy 
1–30 Tz-Kih, Tz-Kek, Tok-Xil Easy waves. Use Italy Rock safespot. Kill bats (Tz-Kih) FIRST — they drain your prayer.

5. Jad Strategy — The Moment of Truth 
Stay calm. You have ONE job: switch prayers correctly.

5. Jad Strategy — The Moment of Truth 
Stay calm. You have ONE job: switch prayers correctly. 
Attack Type Visual Cue Sound Cue Prayer 
🦶 Stomp (Melee) Jad stomps his feet. Close range only — stay away! Thud Protect from Melee 
🏹 Ranged Jad lifts front legs HIGH and slams down hard Quick thud Protect from Missiles

leans BACK, fireball appears above his head Distinct charging sound (key cue!) Protect from Magic 
🔊 USE SOUND — IT'S YOUR #1 CUE: The Magic attack makes a distinct charging sound BEFORE the fireball appears. When you hear it, switch to Protect from Magic IMMEDIATELY. If you DON'T hear the sound, it's Ranged — switch to Protect from Missiles. Many players play with game sounds ON specifically for Jad. 
Jad Fight Step-by-Step 
Wave 62 END: Kill the final Orange Mage (360) last.

text:
N specifically for Jad. 
Jad Fight Step-by-Step 
Wave 62 END: Kill the final Orange Mage (360) last. As it dies, switch to Protect from Missiles (Jad's default starting attack is often Ranged). 
Jad spawns: Stay near Italy Rock. Keep your distance — if you're too close, he'll Stomp (Melee). 
Attacking: ONE action per Jad attack. Click Jad → watch for his animation → switch prayer → ONE more action. Never try to do two things between attacks.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
tch for his animation → switch prayer → ONE more action. Never try to do two things between attacks. 
Healers appear (50% HP): When Jad hits 50%, four Yt-HurKot (Healers) spawn. Tag each one by hitting it ONCE (to get its attention). Do NOT kill them unless you're confident. 
Healer Strategy — LINE THEM UP: After tagging all 4, run behind Italy Rock so the Healers line up single-file. Only ONE Healer can attack you at a time — you can tank one easily.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
the Healers line up single-file. Only ONE Healer can attack you at a time — you can tank one easily. 
Finish Jad: Once Healers are tagged and lined up, go back to attacking Jad. ONE action per prayer switch. Keep the rhythm until he dies. 
Fire Cape acquired! Congratulations — you've earned one of the most iconic items in OSRS. 

6. Safespots & Italy Rock 
The Italy Rock (named because it looks like Italy on the minimap) is your home for 62 waves.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
y Rock 
The Italy Rock (named because it looks like Italy on the minimap) is your home for 62 waves. Here's how to use it: 
East side (boot): Safespot — melee enemies get stuck here. Stand on the south side of this boot-shaped section. 
North side: Stand behind the northern tip to block larger enemies (Yt-MejKot 180s). 
Corner trap: The southeast corner traps enemies perfectly. Lure them to one side, run to the other.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
Corner trap: The southeast corner traps enemies perfectly. Lure them to one side, run to the other. 
Watch a quick YouTube guide for the exact Italy Rock tile positions — seeing it once is worth a thousand words. 

7. Common Mistakes That Get You Killed 
Panic-switching prayers. After each Jad attack, keep your current prayer ON until you know what the next attack is. 
Looking at your inventory too long. Every second your eyes are off Jad is a second you can miss the prayer cue.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
your inventory too long. Every second your eyes are off Jad is a second you can miss the prayer cue. 
Trying to kill Healers. Just tag them. Killing all 4 while prayer-switching Jad is how most first-timers die. 
Running into melee range. Stay at range. Jad's stomp is a guaranteed 50+ damage if you're too close. 
Not using F-keys. Bind your inventory and prayer tab to F1/F2. You'll need to switch tabs quickly during Healer phase. 
Underestimating Wave 61.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
ayer tab to F1/F2. You'll need to switch tabs quickly during Healer phase. 
Underestimating Wave 61. The double-mage wave before Jad kills more players than Jad himself. Use Italy Rock and take your time. 

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I log out during the Fight Caves? 
A: Yes! Click the logout button between waves. You'll be prompted to log out after the current wave ends. When you log back in, you resume on the next wave.
title: OSRS Fire Cape Guide 2026 — Jad Strategy, Fight Caves Walkthrough & Budget Setup

text:
prompted to log out after the current wave ends. When you log back in, you resume on the next wave. Use this to take breaks — the Fight Caves are mentally draining. 

Q: What if I die — do I lose all my items? 
A: No! The Fight Caves are a safe death. You keep ALL your items. You only lose the supplies you used. Don't be afraid to fail — it costs you nothing but time and a few thousand GP in potions. 

Q: Is a Blowpipe required or can I use a Crossbow? 
A: Blowpipe is strongly recommended.

potions.

Q: Can I practice Jad without doing all 62 waves? 
A: Yes — use the Speedrun World method.

Practice there until you're comfortable with prayer switching.

OSRS First Week Progression Guide — Day-by-Day Roadmap (2026) 
Beginner Roadmap Progression Updated 2026 
Welcome to Gielinor, adventurer! Your first week in Old School RuneScape shapes everything that follows. This isn't just a list of things to do — it's your personal coach for the next seven days.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
follows. This isn't just a list of things to do — it's your personal coach for the next seven days. Follow this day-by-day roadmap and you'll transform from a level 3 noob standing in Lumbridge to a combat-ready player with solid stats, finished quests, and a growing bank account. Let's get to work. 

Before You Start — Mindset & Preparation 
OSRS is a marathon, not a sprint. Your first week sets habits that will serve you for years.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
ration 
OSRS is a marathon, not a sprint. Your first week sets habits that will serve you for years. Before you even create your account, internalize these three golden rules that separate successful players from those who quit after two days. 
🏆 The Three Golden Rules 
Golden Rule #1: Always have a goal when you log in. Aimless play is the #1 cause of burnout. This guide provides those goals — stick to them and you'll see progress every single session.

text:
rnout. This guide provides those goals — stick to them and you'll see progress every single session. 
Golden Rule #2: Quests give more XP per hour than any skill training. A single quest can give you more XP than three hours of grinding. Always prioritize quest rewards over mindless skilling. 
Golden Rule #3: Membership multiplies everything. F2P is a tutorial. Member content gives you 10x the training methods, money makers, and fun.

ything. F2P is a tutorial. Member content gives you 10x the training methods, money makers, and fun. Aim to earn enough for a bond (7 days = ~1.3M GP, 14 days = ~2.3M GP) by the end of week 1 or 2. 

⚙️ Account Setup Checklist 
Create your account (or use an existing fresh account if you have one). 
Choose your game mode: Normal (recommended for first account), Ironman (self-sufficient challenge), Hardcore Ironman (one life only — not recommended for first-timers).

an (self-sufficient challenge), Hardcore Ironman (one life only — not recommended for first-timers). 
Set your interface: Modern mode is recommended for beginners (cleaner UI, easier to read). Legacy mode is available if you prefer the old-school feel. 
Pick your username wisely: You'll be stuck with it forever. Make it something you're proud to have above your head in crowded worlds. 

Once your account is created, you'll spawn on Tutorial Island .

above your head in crowded worlds. 

Once your account is created, you'll spawn on Tutorial Island . Don't skip it! Even if you've played before, the tutorial teaches the absolute basics and gives you a small head start. 

Day 0 — Tutorial Island & Setup (30-60 min) 
📍 Tutorial Island Walkthrough 
Tutorial Island is your introduction to Gielinor. Completing it is mandatory for new accounts, and it teaches you the fundamentals: movement, combat, skilling, questing, and the bank.

w accounts, and it teaches you the fundamentals: movement, combat, skilling, questing, and the bank. Don't rush — read the dialogue. The information is genuinely useful. 
Combat Instructor: Learn to attack, use magic, and range. You'll finish with level 3 in Attack, Strength, Defence, Ranged, Magic, and Hitpoints. 
Mining & Smithing Instructor: Mine tin/copper, smelt bronze bars, smith a bronze dagger. Basic crafting loop. 
Cooking Instructor: Cook shrimp without burning them.

, smith a bronze dagger. Basic crafting loop. 
Cooking Instructor: Cook shrimp without burning them. Cooking is essential for sustaining yourself in combat. 
Fishing Instructor: Catch shrimp with a small net. Fishing + Cooking = free food forever. 
Prayer Instructor: Bury bones to gain Prayer XP. Prayer levels unlock protection prayers that are crucial for survival. 
Quest Instructor: Complete a mini-quest to learn the quest interface. Quests are the fastest XP in the game.

Account Creation: Choose your character appearance (cosmetic only — doesn't affect gameplay at all). 

⚙️ Settings to Change Immediately 
Turn on "Accept Aid": Allows other players to heal you, teleport you, and help in co-op content. Essential for community play.

Set camera to resizable mode: Maximum view distance. Being able to see more of the world helps with navigation and awareness.

First Actions After Tutorial Island 
You spawn in Lumbridge — the central hub for new players. Before you run off to adventure, do these three things: 
Check your bank: Located in Lumbridge Castle basement (down the trapdoor south of the castle) or upstairs in the kitchen area.

n Lumbridge Castle basement (down the trapdoor south of the castle) or upstairs in the kitchen area. Open it, get familiar with the interface. 
Open your world map: Press the key. This is your best friend. Every location in the game is on this map — learn to read it. 
Familiarize yourself with movement: Click to walk, hold to run (drain run energy), use minimap to orient yourself. Simple but essential.

Day 1 — Quest Blitz & First Combat Levels (2-3 hours) 
Day 1 is all about momentum. You're going to knock out six F2P quests in about an hour, then spend the rest of your time getting your first combat levels. By the end of today, you'll have 6 Quest Points, combat level ~12-14, and your first ~2k GP from drops. 
🌅 Morning Session (1 hour) — Speed-Run F2P Quests 
1. The Blood Pact 5 min 
Start: Talk to Xenia south of Lumbridge Castle (she's right outside, near the river).
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
od Pact 5 min 
Start: Talk to Xenia south of Lumbridge Castle (she's right outside, near the river). 
Walkthrough: She sends you into a small dungeon to kill three minions. Super easy, designed for level 3 players. 
Rewards: +1 Attack, +1 Strength, +1 Defence, 300 XP in a combat stat of your choice, 1 Quest Point. 
Why do it: Instant free combat levels. This is the fastest way to get to level 4+ in your chosen combat style.

2. Imp Catcher 10 min 
Start: Wizard Mizgog on the top floor of the Wizard Tower (south of Draynor Village, south of Lumbridge). 
Walkthrough: He asks you to find a missing bead. Talk to Wizard Traiborn (same tower, different floor) who gives you a location hint. The bead is actually just sitting in a chest in the tower basement — no combat required.

Rewards: +1 Magic level (if your Magic is low), Amulet of Accuracy (minor accuracy boost for melee). 
Why do it: Unlocks the Amulet of Accuracy, which is a decent early-game amulet. Also gives Magic XP. 

3. Restless Ghost 15 min 
Start: Father Aereck in Lumbridge Church (east side of Lumbridge).

3. Restless Ghost 15 min 
Start: Father Aereck in Lumbridge Church (east side of Lumbridge). 
Walkthrough: He sends you to talk to Father Urhney in Lumbridge Swamp (south-west of Lumbridge, enter the small shack). Father Urhney gives you a Ghostspeak Amulet. Go back to the Lumbridge Church basement, talk to the ghost, find the ghost's remains in the Wizards' Tower basement, and bury them. 
Rewards: 1,125 Prayer XP — this jumps your Prayer from level 1 to level 9! Also 1 Quest Point.

Why do it: 1,125 Prayer XP is massive at this stage. Level 9 Prayer gets you access to better protection prayers sooner. 

4. Ernest the Chicken 20 min 
Start: Veronica outside Draynor Manor (north-west of Draynor Village). 
Walkthrough: Ernest has been turned into a chicken. Enter Draynor Manor, go to the basement, find the oil can, poison, and key. Use them on the machine to turn Ernest back.

Rewards: 300 XP each in Attack, Strength, Defence, Prayer, and Woodcutting. That's 1,500 total XP! Also 1 Quest Point. 
Why do it: The distribution of XP across multiple skills makes this amazing for overall account progression. You get Prayer XP without burying bones!

5. Cook's Assistant 5 min 
Start: Cook in Lumbridge Castle kitchen (first floor). 
Walkthrough: He needs milk, egg, and flour. Milk a cow at the Lumbridge cow field (north-east of castle), grab an egg from the Chicken coop (same area), and use a pot on the flour bin in the Lumbridge Castle basement. Bring all three to the Cook. 
Rewards: +1 Cooking level (if your Cooking is level 1), 1 Quest Point.

Why do it: Quick and easy. Every Quest Point counts toward Dragon Slayer prerequisites. 

6. Sheep Shearer 10 min 
Start: Fred the Farmer north of Lumbridge (north of the cow field, follow the path north). 
Walkthrough: Shear 20 sheep with shears (Fred gives you shears). Use a spinning wheel (Lumbridge Castle second floor or Falador) to turn wool into balls of wool. Bring 20 wool balls to Fred.

Why do it: Gives Crafting and Farming XP, which are annoying to train early on. Also another Quest Point toward Dragon Slayer. 

🌇 Afternoon Session (1-2 hours) — Combat Training 
Now that you have some Quest Points and a few combat levels, it's time to actually fight things.

Now that you have some Quest Points and a few combat levels, it's time to actually fight things. Head to the Chicken coop north-east of Lumbridge Castle (just past the cow field). 
🐔 Chicken Training Strategy 
Set your attack style to "Attack" (top option on the combat tab). Train Attack to 10 first — this unlocks better weapons. 
Collect feathers: Chickens drop feathers. Pick up EVERY feather — they sell for ~5-10 GP each on the Grand Exchange. 500 feathers = 2.5-5k GP.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
Pick up EVERY feather — they sell for ~5-10 GP each on the Grand Exchange. 500 feathers = 2.5-5k GP. 
Chickens are level 1: You'll kill them in 1-2 hits. Extremely fast XP at low levels. 
Train Attack to 10: This unlocks the Iron scimitar (buy from Lev's General Store in Al Kharid for ~24 GP). The scimitar is the best F2P attack speed weapon. 

Once Attack hits 10, switch your combat style to "Strength" (bottom option) and head to the Cow field east of Lumbridge .
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
switch your combat style to "Strength" (bottom option) and head to the Cow field east of Lumbridge . Cows are level 2, slightly tougher than chickens, but they drop cowhides — and cowhides are worth ~110-120 GP each at the Grand Exchange. 
🐄 Cowhide Money Maker (Your First Serious Income) 
Kill cows. Loot every cowhide . 
Do NOT drop cowhides. They are your primary income source right now.

ill cows. Loot every cowhide . 
Do NOT drop cowhides. They are your primary income source right now. 
Bank cowhides at the Lumbridge Castle basement deposit box (or run to Al Kharid deposit box if you have the GP for the toll). 
Sell cowhides on the Grand Exchange when you have 50-100. Don't sell for less than 100 GP each. 
100 cowhides = ~11,000 GP.

100 cowhides = ~11,000 GP. That's your first real money! 

✅ End of Day 1 Milestone 
✅ 6 Quest Points (out of 33 needed for Dragon Slayer) 
✅ Combat level ~12-14 
✅ Attack ~10, Strength ~8-10, Defence ~5-7 
✅ ~2,000 GP from feathers and cowhides 
✅ Iron scimitar equipped (or better if you bought one) 
✅ Understanding of basic combat and money making loops 

Day 2 — Stronghold of Security & More Quests (2-3 hours)

Day 2 — Stronghold of Security & More Quests (2-3 hours) 
Day 2 is about two things: getting your first serious GP stack from the Stronghold of Security, and pushing your combat levels higher. The Stronghold of Security (SoS) is a F2P minigame that gives you 17,000 GP , an emote, and outfit pieces. It's mandatory for every new account.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
nigame that gives you 17,000 GP , an emote, and outfit pieces. It's mandatory for every new account. 
🌅 Morning Session (1 hour) — Stronghold of Security 
🏰 Stronghold of Security Guide 
Location: Barbarian Village (west of Varrock, use the spirit trees or walk). Look for a large hole in the ground with a ladder. 
Overview: Four floors, each with a safety question and a reward. You must find the correct door on each floor by reading the riddles.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
a safety question and a reward. You must find the correct door on each floor by reading the riddles. Monsters on each floor scale with your combat level. 
Floor 1 — "Hope" (Pest Control) 
Monsters: Minotaurs (level 12, great for combat training after the quest). 
Goal: Find the door with the word "Pest" (answer to the riddle). 
Reward: 2,000 GP + Safety shoes (cosmetic). 
Combat tip: Minotaurs are level 12 and drop iron arrows. They're actually the best combat training spot for levels 10-25.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
are level 12 and drop iron arrows. They're actually the best combat training spot for levels 10-25. 
Floor 2 — "Solitude" (Isolation) 
Monsters: Flesh Crawlers (level 8-12, very weak). 
Goal: Find the correct levers to Pull (not push). Read the riddle carefully. 
Reward: Genie lamp (use on any skill level 10+ for XP) + Chameleon boots (cosmetic). 
Note: Flesh Crawlers are AFKable. You can train here while watching a video.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
leon boots (cosmetic). 
Note: Flesh Crawlers are AFKable. You can train here while watching a video. 
Floor 3 — "Affection" (Barbarian skill) 
Monsters: Catablepon (level 15-21, moderately tough). 
Goal: Avoid ghosts (they drain prayer), follow the path carefully. Answer involves "Affection". 
Reward: 5,000 GP + Fancy boots (cosmetic). 
Floor 4 — "Temptation" (Self-control) 
Monsters: Ankou (level 22-35, tough but rare). The floor is mostly a maze. 
Goal: Navigate the box maze.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
ters: Ankou (level 22-35, tough but rare). The floor is mostly a maze. 
Goal: Navigate the box maze. Don't step on dangerous floor tiles (they damage you). Answer involves "Temptation". 
Reward: 10,000 GP + Rainbow boots (cosmetic) + "Idea!" emote . 
Total SoS Rewards: 17,000 GP + 4 boots (cosmetic) + Genie lamp + "Idea!" emote. This is HUGE for a new account! 

🌇 Afternoon Session (1-2 hours) — Combat Training + Quests 
After the Stronghold, you have 17,000 GP in your bank.

⚔️ Combat Training: Minotaurs (SoS Floor 1) 
Go back to Floor 1 of the Stronghold (or stay there after completing it). Minotaurs are level 12 and give excellent XP for your level range. They also drop iron arrows and iron scimitars.
