Food: Bring lobsters or swordfish if you have the GP, otherwise cook your own shrimp/anchovies.

📜 Quests: Romeo & Juliet + Doric's Quest 
Romeo & Juliet (no requirements): 
Start: Romeo in Varrock square (middle of Varrock, near the fountain). 
Walkthrough: Talk to Romeo, then Juliet (second floor of a house in Varrock east side), then back to Romeo. Very simple. 
Rewards: 1 Quest Point (that's it, but Quest Points count toward Dragon Slayer).

Doric's Quest (no requirements): 
Start: Doric north of Falador (west of Edgeville, follow the path). 
Walkthrough: He needs 6 clay, 4 copper ore, 2 iron ore. Mine them yourself (clay/copper near Doric, iron in Al Kharid mine) or buy from GE (cheaper). 
Rewards: 1,300 Mining XP — this jumps Mining from level 1 to level 11! Also 1 Quest Point, and free use of Doric's anvils (minor convenience).

Day 3 — Money Focus & Dragon Slayer Prep (2-3 hours) 
By Day 3, you should have a feel for the game. Today is about making real money and preparing for Dragon Slayer — the most important F2P quest. Dragon Slayer lets you wear a Rune platebody (the best F2P torso armor) and gives massive Strength and Defence XP.

🌅 Morning Session (1.5 hours) — Money Making 
💰 Option A: Cowhides (If You Haven't Collected 200+ Yet) 
Location: Cow field east of Lumbridge (east of River Lum). 
Method: Kill cows, loot cowhides, bank at Lumbridge Castle basement deposit box. 
100 cowhides = ~11,000 GP (sell on GE). 
Goal: Collect 200-300 cowhides = ~22,000-33,000 GP. 
Time: ~1 hour for 200 hides.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

GP (sell on GE). 
Goal: Collect 200-300 cowhides = ~22,000-33,000 GP. 
Time: ~1 hour for 200 hides. 
Why it's good: Zero requirements. Cows are level 2, you can't fail. It's boring but reliable. 

💰 Option B: Hill Giants (Better GP/HR at Your Level) 
Location: Edgeville Dungeon (west of Edgeville bank, enter the dungeon entrance). You need a Brass Key to access the Hill Giant area. Buy a Brass Key from the Grand Exchange for ~500 GP (or get a friend to drop one).

Hill Giant area. Buy a Brass Key from the Grand Exchange for ~500 GP (or get a friend to drop one). 
Requirements: ~15+ Combat (20+ recommended for efficiency). 
Hill Giants: Level 28, 35 HP. They drop Big Bones (Prayer XP), Limpwurt roots (herb, ~500-1,000 GP each), Nature runes (alchemy, ~200 GP each), and other valuable loot. 
Banking: Edgeville bank is RIGHT THERE. Super convenient. Loot → bank → repeat. 
Profit: ~30-50k GP per hour (depending on drops).

Strategy: Use "Strength" attack style (bottom option) to maximize damage. Bring food (lobsters recommended). 
Prayer tip: Bury Big Bones for Prayer XP, or save them for a member altar (3.5x XP). Your choice. 
Why Hill Giants are amazing: They're the best F2P money maker for low-to-mid combat levels. Plus you get Prayer XP from Big Bones and decent combat XP.

🌇 Afternoon Session (1-2 hours) — Dragon Slayer Prerequisite Quests 
Dragon Slayer has NO skill requirements, but it DOES require 33 Quest Points. You should have ~8-10 by now. You need ~23-25 more. These three quests get you closer: 
1. Pirate's Treasure 15 min 
Requirements: None. 
Start: Redbeard Frank in the Blue Moon Inn (Varrock, south side of the city, look for the inn sign).

Start: Redbeard Frank in the Blue Moon Inn (Varrock, south side of the city, look for the inn sign). 
Walkthrough: He wants rum. Buy rum from Port Sarim (Wydin's Food Store, ~40 GP). Give him the rum, follow the clues to find a treasure map, dig in the correct spot (use a spade). 
Rewards: +2 Thieving (if Thieving is low), 1/4 of a Sword (cosmetic), ~10,000 GP worth of emeralds and rings, 1 Quest Point. 
Why do it: Gives GP and a Quest Point.

Requirements: None. 
Start: Morgan in Draynor Village (west of Lumbridge, north of the market area). 
Walkthrough: He wants you to kill Count Draynor (a vampire). Get a stake and hammer (buy from Grand Exchange or find in Draynor manor basement).

Why it's essential: 4,825 Attack XP for a 20-minute quest is one of the best XP rates in the entire game. Do this as soon as possible.

Day 4 — DRAGON SLAYER & Big Milestones (2-3 hours) 
Today is the day. You're going to attempt Dragon Slayer — the hardest F2P quest and one of the most iconic quests in OSRS. Completing it is a rite of passage. The rewards are insane: 18,650 Strength XP, 18,650 Defence XP, the ability to wear a Rune platebody, and 2 Quest Points.

ne: 18,650 Strength XP, 18,650 Defence XP, the ability to wear a Rune platebody, and 2 Quest Points. 
🐉 Dragon Slayer — Full Walkthrough 
Requirements: 33 Quest Points (you should have ~15-20 by now — if not, do more F2P quests first!). 
Step 1: Start the Quest 
Talk to Oziach west of Edgeville (he's standing outside the building west of the Edgeville bank, near the wilderness entrance). He won't give you the quest yet — he tells you to go to the Champions' Guild first.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
rness entrance). He won't give you the quest yet — he tells you to go to the Champions' Guild first. 
Step 2: Champions' Guild 
The Champions' Guild is south of Varrock (south of the city, across the river, follow the path). Talk to the Guildmaster. He gives you Map Piece 1 and tells you to find the other two pieces. 
Step 3: Map Piece 2 — Melzar's Maze 
Location: Melzar's Maze is north of Rimmington (south of Falador, west of Port Sarim). It's a maze building with multiple rooms.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
orth of Rimmington (south of Falador, west of Port Sarim). It's a maze building with multiple rooms. 
Walkthrough: 
Enter the maze. You'll go through rooms with different monsters. 
Zombie room: Kill the zombie, loot the Orange key . 
Ghost room: Kill the ghost, loot the Yellow key . 
Skeleton room: Kill the skeleton, loot the Blue key . 
Wizard room: Kill the wizard, loot the Magenta key . 
Demon room: Use all keys on the door. Kill the demon (level 36, dangerous!). Loot Map Piece 2 .

Total time: ~15-20 minutes. 
Step 4: Map Piece 3 — Dwarven Mines (Ice Mountain) 
Location: The Oracle is above the Dwarven Mines (Ice Mountain, west of Falador, north of the Dwarven Mine entrance). 
What to bring: Silk, Lobster pot, Unfired bowl, Nails (any type). Buy these from the Grand Exchange before you go. 
Walkthrough: 
Talk to the Oracle. He speaks in riddles.

Read the blueprints in the Dwarven Mine (enter the mine, read the board on the wall). 
The blueprints tell you which item to get and where to find it. Follow the clues, get the item, bring it back to the Oracle. 
The Oracle gives you Map Piece 3 . 
Total time: ~10-15 minutes (if you read carefully).

Step 5: Combine Map Pieces & Talk to Klarense 
Use the three map pieces together (inventory, right-click "Use"). You get a map . Now talk to Klarense in Port Sarim (south of Port Sarim, near the docks). 
Klarense offers to sell you a ship for 2,000 GP. Try to negotiate — you might get it for 1,000-1,500 GP. Pay him, then board the ship.

Step 6: The Dragon — Elvarg 
Once you sail to Crandor, you'll face Elvarg — a level 83 dragon. This is the hardest fight in F2P. Here's how to win: 
⚔️ Elvarg Fight Strategy 
Required gear: Anti-dragonfire shield (get from Oziach for FREE before the fight), best weapon (Mithril/Adamant scimitar + shield), best armor you can afford.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
FREE before the fight), best weapon (Mithril/Adamant scimitar + shield), best armor you can afford. 
Required inventory: 15-20 lobsters (or swordfish if you're rich), Prayer potions (optional but helpful), teleport tab (just in case). 
Combat style: Use "Strength" attack style (bottom option) for max damage. Activate "Protect from Magic" prayer if you have Prayer level 12+ (reduces dragonfire damage). 
Dragonfire: Elvarg uses dragonfire which can hit 30-50 damage.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
vel 12+ (reduces dragonfire damage). 
Dragonfire: Elvarg uses dragonfire which can hit 30-50 damage. The Anti-dragonfire shield reduces this to ~10-15. Protect from Magic prayer reduces it further. 
Melee phase: After dragonfire, Elvarg fights with melee. She can hit 10-15 per hit. Eat food to stay above 20 HP at all times. 
The fight: It takes ~3-5 minutes with 25-30 combat. Stay calm, eat when needed, and keep attacking. She WILL die. 
If you die: It's okay! You respawn at Lumbridge.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
eat when needed, and keep attacking. She WILL die. 
If you die: It's okay! You respawn at Lumbridge. Retrieve your stuff from your grave (3 minutes). Try again with better gear/more food. 

Step 7: Claim Your Reward 
After killing Elvarg, return to Oziach in Edgeville. He congratulates you. Quest complete! 
Rewards: 
18,650 Strength XP — This is ~2 full levels at level 30 Strength! 
18,650 Defence XP — Same massive boost for Defence.

— This is ~2 full levels at level 30 Strength! 
18,650 Defence XP — Same massive boost for Defence. 
Ability to wear Rune platebody (the best F2P torso armor, ~200 defence rating). 
2 Quest Points (now at ~17-22, almost there!). 
Ability to buy/compose Dragon battleaxe and Dragon longsword from other players (member content, but unlocked by this quest). 

🎉 Post-Dragon Slayer Celebration 
You just completed the hardest F2P quest in the game! Take a screenshot. Tell your clan chat.

You just completed the hardest F2P quest in the game! Take a screenshot. Tell your clan chat. This is a genuine achievement. 
Buy a Rune platebody: Go to Scavvo's shop in the Champions' Guild (south of Varrock) or buy from the Grand Exchange (~20-25k GP). Equip it. You're now wearing the best F2P torso armor in the game. 
Check your stats: Your Strength and Defence probably jumped 3-4 levels each from the quest rewards. You're significantly stronger now.

Combat level: Should be around 30-35. You're no longer a noob — you're a legitimate adventurer. 

🌇 Afternoon — Skill Training (Optional) 
If you have time left, start training a gathering skill. You'll need one at level 30+ for member money making. 
Woodcutting: Cut oak trees (level 15) near Draynor bank. AFKable, decent money (oak logs ~20-30 GP each).

text:
utting: Cut oak trees (level 15) near Draynor bank. AFKable, decent money (oak logs ~20-30 GP each). 
Fishing: Fish trout/salmon (level 20) at the Barbarian Village fishing spot. Good food source for future combat. 
Mining: Mine iron ore (level 15) in Al Kharid. Iron ore ~100 GP each, fast respawn.

💳 Membership Math — Reality Check 
Let's talk about membership. A 14-day bond costs ~2.3 million GP on the Grand Exchange (as of 2026). You've been playing for 5 days and probably have ~80-150k GP in the bank. That's... not close.

And that's NORMAL. Almost no one can afford a bond within their first week of F2P. It typically takes 2-4 weeks of consistent F2P playing to afford a bond. 
Your options: 
Option 1: Buy a $7-8 USD bond. This is the cheapest option for most players. One month of membership is less than a Netflix subscription. If you can afford it, just buy it — your account progression will 10x.

Option 2: Keep playing F2P until you can afford a bond. It takes 2-4 weeks typically. Use the money making methods in this guide. Once you get your first bond, maintaining bonds via in-game GP is easier (you'll have member money makers). 
Option 3: Wait for a bond price drop. Bond prices fluctuate based on GE supply/demand. Check the GE regularly — sometimes you can snipe a cheap bond.

Common Mistakes to Avoid 
Every OSRS player makes mistakes in their first week. Learn from theirs (and mine) so you don't have to learn the hard way. 
Don't spread yourself too thin. There are 23 skills in OSRS. You cannot train them all at once. Pick 1-2 skills to focus on, and ignore the rest for now. You have years to max everything. 
Don't ignore quests. I said this in the Golden Rules and I'll say it again: quests give more XP per hour than any skill training.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
his in the Golden Rules and I'll say it again: quests give more XP per hour than any skill training. Every quest you complete makes future training 2x easier. If you're stuck, go do a quest. 
Don't hoard junk items. Bank space is limited (available in F2P). If an item isn't useful, sell it on the GE, alch it (if you have the Magic level), or drop it. A cluttered bank is a cluttered mind. 
Don't train Prayer only by burying bones. Burying bones is slow (XP rate ~10-20k/hr).
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
uttered mind. 
Don't train Prayer only by burying bones. Burying bones is slow (XP rate ~10-20k/hr). The Chaos Altar in the Wilderness (member content) gives 3.5x XP per bone. One trip to Chaos Altar = 10 hours of burying. It's dangerous (PvP zone) but worth it. 
Don't buy expensive gear too early. An Adamant scimitar (costs ~1,000 GP) is fine until 40 Attack. You don't need a Dragon scimitar (member content, expensive) at level 30. Save your money for membership — bonds are expensive.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
mitar (member content, expensive) at level 30. Save your money for membership — bonds are expensive. 
Don't forget food. Always bring food when training combat. Lobsters (heals 12 HP) or Swordfish (heals 14 HP) are ideal. Nothing is more embarrassing than dying to a level 12 Minotaur because you forgot to bring food. 
Don't skip Dragon Slayer. I don't care how hard it looks. Dragon Slayer is the gateway to the rest of the game.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
Dragon Slayer. I don't care how hard it looks. Dragon Slayer is the gateway to the rest of the game. The Rune platebody (unlocked by this quest) is the best F2P torso armor. The XP rewards are massive. Do the quest. 
Don't play alone. OSRS has a massive, friendly community. Join a clan. Ask questions in the help channel. Watch YouTube guides. Read the OSRS Wiki (it's amazing). You'll learn faster and have more fun. 
Don't get discouraged. Your first week will feel slow. That's normal.
title: OSRS First Week Progression Guide 2026

text:
arn faster and have more fun. 
Don't get discouraged. Your first week will feel slow. That's normal. OSRS is a game where progress compounds. Week 2 is faster than Week 1. Week 4 is faster than Week 2. Stick with it and you'll be amazed at how far you've come. 
Don't forget to have fun. OSRS is a game. If you're not enjoying yourself, take a break. Try a different activity. Set a silly goal (like collecting every dye color). The best OSRS players are the ones who find joy in the grind.

⚡ Efficiency Tips 
Use the OSRS Wiki: wiki.gg/oldschool-runescape — it's the best game wiki ever made. Every item, NPC, quest, and skill has a dedicated page with exact numbers and strategies. 
Install RuneLite (if on PC): RuneLite is a free, open-source OSRS client with quality-of-life plugins (entity hider, ground item indicators, tile markers, etc.).

Learn to use the Grand Exchange: The GE is the economy hub. Learn to buy/sell items efficiently (undercut buy orders, instant sell offers). Don't be the person selling items at 50% of GE mid-price.

Watch YouTube guides: Channels like "OSRS Guide", "Echo", "Protoxx", and "Woox" make amazing content. Watch a 10-minute guide before starting a new activity — it'll save you hours of trial and error.

Take breaks: OSRS is designed to be played for years. You don't need to grind 12 hours a day. Play for an hour, then do something else. Consistency beats intensity.

1. Gear Basics — Slots, Stats, and Set Bonuses 
Your character has 11 equipment slots: Head, Cape, Neck, Weapon, Body, Shield, Legs, Gloves, Boots, Ring, and Ammo. Each item in a slot provides stat bonuses.
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
eapon, Body, Shield, Legs, Gloves, Boots, Ring, and Ammo. Each item in a slot provides stat bonuses. 
📊 Key Combat Stats to Understand 
Stat What It Does 
Attack Bonus (Stab/Slash/Crush) Increases chance to hit with Melee 
Defence Bonus Reduces chance of being hit 
Strength Bonus Increases max damage with Melee

8. Gear FAQs 
When should I upgrade my armor? Upgrade when you hit the next tier's Defence requirement: 20 for Mithril, 40 for Rune, 70 for Barrows. Don't spend GP on armor upgrades between these milestones — the difference is minimal. 
Is Bandos/Armadyl worth it for beginners? Not at all. Bandos (70 Defence) and Armadyl (70 Ranged + Defence) cost millions and require end-game bossing to obtain.
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
(70 Defence) and Armadyl (70 Ranged + Defence) cost millions and require end-game bossing to obtain. Barrows is 20–30x cheaper and nearly as good for most content. 
Should I repair Barrows armor? Yes, but it's cheap. Repair at Bob in Lumbridge (NPC) or use an Armour Stand in your Player-Owned House. A full set in disrepair costs ~300,000 GP to repair — well worth the benefits. 
What's the best weapon for training Strength? Set your combat style to Aggressive (trains Strength).
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
What's the best weapon for training Strength? Set your combat style to Aggressive (trains Strength). Use Scimitar for fast attack speed + decent Strength bonus. At 70 Attack, the Abyssal Whip is best — but it only trains Defence or Attack, not Strength. Use a Berserker necklace + Obsidian maul setup for Strength. 
What goes in the cape slot for beginners? Ava's Accumulator (from Animal Magnetism quest) is the best cape for Ranged — it auto-collects arrows.
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
a's Accumulator (from Animal Magnetism quest) is the best cape for Ranged — it auto-collects arrows. Fire Cape (from TzHaar Fight Cave) is the best melee cape (requires 70+ combat). God Cape (from Horror from the Deep quest) is the best Magic cape. Don't leave the cape slot empty — even a cheap cape adds Defence bonuses. 
Should I buy cosmetics over stats? Never. In OSRS, cosmetics (party hats, Santa hats, etc.) cost millions and provide zero stats.
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

tats? Never. In OSRS, cosmetics (party hats, Santa hats, etc.) cost millions and provide zero stats. Every GP should go into combat efficiency until you have 10M+ GP in the bank. Cosmetics are for end-game flexing, not for beginners. Focus on stats, not looks. 
What's the difference between "Rune armor" and "Rune equipment"? Rune armor includes: Rune Platebody, Rune Platelegs, Rune Full Helm, Rune Kiteshield. This is the best F2P armor set.
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
es: Rune Platebody, Rune Platelegs, Rune Full Helm, Rune Kiteshield. This is the best F2P armor set. Rune equipment can also include: Rune Scimitar (weapon), Rune Boots (not the same as Dragon Boots), Rune Defender. Buy the full set (Platebody + Legs + Helm + Shield + Scimitar) for ~200k GP total. 
How do I know if my gear is good enough for a boss? Rule of thumb: If your combat level matches the boss's recommended level, Rune armor + Dragon Scimitar is usually sufficient for beginner bosses.

he boss's recommended level, Rune armor + Dragon Scimitar is usually sufficient for beginner bosses. For harder bosses (Barrows brothers), use Prayer potions + Protect-from-Melee to supplement your Defence. Gear matters less than Prayer usage in most early boss encounters. 
What's Barrows armor and why is everyone talking about it? Barrows armor is a set of 6 armor pieces dropped by the Barrows Brothers (a boss activity).
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

text:
bout it? Barrows armor is a set of 6 armor pieces dropped by the Barrows Brothers (a boss activity). Each piece costs 100k–300k GP and has special effects (e.g., Dharok's hits harder at low HP, Verac's ignores Defence). Barrows is the best mid-level armor — cheaper than Bandos and better than Rune. You can start doing Barrows at 43 Prayer + 50+ combat. 
Should I use a shield or dual wield? For most training, use a shield + weapon (defensive style).
title: OSRS Gear Guide for Beginners 2026 — Best Equipment at Every Level

Should I use a shield or dual wield? For most training, use a shield + weapon (defensive style). The shield adds Defence bonuses and you train Defence simultaneously. Dual wield (two weapons) gives slightly more DPS but only at high levels. Dragon Defender (off-hand slot, from Warrior's Guild) is the best off-hand item for melee — it gives +25% accuracy and +24% damage bonus. 
How much should I spend on gear at each combat level? Level 1–20: ~5k GP (iron/steel).

How much should I spend on gear at each combat level? Level 1–20: ~5k GP (iron/steel). Level 20–40: ~15k GP (mithril/adamant). Level 40–60: ~100k GP (rune set). Level 60–70: ~200k GP (dragon weapon + rune armor). Level 70–99: ~3–5M GP (barrows + dragon defender). Don't overspend early — save GP for food and supplies. 
Should I use a shield or a 2-handed weapon? Use a shield (or Dragon Defender) unless you have a specific reason not to.

ld or a 2-handed weapon? Use a shield (or Dragon Defender) unless you have a specific reason not to. The Defence bonus from a shield or the +6 Strength bonus from a Dragon Defender makes a huge difference. Only use 2-handed weapons if the DPS gain outweighs the lost defence — which is rare for beginners. 
What's the difference between Attack bonus and Strength bonus? Attack bonus increases your accuracy (chance to hit), while Strength bonus increases your max hit (damage dealt).

increases your accuracy (chance to hit), while Strength bonus increases your max hit (damage dealt). For training, Strength bonus is generally more important — hitting harder means fewer hits needed, which means less food consumed. Stack Strength first, then Attack.

2. Dawn & Dusk Phase Mechanics 
The Grotesque Guardians fight has two distinct phases. You fight Dawn first, then Dusk. Understanding each phase's mechanics is critical for low-stat survival.

rning Gargoyles: Dawn summons 3 burning gargoyles that chase you. They deal heavy damage on contact. Run to the opposite side of the roof until they despawn. Do NOT try to tank them — they can combo 30+ damage quickly. 
Fire Drop: Dawn flies up and orange tiles appear on the ground. After 2-3 seconds, fire drops on every orange tile. Move to any non-orange tile immediately. This attack hits 20-30+ if you're caught in it.

Rock Throw: Dusk throws a rock that cannot be prayed against. It deals up to 15 damage. This is chip damage — eat when HP drops below 40.

6. Advanced Tips for Low-Stat Kills 
Use a house tab between kills for banking. A POH with a rejuvenation pool lets you restore all stats, prayer, and HP instantly, then teleport back via the Slayer Ring to the Slayer Tower. This dramatically increases kills per hour. 
Spec at the start of each phase. Use Dragon Claws or DDS special attacks immediately when Dawn spawns and again at Dusk's enrage phase. The burst damage shortens the fight's most dangerous windows.

awns and again at Dusk's enrage phase. The burst damage shortens the fight's most dangerous windows. 
Prioritize avoiding burning gargoyles over dealing damage. They deal significantly more damage than Dawn's auto-attacks. Run first, attack second. 
Count Dawn's fire drop timing. After Dawn flies up, you have 2-3 seconds before fire hits. Position yourself near a safe tile BEFORE she flies up, so you only need to move 1-2 tiles. 
Stay close to Dusk to minimize rock throws.

Stay close to Dusk to minimize rock throws. Dusk throws rocks more frequently when you're far away. Hugging him reduces incoming chip damage. 

7. Common Mistakes 
Standing in the fire drop. Dawn's fire tiles hit 20-30+. This is the #1 cause of death for new Guardians killers. Watch the ground and move immediately. 
Tanking burning gargoyles. They each hit 10+ and there are 3 of them. Running is always the right move.

text:
Q: What stats do I need for Grotesque Guardians?
A: Minimum requirements: 75 Slayer (to receive Gargoyle tasks), an active Gargoyle Slayer task, and a brittle or cornerstone roofing tile to access the Slayer Tower roof. Combat-wise, 75+ Attack, Strength, and Defence are recommended for consistent kills. You can kill them with lower stats (70+ melees), but expect fewer kills per trip and slower kill times. title: OSRS Grotesque Guardians Guide for Low Stats 2026 — Dawn & Dusk Strategy

text:
Q: How do I access the Grotesque Guardians?
A: The Grotesque Guardians are on the Slayer Tower roof. To access them: (1) Have an active Gargoyle Slayer task, (2) Obtain a brittle roofing tile (drops from gargoyles) or cornerstone roofing tile (200 Slayer points from any Slayer master), (3) Use the tile on the roof entrance on the top floor of the Slayer Tower. The tile is consumed on use — you need one per trip. Cornerstone tiles are recommended because they save inventory space.
title: OSRS Grotesque Guardians Guide for Low Stats 2026 — Dawn & Dusk Strategy

text:
Q: What is the best strategy for the Dawn phase?
A: During the Dawn phase, use Protect from Missiles (she attacks with Ranged). When she summons burning gargoyles, run away — they deal heavy damage and despawn after a few seconds. When she flies up, watch for orange tiles on the ground and move to any safe (non-orange) tile. Use your special attack weapon at the start of each phase for burst damage. At 50% HP, Dawn gains attack speed — eat up and prepare for more damage.

title: OSRS Guardians of the Rift Guide 2026 — Fast Runecrafting 1-99, Strategy & Rewards

text:
he Rift turned Runecrafting from the most hated skill in OSRS into something players actually enjoy. This guide covers the Temple of the Eye quest, optimal GOTR strategy, reward priorities, XP rates from 27 to 99, and how to maximize both XP and points every game. 
💎 GOTR — Quick Reference 
Location: Wizards' Tower south of Draynor Quest: Temple of the Eye 
Max XP/hr (99): ~75K XP/hr 27-99 Time: ~170-200 hours

text:
outh of Draynor Quest: Temple of the Eye

1. Temple of the Eye Quest — Getting Started 
Complete the Temple of the Eye quest to unlock Guardians of the Rift. No combat required. Takes ~20 minutes. Rewards: access to the minigame + 1,000 Runecrafting XP. After the quest, talk to Apprentice Felix at the Wizards' Tower to start playing. 

2. How GOTR Works 
Enter the rift: Games run continuously.
title: OSRS Guardians of the Rift Guide 2026 — Fast Runecrafting 1-99, Strategy & Rewards

text:
at the Wizards' Tower to start playing. 

2. How GOTR Works 
Enter the rift: Games run continuously. Enter the portal at any time — you'll join the next available game. 
Mine guardian fragments: Mine the large guardian remains in the center. Fragments are stackable. 
Craft guardian essence: Use fragments on the workbench to create guardian essence (scales to your RC level). 
Charge essence at altars: Enter the rift portal → choose elemental or catalytic altar → craft runes (gives RC XP).
title: OSRS Guardians of the Rift Guide 2026 — Fast Runecrafting 1-99, Strategy & Rewards

text:
at altars: Enter the rift portal → choose elemental or catalytic altar → craft runes (gives RC XP). Charged essence goes into your inventory. 
Power up guardians: Use charged essence on the barrier guardians and the Great Guardian in the center. This builds the barrier and earns points. 
Heal pouches: Overcharged guardians spawn near the Great Guardian. Click them to heal damaged pouches. 
Win the game: When the Great Guardian is fully powered, the game ends.

Win the game: When the Great Guardian is fully powered, the game ends. Collect reward points based on contribution.

Phase Action Why 
1. Start Mine fragments → craft essence → build barrier Early barrier building = more points 
2. Altar Opens Enter portal → go to HIGHEST altar you can access → craft runes → exit → power guardian Highest altar = max XP per trip 
3. Mid-game Repeat: mine → craft → altar → power up.

4. Overcharged Click overcharged guardians to heal pouches. Keep pouches FULL. More essence per trip = more XP 
5. Final Push When guardian ~80% powered, dump ALL remaining essence. Maximize your contribution before game ends

6. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Is GOTR better than traditional Runecrafting? 
A: For XP — ZMI altar is slightly better at 80+ RC. For enjoyment — GOTR wins by a landslide. Most players find GOTR genuinely fun, and the rewards (outfit, pouch) make traditional RC better too.
title: OSRS Guardians of the Rift Guide 2026 — Fast Runecrafting 1-99, Strategy & Rewards

text:
ost players find GOTR genuinely fun, and the rewards (outfit, pouch) make traditional RC better too. Do GOTR until you have full Raiments + Colossal Pouch, then decide which method you prefer. See our Cheapest 99 Runecrafting guide for comparison. 

Q: Do I need pouches for GOTR? 
A: Yes — strongly recommended. Pouches multiply the essence you carry per altar trip. At 85 RC with Colossal Pouch (40 essence), you craft 40 runes per trip instead of 28.
title: OSRS Guardians of the Rift Guide 2026 — Fast Runecrafting 1-99, Strategy & Rewards

Q: Can I AFK Guardians of the Rift? 
A: Semi-AFK only. Mining fragments is 30-45 seconds of AFK. Altar trips require full attention. The minigame is more active than Wintertodt but less intense than bossing. Perfect for second-screen play.

💎 Runecrafting Without the Pain 
Guardians of the Rift single-handedly saved Runecrafting. The XP rates are competitive, the rewards make traditional RC better, and — most importantly — it's actually fun. Get your Temple of the Eye quest done, grab your pouches, and join the game. Your Runecrafting cape has never been more achievable.

text:
Q: What level should I start Guardians of the Rift?
A: You can start at level 27 Runecrafting after completing the Temple of the Eye quest. The minigame scales with your level — higher RC means access to better altars and more XP per game. Start as soon as you unlock it.

text:
Q: How much XP/hr is Guardians of the Rift?
A: At level 27 RC: ~25K XP/hr. Level 50: ~40K XP/hr. Level 75 (Giant Pouch): ~55K XP/hr. Level 85 (Colossal Pouch): ~65K XP/hr. Level 99: ~75K XP/hr. These rates are significantly better than traditional Runecrafting methods.

text:
Q: Elemental or Catalytic altar in GOTR?

Blue/Tanzanite Phase (Range Attacks — Use Magic) 
Zulrah is blue. It shoots Ranged attacks that can deal heavy damage. Activate Protect from Missiles. Switch to Mage gear. Watch for snakelings and venom clouds.

damage. Activate Protect from Missiles. Switch to Mage gear. Watch for snakelings and venom clouds. The blue phase often includes moving toxic clouds — keep an eye on your feet. 
Red/Melee Phase — DO NOT ATTACK 
Zulrah is red and on the ground. It uses Melee attacks. You CANNOT attack Zulrah in Red form — any attack will be nullified. Just focus on avoiding toxic clouds and repositioning for the next phase.

Jad Phase (Rotation 3 Only) — Prayer Switching Required 
Zulrah alternates between Magic and Ranged attacks, one per tick. You must switch prayers between each attack. Watch the projectile: Green/blue = Magic (Protect from Magic), White/pink = Ranged (Protect from Missiles). Switch your prayer AFTER you see the projectile but BEFORE it hits you.

🔑 Jad Phase Survival Tip: During the Jad phase, PRIORITIZE PRAYER SWITCHING over attacking. If you attack between switches, you'll lose ticks and potentially miss a prayer swap. Just focus on surviving the phase — you can DPS Zulrah in the next green phase. Many beginners die here because they try to do too much at once.

7. Common Mistakes Beginners Make 
Focusing on DPS instead of survival. Your first 20 kills should prioritize staying alive, not speed. Learn the rotations, the safe tiles, and the prayer switches. Speed comes after consistency. 
Not memorizing safe tiles. During each phase, specific tiles are safe from toxic clouds. Stand on the east side pillars for most phases.

Panic eating during Jad phase. Eat between attacks, not during them. If you try to eat while Zulrah is attacking, you'll miss the prayer switch and take 41 damage. Brews are better than food for Zulrah because they don't delay your attacks. 
Forgetting to re-equip Ring of Recoil.

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: How many attempts does it take to get the first Zulrah kill? 
A: Typically 10-20 attempts. Most players die 5-10 times before surviving long enough to see the Jad phase, and another 5-10 times learning the Jad phase. Don't get discouraged — every death teaches you the rotations. The first kill is the hardest by far.

Q: Is Trident of the Seas required for Zulrah? 
A: Strongly recommended but not strictly required. You CAN use Iban's Blast (level 50 Magic) for budget kills, but kills will be significantly slower (4-5 minutes vs 2-3 minutes) and you'll use more supplies. The Trident of the Seas (75 Magic, ~3M GP) is the single best upgrade for learning Zulrah.

Trial Details & Solutions 
1. Music Trial (Olaf the Bard) 
Create a lyre and perform in the longhall. To make the lyre: cut a branch from the rare tree east of Rellekka (requires woodcutting), fletch it into an unstrung lyre, and string it with golden wool from the golden sheep. Then play the lyre in the longhall to earn Olaf's vote. 
2. Combat Trial (Thorvald the Warrior) 
Fight Koschei the Deathless in the warrior's arena.

2. Combat Trial (Thorvald the Warrior) 
Fight Koschei the Deathless in the warrior's arena. You must defeat him without dying — and he has 4 phases, each progressively harder. You don't need to kill all 4 phases; surviving the first 3 is sufficient. Use food and protect prayers. If you die, you can retry immediately. 
3. Hunting Trial (Swensen the Navigator) 
Navigate Swensen's maze by following the correct paths through a series of portals.

Obtain a romantic poem for Sigmund.

Switch your drink with Manni's without being caught.

2. Step-by-Step Walkthrough 
Start the Quest: Go to Lumbridge Swamp (south of Lumbridge Castle). Find the group of adventurers in the south-east corner of the swamp. Talk to any of them about Zanaris — they'll mention a Leprechaun who knows more. 
Find the Leprechaun: The Leprechaun is hiding in a tree near the adventurers. Right-click the tree and select "Search" to find him.
title: OSRS How to Complete Lost City Quest Guide 2026 — Dramen Staff & Fairy Rings

text:
haun is hiding in a tree near the adventurers. Right-click the tree and select "Search" to find him. He'll tell you about the Dramen Tree in Entrana Dungeon and warn about the Tree Spirit that guards it. 
Travel to Entrana: Go to Port Sarim docks. Take the boat to Entrana. Important: The monks on the dock will search you. You cannot bring any armor or weapons onto the island — they will be confiscated. Bring only food, potions, runes, and a knife.
title: OSRS How to Complete Lost City Quest Guide 2026 — Dramen Staff & Fairy Rings

or weapons onto the island — they will be confiscated. Bring only food, potions, runes, and a knife. 
Enter the Dungeon: On Entrana, walk to the north-east corner of the island to find the dungeon entrance (a cave). Inside, you'll find zombies and greater demons. Avoid combat — you have no armor. 
Find the Dramen Tree: Navigate through the dungeon to the back area where a large glowing tree stands. This is the Dramen Tree. When you attempt to cut it, the Tree Spirit appears.

e glowing tree stands. This is the Dramen Tree. When you attempt to cut it, the Tree Spirit appears. 
Defeat the Tree Spirit: See Section 3 for detailed fight strategies. The Tree Spirit is level 101 and uses Melee attacks only. 
Cut a Dramen Branch: After defeating the Tree Spirit, use your knife on the Dramen Tree to cut a Dramen Branch. 
Craft the Dramen Staff: Use the Dramen Branch with a knife (in inventory) to carve it into a Dramen Staff.

the Dramen Staff: Use the Dramen Branch with a knife (in inventory) to carve it into a Dramen Staff. This can be done anywhere — you don't need to be near a crafting station. 
Quest Complete! With the Dramen Staff in your inventory, the quest is complete. You now have access to Zanaris and all Fairy Rings across Gielinor.

Fire Magic: Bring fire runes and a staff. Cast Fire Strike (13 Magic) or Fire Bolt (35 Magic). The Tree Spirit is weak to fire and takes bonus damage.

6. Common Mistakes 
Bringing armor to Entrana. The monks confiscate it. You won't get it back until after the quest. Read the allowed items list carefully.

7. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: What do I need for the Lost City quest? 
A: Requirements: 31 Crafting, 36 Woodcutting. No quest prerequisites. Recommended: 43 Prayer (Protect from Melee trivializes the Tree Spirit), 60+ Combat, a knife, food (lobsters+), and optionally fire runes + a staff.

Q: How do I beat the Tree Spirit in Lost City? 
A: The Tree Spirit (level 101) uses Melee only and is weak to fire. Best strategy: Pray Protect from Melee (43 Prayer) to block all damage. Alternatively, cast Fire Strike/Fire Bolt for increased damage, or use a bow and arrows from a safe distance.

A: The Dramen Staff unlocks the entire Fairy Ring network — a teleportation system connecting dozens of locations across Gielinor. Equip the staff and click any Fairy Ring to use it.

🐒 Quest Guide -- Updated June 2026 Monkey Madness I is one of the most iconic quests in OSRS. It unlocks the Dragon Scimitar -- the best-in-slot scimitar for 60 Attack -- and grants access to Ape Atoll for high-level Woodcutting and Hunter training. This guide covers every step, the Jungle Demon boss fight, and all the crucial details for 2026.

Monkey Madness I has no strict level requirements, but 43 Prayer is functionally mandatory for the Jungle Demon boss fight. Without protection prayers, the demon can hit 32+ damage and will quickly overwhelm low-level players. Complete the prerequisite quests before starting -- they take about 1-2 hours combined.

2. Step-by-Step Walkthrough 
Part 1: Gnome Stronghold -- Starting the Quest 
Speak to King Narnode Shareen in the Tree Gnome Stronghold (Grand Tree, 1st floor). 
He explains that the 10th Squad went missing on Ape Atoll and asks for your help. 
Agree to help and speak to Daero on the 1st floor of the Grand Tree (near the spirit tree). 
Daero takes you to a hangar.
title: OSRS How to Complete Monkey Madness Quest 2026 -- Full Walkthrough

ak to Daero on the 1st floor of the Grand Tree (near the spirit tree). 
Daero takes you to a hangar. Speak to Waydar to board the glider to Crash Island. 
Part 2: Crash Island & Reaching Ape Atoll 
After crash-landing, speak to Garkor (the wounded gnome on the beach). 
Learn about the monkeys' oppressive rule and the 10th Squad's capture. 
Take the boat (operated by a gnome on the dock) to Ape Atoll.

ve rule and the 10th Squad's capture. 
Take the boat (operated by a gnome on the dock) to Ape Atoll. 
On Ape Atoll, immediately activate Protect from Melee -- monkey archers and guards patrol the area and will attack you on sight. 
Run to the temple in the center of the island. Enter the dungeon beneath the temple. 
🐒 Monkey Guards Warning: The monkey guards on Ape Atoll are aggressive and can hit through prayer with magic attacks. Keep Protect from Melee active and run past them quickly.

can hit through prayer with magic attacks. Keep Protect from Melee active and run past them quickly. If you get low on health, teleport out and try again. The guards are the most dangerous standard monsters in the quest. 
Part 3: Creating the Monkey Greegree 
In the temple dungeon, find Zooknock (the gnome mage prisoner). 
He explains that you need a monkey talisman and monkey bones (or monkey corpse) to create a transformation greegree.
title: OSRS How to Complete Monkey Madness Quest 2026 -- Full Walkthrough

you need a monkey talisman and monkey bones (or monkey corpse) to create a transformation greegree. 
Monkey Talisman: Kill any monkey on Karamja (near the volcano) for a drop -- it's common, roughly 1/20. 
Monkey Bones: Kill a monkey on Karamja and pick up the bones. Do NOT bury them. 
Return to Zooknock with the talisman and bones. He will craft your greegree. 
Wield the greegree to transform into a monkey -- while transformed, monkey guards will not attack you.

Speak to King Awowogei in the palace (central building). 
Attend the monkey ceremony in the temple (just follow the monkeys). 
During the ceremony, signal Garkor (he's hiding in the temple rafters -- look up). 
After the signal, the gnome squad will ambush.

The Jungle Demon (level 195) is the final boss. It attacks with both Magic and Melee, and can hit up to 32 damage. Preparation is key.

Fight Strategy -- Step by Step 
Enter the arena (south of the temple, through the gate after the signal). 
Immediately activate Protect from Magic. The demon's magic attack hits harder and is its primary attack. 
Attack with melee (or ranged if safe-spotting). Use Piety/Chivalry if available for faster kills. 
Keep your HP above 35. The demon can deal a combo hit (magic + melee) in quick succession.

Keep your HP above 35. The demon can deal a combo hit (magic + melee) in quick succession. 
Drink prayer potions at ~20 prayer points. Running out of prayer during the fight is a guaranteed death. 
If you die: Your items appear in a gravestone outside the boss arena. The demon does NOT heal between attempts. Return and continue the fight -- its HP stays where you left it.

🎯 Safe-Spot Strategy: For low-level players, you can safe-spot the Jungle Demon using Ranged or Magic by standing behind the pillars (stalagmites) in the boss arena. Stand on one side of the pillar, let the demon get stuck on the other side, and attack from a distance. The demon cannot melee you through the pillar, and Protect from Magic blocks its magic attack.

6. Advanced Tips 
Get the greegree as early as possible. The moment you can transform into a monkey, navigating Ape Atoll becomes trivial. Prioritise this step above all else in the quest. 
Keep the greegree after the quest.

comes trivial. Prioritise this step above all else in the quest.

Use the pillar safe-spot if you're a pure or low defence. 1 Defence pures complete this quest regularly using the safe-spot method. You don't need Defence -- you need patience and Protect from Magic.

Ape Atoll agility course: After the quest, the Ape Atoll agility course (level 48 Agility) is one of the best mid-level agility training spots, yielding 580 XP per lap. 
Bring alch runes. Monkey guards drop Rune Scimitars (~15K GP alch) fairly commonly. Alching them covers your supply costs for the quest. 
Stamina potions save time. The run from the boat to the temple is long and has obstacles.

7. Common Mistakes 
Walking around Ape Atoll without the greegree. The monkey archers and guards will destroy an unprotected player. If you must travel ungreed, use Protect from Melee and run without stopping. 
Forgetting monkey bones.

you must travel ungreed, use Protect from Melee and run without stopping.

Forgetting to buy the Dragon Scimitar.

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I do Monkey Madness I as a 1 Defence pure? 
A: Yes! Many pures complete Monkey Madness I for the Dragon Scimitar. Use the safe-spot method (stand behind a pillar, attack with ranged/magic).

A: Death is safe in this instance.

You can return with more supplies and continue the fight from where you left off. This makes the boss forgiving -- even if you die 3-4 times, the cumulative damage will eventually kill it. 

Q: Do I need to keep the greegree after the quest?

text:
Q: What quests do I need before Monkey Madness I?
A: Three quests are required: Tree Gnome Village, The Grand Tree, and Rune Mysteries. Tree Gnome Village and The Grand Tree set up the gnome storyline and are both intermediate quests taking ~30-45 minutes each. Rune Mysteries is a short novice quest required to access teleportation runes. It's highly recommended to also complete Tree Gnome Stronghold for the Spirit Tree network.
title: OSRS How to Complete Monkey Madness Quest 2026 -- Full Walkthrough

text:
Q: How do I kill the Jungle Demon if I have low combat stats?
A: If you have low combat stats (below 60 Attack/Strength/Defence), you have two options: (1) Safe-spot the demon using Ranged or Magic by standing behind one of the pillars in the boss arena. The demon cannot melee you from behind the pillar, and you can protect from Magic to block its magic attack. (2) Use Protect from Magic and melee with your best weapon. The demon's max hit is 32, so keep your HP above 35. Bring 10-15 sharks and 6 prayer potions. You can also leave and return -- the demon does not heal.
title: OSRS How to Complete Monkey Madness Quest 2026 -- Full Walkthrough

text:
Q: What is the best way to get monkey bones for the greegree?
A: The easiest method: go to the Karamja jungle (near the volcano), kill any monkey and pick up the bones. Do NOT bury them! Bring the bones back to Zooknock in the Ape Atoll dungeon. Alternatively, you can kill the monkey guards on Ape Atoll (use Protect from Melee) -- but the Karamja method is safer and faster. You also need a monkey talisman, which drops from monkeys on Karamja or the monkey guards on Ape Atoll.
title: OSRS How to Complete Monkey Madness Quest 2026 -- Full Walkthrough

text:
Q: How much GP do I need to buy the Dragon Scimitar after the quest?
A: The Dragon Scimitar costs 100,000 GP from Daga's Scimitar Smithy on Ape Atoll (located in the marketplace area, east of the entrance gate). You must have the monkey greegree equipped to trade with Daga -- he will not sell to humans. The scimitar requires 60 Attack to wield. On the Grand Exchange, Dragon Scimitars sell for ~60,000-80,000 GP, but Ironmen must buy them from Daga for the fixed 100K GP price.

🛡️ Why Karil's for Budget?: Corp's magic-based splash attack hits through melee defense. Karil's provides higher Magic Defense than melee armor, reducing chip damage from the splash. Once you can afford Bandos/Torva, the extra Strength bonus outweighs the magic defense trade-off — but for budget setups, Karil's keeps you alive longer.

3. Corporeal Beast Mechanics & Attack Patterns 
Attack Effect Counterplay 
⚫ Dark Energy Core A bouncing orb spawns and pings between players, dealing 10+ damage per hit. Moves faster with fewer players. Hit the core 3-5 times with a spear or fire spell to destroy it temporarily. It respawns after ~30 seconds.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
core 3-5 times with a spear or fire spell to destroy it temporarily. It respawns after ~30 seconds. In teams, assign one person as the "core killer." 
🦶 Stomp Attack Massive melee damage (40+) to everyone standing under Corp. Never stand directly under Corporeal Beast. Stay 1-2 tiles adjacent. Stomp triggers when someone is on the same tile as Corp. 
✨ Magic Splash AOE magic attack that hits all players in the cave for 20-40 damage. Protect from Magic reduces splash damage by ~50%.

📉 Stat Drain Periodically reduces Attack, Strength, and Defence by 10-15%. Drink Super Restores or Super Combat potions when your stats drop. The drain frequency increases as Corp's HP decreases. 
⚠️ Core Priority Rule: The Dark Energy Core is the #1 team-wipe mechanic at Corp.

⚠️ Core Priority Rule: The Dark Energy Core is the #1 team-wipe mechanic at Corp. If the core bounces freely, it can deal 50+ damage per player in seconds. In teams, ALWAYS have one player dedicated to killing the core whenever it spawns. A fire spell (Fire Surge) on autocast with a staff swap is the fastest way to three-shot the core.

4. Pre-Damage Strategy (Solo & Team Speccing) 
Before the "real" fight begins, experienced teams and solo players pre-damage Corp using special attack weapons. Corp's high Defence makes standard attacks inaccurate — spec weapons bypass this.

Bandos Godsword Reduces Corp's Defence by 10-30% (alt to DWH) 3-5 (budget option if no DWH)

6. Solo Corp Strategy (Advanced) 
Pre-Damage (Mandatory): Use the spec method described in Section 4. Land 3+ DWH specs and 3+ Arclight specs before the fight.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
se the spec method described in Section 4. Land 3+ DWH specs and 3+ Arclight specs before the fight. Without this, your melee accuracy against Corp's massive Defence is abysmal. 
Core Management: As a solo player, you're the core killer. The moment the dark energy core spawns, switch to a fire spell (Fire Surge) and 3-hit the core. Do not ignore it — the core deals exponential damage if left unchecked. 
Prayer Flicking: Flick between Piety and Protect from Magic.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
exponential damage if left unchecked. 
Prayer Flicking: Flick between Piety and Protect from Magic. When Corp does the magic splash animation, switch to Protect from Magic for 1 tick, then back to Piety. This takes practice but saves massive amounts of food. 
Stat Restoration: Bring 4-5 Super Restores. Corp's stat drain intensifies below 50% HP. If your Attack drops below ~80, your DPS collapses — restore immediately. 
Food Efficiency: Manta Rays + Karambwans for combo eating.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
our DPS collapses — restore immediately. 
Food Efficiency: Manta Rays + Karambwans for combo eating. Sara Brews + Super Restores if you're comfortable with brew mechanics. Solo kills require 10-20 food per kill depending on your gear and prayer flicking. 
⚔️ Solo Kill Time Expectation: First solo kills will take 8-12 minutes. After 50+ kills with good spec technique, expect 4-6 minutes. World record speedruns (max gear, perfect specs) clock around 2 minutes.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
hnique, expect 4-6 minutes. World record speedruns (max gear, perfect specs) clock around 2 minutes. Don't be discouraged by slow early kills — Corp is mechanically the simplest but most endurance-heavy boss in OSRS. 

7. Team Corp Strategy (3-5 Players) 
Role Assignment: Designate one "core killer" who carries a staff swap and autocasts fire spells. The other 2-4 players focus on DPS and never touch the core. 
Spec Rotation: Each player specs Corp with DWH/Arclight at the start.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
DPS and never touch the core. 
Spec Rotation: Each player specs Corp with DWH/Arclight at the start. After specs land, everyone switches to their main spear for the rest of the kill. 
Spread Out: Don't stack on the same tile — Corp's magic splash is AOE. Spreading out prevents overlapping damage that overwhelms your food supply. 
Kill Speed: A competent 4-man team kills Corp in 1-2 minutes (after specs). Teams of 5+ kill in under 1 minute.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
A competent 4-man team kills Corp in 1-2 minutes (after specs). Teams of 5+ kill in under 1 minute. At this pace, you'll see a sigil roughly every 45-80 hours of teaming. 
Loot Splitting: Agree on loot rules before starting. Standard practice: Elysian splits evenly; Arcane/Spectral splits or goes to the MVP; common drops cover supply costs for the trip. 

8. Common Mistakes at Corporeal Beast 
Not bringing a spear. This is the #1 beginner mistake. Any non-spear weapon deals 50% less damage.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
Not bringing a spear. This is the #1 beginner mistake. Any non-spear weapon deals 50% less damage. Your whip hits like a paper towel. Always use a Zamorakian Hasta or better spear. 
Ignoring the Dark Energy Core. The core can wipe a team in 10 seconds. If you're the designated core killer, your ONLY job is the core. DPS is secondary. 
Forgetting to spec before the kill. Corp has massive Defence. Fighting it with 0 DWH/Arclight specs means your accuracy is ~30-40% instead of 70-80%.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
ive Defence. Fighting it with 0 DWH/Arclight specs means your accuracy is ~30-40% instead of 70-80%. This doubles or triples kill time. 
Standing under Corp. The stomp hits 40+ and can be triggered by simply walking under the boss. Always stay adjacent, never directly under. 
Not restoring drained stats. Below 60 Attack, you'll splash constantly. Drink a Super Restore the moment you notice attack splashing more than usual. 
Expecting fast profit. Corp is a marathon, not a sprint.

Expecting fast profit. Corp is a marathon, not a sprint. You might go 500+ kills dry without a sigil, then hit an Elysian and a Spectral in the same hour. Budget for supplies and think long-term. 

9. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Is Corporeal Beast worth doing without a team? 
A: It depends on your goals. Solo Corp is inefficient for GP/hour compared to Zulrah or Vorkath (unless you get lucky with a sigil).
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
o Corp is inefficient for GP/hour compared to Zulrah or Vorkath (unless you get lucky with a sigil). However, solo Corp is the only way to guarantee you get the pet drop for yourself, and the challenge of soloing a 2,000 HP boss is rewarding in itself. Most players do Corp in teams for efficiency and solo it occasionally for the pet roll. 

Q: How many kills does it take to see a sigil drop? 
A: On drop rate, you'll see ANY sigil (Elysian, Arcane, or Spectral) approximately every 910 kills.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
A: On drop rate, you'll see ANY sigil (Elysian, Arcane, or Spectral) approximately every 910 kills. The combined sigil rate is roughly 1/910. Elysian specifically is 1/4,096. In a team of 4, that's about 230 kills per person between sigil drops. Corps drop rates mean you could go 3,000+ kills dry or get back-to-back sigils — variance is extreme. 

Q: Is Osmumten's Fang good at Corp? 
A: No. The Fang's passive (rerolling accuracy) works against it at Corp.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
umten's Fang good at Corp? 
A: No. The Fang's passive (rerolling accuracy) works against it at Corp. While the Fang bypasses Defence checks, Corp's 50% damage reduction for non-spear weapons applies regardless. You'll hit accurately but deal half damage, making the Fang strictly worse than a Dragon Hunter Lance. Use a spear/hasta — no exceptions. 

👹 Patience Pays at Corp 
Corporeal Beast is a test of endurance, not mechanical skill.

exceptions. 

👹 Patience Pays at Corp 
Corporeal Beast is a test of endurance, not mechanical skill. There are no prayer switches to memorize, no complex movement patterns — just a 2,000 HP tank that hits hard and takes forever to die. The reward, however, is one of OSRS's most legendary drops: the Elysian Spirit Shield, a piece of gear so rare and valuable that a single drop changes your entire bank value. 
Grab your spear, find a team, and start grinding. That sigil is out there somewhere.

text:
Q: What are the minimum requirements to fight Corporeal Beast?
A: Minimum: 90+ combat level, 70+ Attack/Strength/Defence, 70+ Prayer (Piety), and a spear-type weapon (Zamorakian Hasta minimum). No quest is required — Corp is accessed via the Games Necklace teleport to the Corporeal Beast Cave (level 20 Wilderness area, but the cave itself is safe). For solo, 90+ combat stats are strongly recommended.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
Q: Why do I need a spear to fight Corporeal Beast?
A: Corporeal Beast has a unique mechanic: all non-spear/hasta weapons deal 50% reduced damage. This applies to every weapon type except spears and hastae. Acceptable weapons: Zamorakian Hasta (budget, ~10M), Dragon Hunter Lance (mid, ~95M), and Osmumten's Fang (passive accuracy bypass but still suffers 50% reduction — not recommended). Always use a spear.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
Q: How rare are Elysian Spirit Shields from Corp?
A: The Elysian Sigil drops at a rate of 1/4,096. However, the Corporeal Beast is usually killed in teams of 3-5, and the drop is split. In a 4-man team, effective rate per person is 1/16,384 kills. The Elysian Spirit Shield (combining the sigil with a Blessed Spirit Shield) is worth ~600M+ GP — it's one of the most valuable drops in OSRS.
title: OSRS Corporeal Beast Guide 2026 — Loot Table, Strategy & Solo/Team Setup

text:
Q: Can I solo Corporeal Beast, and is it profitable?
A: Yes, solo Corp is viable with 90+ combat stats and a Dragon Hunter Lance. Solo kills take 4-8 minutes each, yielding 7-15 kills per hour. Without sigils, average profit is 500K-1M GP/hr. Sigil drops make or break profitability — a solo Elysian is a 600M payday. Most players solo Corp for the pet (1/5,000 drop rate for the Corporeal Puppy) rather than consistent profit.

Galvek Final Boss 
Galvek is the final boss of Dragon Slayer II and the leader of the dragonkin. The fight takes place on a series of boats and has multiple phases: 
Phase 1: Galvek (Dragon Form) 
Galvek attacks with powerful dragonfire — use super antifire + Protect from Magic 
Move between the boats to avoid his fire breath sweeping attacks 
Attack with ranged while maintaining prayer and antifire 
Phase 2: Enhanced Attacks
title: OSRS How to Finish Dragon Slayer 2 Guide 2026

eping attacks 
Attack with ranged while maintaining prayer and antifire 
Phase 2: Enhanced Attacks 
Galvek adds meteor attacks — run away from shadow markers on the ground 
His dragonfire becomes more powerful — eat more aggressively 
Continue using ranged attacks and keep your prayer up 
Phase 3: Final Push 
Galvek enters a berserk phase with faster attacks 
Use your special attacks (dragon claws, DDS) to burst him down 
Eat to full HP before each attack to survive his max hits

title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
r method in OSRS — zero combat risk, zero skill requirements, and the GE processes orders while you sleep, skill, or boss. This guide covers everything from basic margin checking to advanced multi-slot flipping with 500K to 5M+ capital in 2026. 
📈 GE Flipping — Quick Reference 
Min Capital: 100K GP (high-volume items) Optimal Capital: 5M-20M GP 
GP/Hr (500K capital): 50K-100K per cycle GP/Hr (5M capital): 200K-500K+ per cycle

Key Tool: GE Tracker / RuneLite Flipping plugin Risk: Low-Medium (market crash risk) 

1. Flipping Basics: Buy Low, Sell High 
Flipping exploits the gap between an item's lowest sell offer and highest buy offer. When a player needs an item urgently, they pay above market price. When a player wants to dump items fast, they sell below market. You profit by being the patient middleman.

Term Definition Why It Matters 
Buy Limit Max quantity you can buy every 4 hours Limits how much capital you can deploy per item 
Margin (Spread) Difference between instant sell and instant buy price Your profit per item. Always check before flipping. 
Instabuy Offering 5-20% above market to buy immediately Reveals the real selling price for margin checks.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

buy Offering 5-20% above market to buy immediately Reveals the real selling price for margin checks. 
Instasell Offering 5-20% below market to sell immediately Reveals the real buying price for margin checks. 
Undercutting Listing 1 GP below the current lowest offer Makes your sell orders fill faster (common tactic). 
The Basic Margin Check Process 
Buy 1 of the item at a high price (e.g., +20% of GE listed price) — this reveals what sellers are actually asking.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
tem at a high price (e.g., +20% of GE listed price) — this reveals what sellers are actually asking. 
Immediately sell that 1 item at a low price (e.g., -20% of GE listed price) — this reveals what buyers are actually paying. 
Calculate the margin: Sell price minus Buy price . This is your profit per item. 
If margin exceeds 1% of item value and is at least 5+ GP, the item is worth flipping. 
Never commit full capital without a fresh margin check — margins change constantly.

5. Advanced Flipping Strategies 
Multi-Slot Flipping 
Use all 8 Grand Exchange slots simultaneously with different items at different price points. Diversify across 3-5 item types to spread risk. Keep 1-2 slots open for margin checks on new items. 
Update Flipping (Event-Driven) 
When Jagex releases a new boss, quest, or game update, certain items spike.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
Flipping (Event-Driven) 
When Jagex releases a new boss, quest, or game update, certain items spike. If a new boss drops a specific ammo type, demand for that ammo can jump 50-200% within hours. Watch the OSRS homepage and position buy orders before the update goes live. 
Passive Overnight Flipping 
Before logging off, place conservative buy offers (3-5% below market) on 8 different high-volume items. When you log back in, check which filled and relist them at market price.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:

🔄 The 4-Hour Rule: The GE buy limit resets every 4 hours. Set a phone timer or use Runelite's GE limit tracker. The most efficient flippers space their sessions around the buy limit reset to maximize capital turnover. 

6. Common Mistakes That Tank Your Profit 
Impatient selling.

6. Common Mistakes That Tank Your Profit 
Impatient selling. If your items haven't sold after 2 hours, don't panic-sell at a loss. Check the current margin — prices may have shifted. Adjust your sell price by 5-10%, but never sell below your buy price. 
Going all-in on one item. If that item crashes, you lose everything. Spread capital across 3-5 different item types in different categories (runes, food, potions, ore). 
Ignoring buy limits.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:

Ignoring buy limits. You can only buy a fixed amount every 4 hours. If you try to flip 50 Abyssal Whips at once, you'll fill 8 and wait 4 hours for the rest. Calculate your maximum deployable capital per item: Buy Limit x Item Price. 
Flipping obscure items. Items with low daily volume (under 10K traded per day) may take days to buy or sell. Stick to items that trade 100K+ units daily.

7. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I flip items on F2P worlds? 
A: Yes! F2P flipping is more limited (fewer item types, smaller margins) but still viable. Best F2P items: nature runes, raw lobster, raw swordfish, adamantite ore, and rune scimitars. Margins are typically 3-10 GP per unit on runes and 50-200 GP on equipment. 

Q: How many GE slots should I use at once? 
A: Start with 3-4 slots.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:

Q: How many GE slots should I use at once? 
A: Start with 3-4 slots. Use 1 slot for margin-checking new items and 2-3 slots for active flips. As you gain experience and capital, expand to 6-8 slots. Never use all 8 slots on the same item type — if that market crashes, all your capital is stuck. 

Q: What is the best RuneLite plugin for flipping? 
A: The "Flipping Utilities" plugin is the community standard.

Q: How do I avoid losing money on a bad flip? 
A: Set a stop-loss rule. If an item's GE guide price drops more than 5% while you're holding it, sell immediately at market price.

The players who make billions from flipping don't chase 50% overnight gains; they make 2-5% on 10-15 flips per day, compounding their capital over weeks and months. Start small with cannonballs and nature runes, reinvest your profits, and gradually scale into higher-margin items. 
The GE is open 24/7 — make it work for you.

text:
Q: How much starting capital do I need to flip items in OSRS?
A: You can start flipping with as little as 100K GP using high-volume items like nature runes, cannonballs, or raw shark. With 500K-1M GP, you can flip mid-tier items like prayer potions and dragon bones. With 5M+ capital, you unlock medium-risk flips like Abyssal whips, Dragon hunter crossbows, and high-tier PvM supplies for 500K+ profit per flip.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
Q: How do I check an item's profit margin on the GE?
A: Buy 1 of the item at a high price (e.g., 20% over market) — this reveals the real sell price. Immediately sell that same item at a low price (e.g., 20% under market) — this reveals the real buy price. Calculate margin = sell price - buy price. If the margin is positive and greater than 1% of the item's value, it's worth flipping. Use GE tracker websites or RuneLite plugins to automate this.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
Q: What are the best items to flip with 1M GP in OSRS?
A: With 1M GP capital, focus on high-volume consumables: nature runes (5-15 GP margin each, 25K buy limit), cannonballs (3-8 GP each, 10K limit), ranarr weed (100-400 GP margin), prayer potion (4) (200-600 GP margin), and raw/cooked shark (20-60 GP each). These items have massive daily volume so your orders fill quickly and your capital never sits idle for long.
title: OSRS How to Flip Items for Profit Mid Game 2026 — GE Flipping Guide with 500K-5M Capital

text:
Q: How much profit can I make flipping per hour in OSRS?
A: Flipping profit scales with capital. With 500K capital expect 50K-100K GP per flip cycle (4-8 hour fill times). With 5M capital expect 200K-500K GP per cycle. With 50M+ capital expect 1M-3M+ GP per cycle. The beauty of flipping is that the GE processes orders while you do other activities — it stacks with active moneymaking. Experienced merchers maintain 8-15 active flip slots simultaneously.

Agility is one of the grindiest skills in OSRS, but with the right courses and strategies you can cut hours off the journey to 99. Why Train Agility? 
Agility is a core quality-of-life skill in OSRS. Higher Agility levels mean faster run energy restoration, access to shortcuts across the game world, and the ability to complete certain quests and areas. At 99 Agility, your run energy regenerates nearly twice as fast as at level 1.

Why AFK Fishing? 
Fishing offers some of the highest AFK potential in OSRS. Unlike skills such as Agility or Runecrafting that require constant clicks, Fishing allows you to set up at a spot and only interact every 20–90 seconds depending on the method. This makes it ideal for players who want to level a skill passively alongside other activities. 
Barbarian Fishing simultaneously trains Fishing, Strength, and Agility.

Barbarian Fishing simultaneously trains Fishing, Strength, and Agility. 
Most AFK fishing methods require no banking — fish are dropped or cooked on the spot. 
Completion of certain quests unlocks faster methods earlier. 
Fishing cape (level 99) perk: unlimited teleports to Otto's Grotto. 

Early Levels: 1–58 Fast Progression 
The early levels of Fishing are fast regardless of method.

Early Levels: 1–58 Fast Progression 
The early levels of Fishing are fast regardless of method. Here is the recommended progression before unlocking Barbarian Fishing: 
Level 1–20: Shrimps & Anchovies — Fish at Draynor Village or Lumbridge swamp. Very low XP but quick to level through. 
Level 20–40: Trout & Salmon (Fly Fishing) — Barbarian Village river. Fly fishing is click-light and surprisingly fast XP for the level range.

— Barbarian Village river. Fly fishing is click-light and surprisingly fast XP for the level range. 
Level 40–58: Lobsters or Fly Fishing — Continue fly fishing for speed, or switch to lobsters for Members money. Fly fishing is still faster XP. 
Level 58+: Barbarian Fishing — Unlocked at level 58 with completion of Barbarian Training. This becomes your main method for the rest of 99.

How to Set Up Barbarian Fishing 
Complete the Barbarian Training miniquest with Otto Godblessed (start in his grotto south of Baxtorian Falls). 
Bring a barbarian rod and bait (feathers or fishing bait work). 
Fish the grotto lake — there are multiple fishing spots right next to each other. 
Drop all fish or use Shift+Click to mass drop quickly. 
Set up an auto-drop script or use the drop-all method for maximum AFK time.

5. Full Walkthrough: Step-by-Step 
Enter Warrior's Guild: Speak to Kamfreena. Verify you meet the 130 combined Attack + Strength requirement. 
Farm 500+ tokens: Go to the Animated Armour room with rune armour pieces. Kill animated rune armour until you have 500+ tokens. 
Go to Cyclops room: Climb to the top floor. Enter the Cyclops enclosure (your tokens start draining here).

7. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I get multiple Dragon Defenders? 
A: Yes. Once you have a Dragon Defender, you can continue killing Cyclopes for more. Many players get a second one to keep as a backup, or to use while the first is in Death's Office after dying in dangerous PvM.
title: OSRS How to Get Dragon Defender 2026 — Complete Warrior's Guild Guide

ne to keep as a backup, or to use while the first is in Death's Office after dying in dangerous PvM. You can also show a Rune Defender to Kamfreena to enter the basement where stronger Cyclopes have a higher Dragon Defender drop rate (1/100 instead of 1/100). 

Q: Can I use Ranged or Magic to kill Cyclopes? 
A: Yes, but melee is recommended. Cyclopes use Ranged attacks themselves, so ranging them means taking more damage. Melee is fastest and you can use your defender with it.

A: It happens. At 1/100 drop rate, about 5% of players will need 300+ kills for their Dragon Defender. If you're going dry, take a break, refarm tokens, and try again.

The Dragon Defender will be your melee off-hand for dozens of hours — from Slayer tasks through your first boss kills. It's one of the few untradeable items that every account should obtain, and the Warrior's Guild grind is a rite of passage for OSRS players.

text:
Q: What are the requirements to get a Dragon Defender in OSRS?
A: You need a combined total of 130 in Attack and Strength (e.g., 65 Attack + 65 Strength), OR a single stat of 99. No quests are required. You also need Warrior Guild Tokens (earned inside the guild) to access the Cyclops room where defenders drop.
title: OSRS How to Get Dragon Defender 2026 — Complete Warrior's Guild Guide

text:
Q: How long does it take to get a Dragon Defender?
A: On average, 1-3 hours depending on your combat stats and luck. The full chain is: Bronze (1/50), Iron (1/50), Steel (1/50), Black (1/50), Mithril (1/50), Adamant (1/50), Rune (1/50), then Dragon (1/100). Expect 200-400 total Cyclops kills from Bronze to Dragon. Each Cyclops kill takes 10-30 seconds depending on your stats.
title: OSRS How to Get Dragon Defender 2026 — Complete Warrior's Guild Guide

text:
Q: Do I need the previous defender in my inventory for the next tier to drop?
A: Yes. You MUST have the previous tier defender in your inventory or equipped for the next tier to drop from Cyclopes. If you bank or drop your Rune Defender, the Dragon Defender will not drop. Keep all previous tier defenders on you until the next one drops. The game checks your inventory and bank for the highest tier you own.
title: OSRS How to Get Dragon Defender 2026 — Complete Warrior's Guild Guide

text:
Q: What is the best way to farm Warrior Guild Tokens?
A: The Animated Armour room is the fastest token source. Bring rune armour pieces (rune platebody, rune platelegs, rune full helm) to the armour animator. Each rune piece yields about 80 tokens when animated and killed. A full inventory of rune armour can generate 500-700 tokens in 10-15 minutes. Dragon med helms give even more tokens but are more expensive.

1. What Makes the Graceful Outfit Essential 
The full Graceful Outfit provides three stacking benefits: 
30% reduced run energy drain — You can run for 30% longer before needing to walk. This is the main benefit and affects literally every activity that involves movement (which is almost everything).

in benefit and affects literally every activity that involves movement (which is almost everything). 
30% increased run energy restoration — Your run energy recovers 30% faster while walking or standing still. Combined with high Agility levels, this means you almost never walk.

Marks spawn on the course tiles themselves (not in your inventory) 
You must manually pick them up — they appear as glowing golden marks on the ground 
Marks despawn after 10 minutes if not collected 
Spawn rate increases with course tier (higher level = more marks per hour, generally)

9. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I lose my Graceful Outfit and how do I reclaim it? 
A: Graceful is reclaimable if lost on death below level 20 Wilderness. If you die above level 20 Wilderness or in PvP while skulled, Graceful is permanently lost.

derness. If you die above level 20 Wilderness or in PvP while skulled, Graceful is permanently lost. If permanently lost, you must re-earn all 260 Marks of Grace — there is no buy-back option. 

Q: Is the full Graceful set worth it for PvM or just skilling? 
A: Graceful is primarily a skilling and questing set. In PvM, you typically want combat stats from your gear.

Q: How many Marks of Grace do I need for the full Graceful Outfit?
A: 260 Marks of Grace total. Individual costs: Graceful hood (35), Graceful top (55), Graceful legs (60), Graceful gloves (30), Graceful boots (40), Graceful cape (40). At Seers' Village (30+ marks/hr at 60 Agility), expect 8-10 hours of rooftop training to collect all 260 marks.
title: OSRS Graceful Outfit Guide 2026 — Marks of Grace, Fastest Route & Recolors

text:
Q: Which rooftop course gives the most Marks of Grace?
A: Seers' Village (level 60 Agility) offers the best marks-per-hour ratio, averaging 25-35 marks/hr. Canifis (level 40) is second best at 22-28 marks/hr. After level 80, marks rates actually DECREASE at higher courses (Rellekka, Ardougne) because course length and XP scale differently than mark spawn rates. If you specifically want marks, stay at Seers' Village until you have all 260.

Q: Can I recolor my Graceful Outfit?
A: Yes. Complete Achievement Diaries to unlock recolors from the Agility Shop: Hosidius (green, Kourend & Kebos Easy+), Arceuus (purple, Kourend & Kebos Easy+), Lovakengj (dark red), Piscarilius (yellow), Shayzien (grey/blue), and more. Each recolor costs additional Marks of Grace. The Brimhaven Agility Arena also offers a unique dark blue recolor via tickets.

text:
Q: Is Graceful Outfit worth getting before training other skills?
A: Absolutely. Graceful is arguably the single best untradeable gear set in OSRS. It reduces run energy drain by 30% (full set) and increases run energy restoration by 30%. This affects almost every activity in the game: questing, herb runs, birdhouse runs, bossing, and skilling. Most efficient players rush Graceful as one of their first major account goals, often before anything else.

Construction Teleports Quality of Life Updated 2026 
House Teleport Tablets are one of the most useful consumable items in OSRS — they teleport you directly to your Player-Owned House without needing to be on the Arceuus spellbook.

text:
hey teleport you directly to your Player-Owned House without needing to be on the Arceuus spellbook. This guide explains the Construction level required, how to create tablets using a lectern, the efficient butler method, and all the uses for house teleport tablets in endgame play. 

What is a House Teleport Tablet? 
A House Teleport Tablet (also called a "Teleport to House" tablet) is a one-use item that teleports you to your Player-Owned House when crushed.

port to House" tablet) is a one-use item that teleports you to your Player-Owned House when crushed. Unlike the Teleport to House spell (which requires being on the Arceuus spellbook at level 40 Magic), the tablet works from any spellbook and any Magic level — making it invaluable for accounts that switch spellbooks frequently. 
Works from any spellbook (Standard, Lunar, Ancient, Arceuus). 
Useful for rushing to your house's portal nexus, fairy ring, or pool of refreshment.

How to Make House Teleport Tablets 
Making tablets requires soft clay and the appropriate magic runes. The process: 
Obtain soft clay — buy from Grand Exchange or make by using a bucket of water on clay rocks. 
Enter your Player-Owned House and go to the Study room with the Lectern.

Select "Teleport to House" from the menu. 
Each tablet uses 1 soft clay + runes for the Teleport to House spell (1 law rune + 1 earth rune). 
Click "Make X" to create as many as your inventory allows.

Butler Method — Fastest Tablet Making 
The Butler method is the most efficient way to mass-produce tablets.

text:
Method — Fastest Tablet Making 
The Butler method is the most efficient way to mass-produce tablets. A butler (hired via the Servant's Moneybag in your POH) will fetch noted soft clay from your bank while you continuously make tablets at the lectern. This allows you to make tablets non-stop without having to bank yourself. 
Butler Method Setup 
Hire a Butler (level 40 Construction) or Demon Butler (level 50 Construction) via your Servant's Moneybag. Demon Butler is faster.
title: OSRS How to Get House Teleport Tablet 2026

text:
uction) or Demon Butler (level 50 Construction) via your Servant's Moneybag. Demon Butler is faster. 
Put a large stack of coins and noted soft clay in your bank. 
Enter your POH and stand at the Lectern. 
When your soft clay runs low, send the butler to the bank ("Speak to butler, Fetch from bank, Soft clay, 26"). 
While the butler is away, continue making tablets with remaining clay. 
When the butler returns, they will un-note the clay and hand it to you automatically.

The real power of House Teleport Tablets comes from what is inside your house. Here are the key reasons to use them frequently: 
Pool of Refreshment (Level 65 Construction): Restores all stats, hitpoints, and special attack energy. Replace prayer potions when bossing by teleporting to your house between attempts. 
Ornate Rejuvenation Pool (Level 90 Construction): Restores prayer as well as all the above — the ultimate house pool. Extremely powerful for boss prep.

Portal Nexus (Level 72 Construction): Teleports to up to 12 different locations from your house. Makes your POH a global hub for travel.

1. Why the Rune Pouch is Essential 
Before the rune pouch, every spell you wanted to cast required physical runes taking up inventory space. Teleports, alchemy, combat spells — each needed 2-3 inventory slots. The rune pouch consolidates three rune types into a single inventory slot, freeing up 2 slots for supplies, loot, or gear switches.

6. Divine Rune Pouch Upgrade 
The divine rune pouch is a direct upgrade that stores 6 rune types instead of 3. It is created by using a Thread of Elidinis on a regular rune pouch. The Thread of Elidinis is obtained from the Tombs of Amascut raid. 
The divine pouch also allows storage of combination runes (Dust, Mist, Mud, Smoke, Steam, Lava), which the basic pouch cannot hold.
