text:
Q: How do I get the rune pouch in OSRS?
A: There are three methods: (1) Purchase from any Slayer master for 750 Slayer points — the most common method requiring about 25-40 tasks to accumulate, (2) Cast NPC Contact on the Mage of Zamorak after completing Lunar Diplomacy and Dream Mentor — this gives a free rune pouch, (3) Buy from the Grand Exchange — quickest but costs GP. The Slayer shop method is recommended for most players since you'll naturally accumulate points through training.
title: OSRS How to Get Rune Pouch Guide 2026 — Slayer, NPC Contact & Best Rune Setups

text:
Q: What are the best runes to store in a rune pouch?
A: The most popular setup is: Air Runes (slot 1), Law Runes (slot 2), Nature Runes (slot 3). This covers High Alchemy (1 nature + 5 fire, with fire runes from a fire staff) and virtually every teleport spell. For bossing, swap in Fire/Death/Blood runes. For PKing, use Death/Blood/Law runes. If you have a divine rune pouch (6 slots), add Dust Runes (counts as Air + Earth), Fire Runes, and Cosmic Runes.
title: OSRS How to Get Rune Pouch Guide 2026 — Slayer, NPC Contact & Best Rune Setups

text:
Q: What is the difference between the rune pouch and divine rune pouch?
A: The basic rune pouch stores 3 rune types (up to 16,000 each = 48,000 total). The divine rune pouch stores 6 rune types (up to 16,000 each = 96,000 total). The divine version is created by using a Thread of Elidinis on a regular rune pouch. Additionally, the divine rune pouch can store combination runes (like Dust Runes or Mist Runes), while the basic pouch cannot. The Thread of Elidinis is obtained from Tombs of Amascut.
title: OSRS How to Get Rune Pouch Guide 2026 — Slayer, NPC Contact & Best Rune Setups

text:
Q: Can I have multiple rune pouches?
A: No, you can only possess one rune pouch (or divine rune pouch) at a time. If you already own one, attempting to get another through the Slayer shop or NPC Contact will be prevented. However, the rune pouch is kept on death in most situations as a protected item, and runes inside the pouch are NOT lost on death — they stay in the pouch even if you die in the Wilderness.

Q: How do I get to the Alchemical Hydra in OSRS?
A: The Alchemical Hydra is located in Karuulm Dungeon on Mount Karuulm in the Zeah region. Fastest access methods: (1) Karuulm Slayer Helmet teleport — teleports directly to the dungeon entrance, (2) Rada's Blessing 4 (Kourend Elite Diary) — teleports near the mountain base, (3) Fairy Ring CIR — west of the mountain, (4) Xeric's Talisman to Mount Quidamortem, then run west. Once in the dungeon, navigate past drakes and wyrms, then take the elevator down to the Hydra area.
title: OSRS How to Get to Alchemical Hydra Guide 2026 — Slayer, Access & Profit

text:
Q: What are the requirements to fight the Alchemical Hydra?
A: Mandatory: 95 Slayer (cannot be boosted), an active Hydra Slayer task assigned by Konar quo Maten, and completion of Dragon Slayer II for Karuulm Dungeon access. Strongly recommended: 90+ Ranged (Hydra is primarily ranged), 90+ Defence, 74 Herblore for Anti-venom+ potions, and insulated/dragon boots to protect against the dungeon's hot floor vents.
title: OSRS How to Get to Alchemical Hydra Guide 2026 — Slayer, Access & Profit

text:
Q: What boots do I need for the Alchemical Hydra dungeon?
A: The Karuulm Dungeon floor has hot vents that deal periodic damage. You must wear boots that provide insulation: Insulated Boots (dropped by wyrms), Dragon Boots, Primordial Boots, Boots of Brimstone (Hydra-specific), or Granite Boots. Without appropriate boots, you'll take constant damage just from standing in the dungeon. Insulated boots are the easiest to obtain — kill a few wyrms in the dungeon before attempting Hydra.

2. Bone Voyage Quest -- Quick Walkthrough 
Bone Voyage is a short novice quest (15-30 minutes) that permanently unlocks Fossil Island. Here is the complete walkthrough: 
Speak to the Curator in the Varrock Museum (ground floor, east wing). He asks you to investigate a barge crew at the Dig Site. 
Travel to the Dig Site (east of Varrock). Go north past the exam centre to find the barge.

ig Site. 
Travel to the Dig Site (east of Varrock). Go north past the exam centre to find the barge. Speak to the Barge Foreman . 
Speak to the Lead Navigator (nearby). He needs help completing the barge. 
Gather these items: 3 oak planks (buy from sawmill or GE), 1 rope (buy from general store or GE), 1 copper wire (buy from Al Kharid general store or make with 1 copper bar). 
Use the items on the barge to repair it. Speak to the Lead Navigator again.

Set sail! You arrive at Fossil Island's camp. 
On the island: help the museum staff set up the camp. Build a crate (planks), make a fire (logs + tinderbox), and speak to the people around the camp. 
After the camp is established, the quest completes automatically. You now have permanent access to Fossil Island.

Activate ALL mushtrees immediately. This is the #1 Fossil Island tip. Run to each of the 5 mushtrees and click "Activate." This takes 10-15 minutes and forever changes your Fossil Island experience.

es and click "Activate." This takes 10-15 minutes and forever changes your Fossil Island experience. The Hilltop mushtree next to the bank is the single most useful teleport on the island.

7. Common Mistakes 
Skipping Mushtree activation. Players often land on Fossil Island, do Birdhouses once, and leave without activating the mushtrees. This makes every future visit 5x slower. Always activate mushtrees on your first visit. 
Not unlocking Fossil Island until late-game.

Forgetting to bring a chisel for fossils. Unidentified fossils pile up in your bank if you don't clean them.

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I access Fossil Island without completing Bone Voyage? 
A: No. Bone Voyage is the ONLY way to unlock Fossil Island. There is no alternative quest, no teleport that bypasses it, and no method to reach the island before completing the quest.

quest, no teleport that bypasses it, and no method to reach the island before completing the quest. The bright side: the quest is short (15-30 minutes), has very low skill requirements, and the prerequisite quests (Dragon Slayer I + The Dig Site) are quests you should complete early anyway for their own rewards. 

Q: How often should I do birdhouse runs? 
A: Every 50 minutes is optimal, but even 2-3 times per play session is worth it. Birdhouses take 50 minutes to fill.

Q: What's the best birdhouse type to use? 
A: Always use the highest-level birdhouse your Hunter level allows. The tiers: Regular (5 Hunter), Oak (14), Willow (24), Teak (34), Maple (44), Mahogany (49), Yew (59), Magic (74), Redwood (89). Higher-tier birdhouses yield more Hunter XP and have a better chance at higher-tier seeds in nests.

text:
Q: How do I unlock Fossil Island in OSRS?
A: You unlock Fossil Island by completing the Bone Voyage quest. Prerequisites: Dragon Slayer I and The Dig Site quest must be completed. The quest also requires low skill levels: 10 Agility, 10 Construction, 10 Crafting, 10 Herblore, and 10 Woodcutting. The quest itself takes 15-30 minutes. After finishing, you gain permanent access to Fossil Island via the barge at the Dig Site, as well as mushtree teleports, fairy rings, and other transport methods.
title: OSRS How to Get to Fossil Island Quick Guide 2026 -- Unlock, Transport & Activities

text:
Q: What is the fastest way to get to Fossil Island?
A: The two fastest methods: (1) Digsite Pendant to the Dig Site, then run north to the barge (free, takes ~30 seconds). (2) Fairy Ring code AKQ -- teleports you to the southern tip of Fossil Island directly (fastest, requires Fairy Ring access). (3) Mushtree Network -- once activated, you can teleport between any mushtrees on the island instantly (free, requires one-time activation of each mushtree).

text:
Q: What are the most important activities on Fossil Island?
A: The most important activities in order: (1) Birdhouse Trapping -- passive Hunter XP every 50 minutes, excellent for Ironmen and mains. (2) Hardwood Trees -- teak and mahogany trees for Woodcutting XP and Construction logs. (3) Seaweed Farming -- Giant Seaweed for Crafting (molten glass) on Ironmen. (4) Fossil Displays -- clean unidentified fossils at the Varrock Museum for Prayer and Slayer XP. (5) Underwater activities -- seaweed spores, drift net fishing, and lobster/crab spots.
title: OSRS How to Get to Fossil Island Quick Guide 2026 -- Unlock, Transport & Activities

text:
Q: Do I really need to unlock all the mushtrees?
A: Yes -- this should be the first thing you do upon arriving at Fossil Island. There are 5 mushtrees: Mushroom Meadow (central hub), Seaweed Swamp, Verdant Valley (hardwood trees), Forest (Ancient Cavern entrance), and Hilltop (next to the bank!). Run to each one and click 'Activate'. This takes about 10-15 minutes. After activation, you can teleport between any mushtree instantly, dramatically reducing travel time for all island activities. The Hilltop mushtree is the most important because it's right next to a bank.
title: OSRS How to Get to Fossil Island Quick Guide 2026 -- Unlock, Transport & Activities

Enter the boss room with Protect from Magic active. 
Stay at range (4+ tiles). The Thermonuclear Smoke Devil has no melee attack, but its AoE damage increases the closer you are. 
Watch for smoke clouds on the ground. When they appear, move 2+ tiles immediately.

Attack continuously. The boss has relatively low defence. Blowpipe with Amethyst darts or Dragon darts shreds through its health. On task with Slayer Helmet (i), kills take 60-90 seconds. 
Heal as needed. Use Saradomin Brews with Super Restores in the standard 3:1 ratio (3 Brew doses → 1 Restore dose).

text:
Q: How do I get to the Thermonuclear Smoke Devil in OSRS?
A: The fastest route: Use Fairy Ring code DKS to teleport to the Stronghold Slayer Cave entrance. Run north-west into the cave entrance. Inside, run all the way north through the cave, past Abyssal Demons and Nechryaels, to the far north-eastern corner where the Smoke Devil area is. The boss room is a separate instance -- right-click the entrance to 'Peek' or 'Enter'. You must have level 93 Slayer and a Facemask/Slayer Helmet to survive.
title: OSRS How to Get to Thermonuclear Smoke Devil 2026 -- Location, Gear & Boss Strategy

text:
Q: What gear should I use for the Thermonuclear Smoke Devil?
A: The best setup is ranged with a Slayer Helmet (i) for the 15% damage boost on task. Use a Toxic Blowpipe with Amethyst Darts if on a budget (under 5M), or a Twisted Bow if you can afford it (1.4B+ GP). Wear Karil's or Armadyl armour, a Necklace of Anguish, and Ava's Assembler. Always wear a Facemask or Slayer Helmet in the dungeon -- the dust will damage you without protection. Protect from Magic is the primary overhead prayer.
title: OSRS How to Get to Thermonuclear Smoke Devil 2026 -- Location, Gear & Boss Strategy

text:
Q: Is the Thermonuclear Smoke Devil worth killing for profit?
A: Moderate profit -- expect 200,000-600,000 GP per hour without rare drops. The Occult Necklace (1/350 drop rate, ~500K GP) is the primary profit driver. The Dragon Chainbody (1/2,000) and Smoke Battlestaff (1/512) are rare but add to the average. However, the boss is generally not considered a top-tier money maker. Most players kill it for the pet (1/3,000) or for the combat/Slayer XP rather than raw GP.
title: OSRS How to Get to Thermonuclear Smoke Devil 2026 -- Location, Gear & Boss Strategy

text:
Q: Can I kill the Thermonuclear Smoke Devil off-task?
A: Yes, you can kill it off-task, but it's strongly recommended to only kill it on a Slayer task. The Slayer Helmet (i) gives a 15% damage and accuracy boost on task, which significantly increases kill speed and profit per hour. The boss has relatively low defence, so off-task kills are still feasible, but you lose the biggest DPS multiplier available. Always save this boss for when you get a Smoke Devil task from Duradel or Konar.
title: OSRS How to Get to Thermonuclear Smoke Devil 2026 -- Location, Gear & Boss Strategy

Q: What Varrock Diary tier should I aim for?

Q: What is the best low level Crafting money maker in OSRS?
A: For pure profit per hour, Battlestaves are the best mid-level method. At 58 Crafting (Earth Battlestaves), you can earn 200K-600K GP/hr depending on your Varrock Diary completion (which determines daily battlestaff stock from Zaff). For very low levels (1-30), cutting uncut gems is the best entry point -- sapphires and emeralds have consistent 200-300 GP profit per gem and can be cut up to 3,000 times per hour for 600K-900K GP/hr theoretical max.
title: OSRS How to Make Money with Crafting Low Level 2026 -- Best Profit Methods

text:
Q: How do I maximize battlestaff profit daily?
A: Complete the Varrock Diary to increase Zaff's daily battlestaff stock: Easy = 15 staves, Medium = 30, Hard = 60, Elite = 120. Buy all staves daily at the discounted price (7K GP each), combine them with the highest-level orbs you can craft (air at 30, water at 43, earth at 58, fire at 62), and sell on the GE. With Elite diary, this is a consistent 100K-200K+ daily profit in under 5 minutes.

text:
Q: Is glassblowing profitable for money making?
A: Yes, glassblowing can be profitable, especially when you gather your own materials. Making unpowered orbs (level 12) from molten glass and selling them yields ~100-200 GP profit per orb. Light orbs (level 87) yield higher margins. The profit margin is highest when you collect seaweed and sand yourself (Fossil Island or charter ships) and superglass make them into molten glass. Commercially buying materials reduces margins to near break-even.

text:
Q: Can I make money while training Crafting for XP?
A: Yes. Dragonhide bodies are the best method for combining XP and profit -- crafting Blue D'hide Bodies (71 Crafting) gives 210 XP per body while profiting ~200-500 GP per body at high volume. At 2,000 crafts per hour, that's 420K XP/hr AND 400K-1M GP/hr. Battlestaves also provide excellent Crafting XP alongside profit. Gem cutting gives XP + profit but the XP rates are relatively low (40-115 XP per gem).

6. Understanding Zulrah's Scales — The Consistent Moneymaker 
Zulrah's Scales are the backbone of Zulrah's profit. With a 100% drop rate of 1–174 scales per kill (averaging ~75), they alone contribute 7,500+ GP per kill. Scales are consumed to charge three BiS items: 
Toxic Blowpipe — Best Ranged weapon for mid-level PvM, consumes scales per attack.

three BiS items:

Serpentine Helm — Venom immunity and strength bonus, requires scale charges. 
Trident of the Swamp — Premium Magic weapon, scales consumed per cast. 
Scale prices typically range from 100–130 GP each and are remarkably stable because demand tracks with the player base doing PvM. This predictable income is what makes Zulrah more reliable than bosses that depend entirely on rare unique drops.

7. Advanced Tips for Maximizing GP/Hour 
Pre-switch gear during phase transitions. The moment Zulrah dives underwater, switch to the next style's gear. The 2–3 seconds saved per rotation adds 3–5 extra kills per hour. 
Use Zul-andra Teleport scrolls. These drop from Zulrah and teleport you directly to the dock. Banking takes under 30 seconds versus 2–3 minutes with Fairy Rings.
title: OSRS How to Make Money with Zulrah 2026 — GP/Kill, Rotations & Budget Setup

text:
leport you directly to the dock. Banking takes under 30 seconds versus 2–3 minutes with Fairy Rings. 
Eat between phases, not during combat. During Zulrah's dive animation, eat to full HP. Eating during the fight costs you attack ticks and reduces DPS. 
Use offensive prayers. Augury (Magic) and Rigour (Ranged) boost your damage by 25% and 23% respectively. These are massive DPS increases that pay for themselves in faster kills. 
Switch to Dragon Darts for Jad phase.

1. OSRS Death Mechanics in 2026 — How Item Recovery Works 
When you die in OSRS, which recovery system applies depends on WHERE and HOW you died. The system is designed so that PvM deaths are forgiving (items are rarely lost permanently), while PvP deaths in the Wilderness carry real risk. 
Death Type What Happens to Items How to Recover 
PvM Death (most areas) Gravestone spawns at death location.

text:
pe What Happens to Items How to Recover 
PvM Death (most areas) Gravestone spawns at death location. All items go inside. Run back within 15 minutes → reclaim for FREE from gravestone. 
Gravestone Expired (15 min passed) Items transfer from gravestone to Death's Office automatically. Visit Death via Lumbridge Graveyard portal → pay retrieval fee → reclaim all items. 
Died Again (before reclaiming) Items from the first death move to Death's Office. New gravestone for the second death.

Wilderness PvP (Skulled) ALL items dropped on the ground for the PKer. Permanent loss. No recovery possible. 
Wilderness PvP (Unskulled) Keep 3 most valuable items (4 with Protect Item prayer). Rest drop. Protected items stay with you. Non-protected items are permanent loss.

Wilderness PvM Death (monster kill) Standard gravestone spawns (even in Wilderness!). Same as PvM death — run back within 15 minutes. Items are safe. 

2. Gravestone Recovery — Free, 15-Minute Timer 
When you die in a PvM death (raids, bosses, Slayer, quests, etc.), a gravestone appears exactly where you died. It contains ALL your items — tradeable, untradeable, and equipped gear.

Step-by-Step Gravestone Recovery: 
Respawn: You appear at your chosen respawn point (Lumbridge by default, or Falador/Camelot if you changed it). 
Check the timer: A death timer appears on your screen showing minutes:seconds remaining. You have 15 minutes (real-world time) to reach your gravestone. 
Travel immediately: Use the fastest teleport available to get near your death location.
title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

text:
gravestone. 
Travel immediately: Use the fastest teleport available to get near your death location. For distant or hard-to-reach bosses (e.g., God Wars Dungeon, DKs), bring emergency teleports and know the route beforehand. 
Find your gravestone: It appears as a glowing pillar of light visible from several tiles away. A minimap icon marks its exact location. 
Right-click "Loot": Right-click the gravestone and select "Loot." Items return to your inventory/equipment slots instantly. Cost: 0 GP.

gravestone and select "Loot." Items return to your inventory/equipment slots instantly. Cost: 0 GP. 
Re-equip and continue: Your Twisted Bow is back. Resume whatever got you killed — hopefully wiser this time. 
⏱️ Gravestone Timer Trick: The 15-minute gravestone timer PAUSES while you're logged out. If you die in a dangerous area and feel panicked, LOG OUT immediately. Take a breath, plan your route, look up teleport combinations, then log back in. The timer resumes where it left off.

an your route, look up teleport combinations, then log back in. The timer resumes where it left off. This is particularly helpful for deaths at God Wars Dungeon (need kill count to re-enter) or DKs (need a partner to open the doors). 
Gravestone Blessing (Extend to 1 Hour) 
If another player right-clicks your gravestone and selects "Bless" (requires 70 Prayer), the timer extends to 1 hour. This is essential for hard-to-reach death locations.

quires 70 Prayer), the timer extends to 1 hour. This is essential for hard-to-reach death locations. Always ask a clanmate or friend to bless your gravestone if you die at a remote boss. 

3. Death's Office Retrieval — Paid Recovery 
If you fail to reach your gravestone within 15 minutes (or die again before reclaiming), your items automatically transfer to Death's Office. Nothing is lost — Death holds your items indefinitely.

How to Retrieve from Death's Office: 
Go to the Lumbridge Graveyard (north of Lumbridge Castle). 
Find the glowing portal in the graveyard and enter Death's Office. 
Speak to Death. He will list all items he's holding and show the total retrieval fee. 
Pay the fee in GP. Items return to your inventory/equipment.

Twisted Bow Retrieval Fee 
Death's fee calculation for items worth 10M+ GP: 
First 100K value: Free 
100K to 10M: 1% of item value (max 99K GP) 
10M+: Roughly 0.05%-0.1% of item value, capped at 500K per item 
For a Twisted Bow valued at ~900M GP, the retrieval fee will be approximately 450,000–500,000 GP . This is a small fraction of the bow's value.

💰 Fee Reduction Strategy: If you died with multiple items, Death's fee covers ALL items in the gravestone. Instead of paying 450K+ just for the Twisted Bow, sacrifice cheap items from the gravestone (rune armor, potions, food) to reduce the total cost.

4. Wilderness PvP Death — The Permanent Loss Scenario 
If you die to another PLAYER in the Wilderness while skulled, your Twisted Bow is gone permanently. No gravestone. No Death's Office. No buy-back option.

⚠️ The Golden Rule of Twisted Bow: Never, ever bring your Twisted Bow into the Wilderness above level 20, period.

Rule of Twisted Bow: Never, ever bring your Twisted Bow into the Wilderness above level 20, period. Even unskulled with Protect Item, a single misclick (attacking a player, entering the Abyss without knowing it skulls you, or an anti-skull trick) can skull you, drop your protection, and cost you 900M GP in one game tick. The risk is NEVER worth it. Use a Bow of Faerdhinen or Dragon Crossbow for Wilderness PvM instead.

5. Special Death Scenarios — Chambers of Xeric, Theatre of Blood, Tombs of Amascut 
Raids have different death mechanics than standard PvM: 
Chambers of Xeric (CoX): Deaths inside the raid do NOT generate a gravestone. Instead, your items stay in the raid and you respawn at the beginning. You keep all items — there is no retrieval fee.

s stay in the raid and you respawn at the beginning. You keep all items — there is no retrieval fee. Simply re-enter the raid and pick up where you left off (or restart). Your Twisted Bow is never at risk in CoX. 
Theatre of Blood (ToB): Similar to CoX. Deaths inside ToB don't trigger normal death mechanics. You respawn in the lobby with all items intact. No gravestone, no Death's Office, no fee. 
Tombs of Amascut (ToA): Standard PvM death mechanics apply.

Tombs of Amascut (ToA): Standard PvM death mechanics apply. If you die in ToA, a gravestone appears outside the tomb entrance. You must run back within 15 minutes to reclaim items. ToA deaths are safe (non-Wilderness), so your Twisted Bow is always recoverable. 

6. Advanced Prevention — How to Never Lose Your Twisted Bow 
Always check "Items Kept on Death." Click the shield icon in your equipment tab before EVERY bossing trip.

check "Items Kept on Death." Click the shield icon in your equipment tab before EVERY bossing trip. Verify the Twisted Bow appears in the protected items list. This takes 2 seconds and prevents catastrophic mistakes. 
Set up RuneLite Death Indicators. This plugin shows a large, prominent countdown for your gravestone timer, minimap markers for your gravestone location, and desktop notifications when timer is low. It's saved countless Twisted Bows from expired gravestones.

Never bring Tbow to Wilderness. This bears repeating: the Wilderness is designed to make you lose items. One mistake, one lag spike, one anti-skull trick, and your Twisted Bow is gone forever. Use a Bow of Faerdhinen (30M, also uses crystal armor) or Dragon Crossbow for Wilderness content. 
Pre-plan death run routes.

30M, also uses crystal armor) or Dragon Crossbow for Wilderness content. 
Pre-plan death run routes. Before learning a new boss, plan exactly how you'll get back to your gravestone. Know which teleports, which paths, and which items you need. Panic kills more players than bosses do. 
Keep a "death run" bank tab. Stock emergency teleports (Ectophial, Fremennik Boots, Royal Seed Pod, House Tabs) in a dedicated bank tab. When you die, open the bank, grab your death run tab, and go.

Use a Ring of Life at dangerous content. If you're at a boss where you might disconnect (internet issues, server lag), equip a Ring of Life. At 10% HP, it automatically teleports you to safety. It's saved more Twisted Bows than any other single item.

7. Common Mistakes After Losing a Twisted Bow 
Panicking and dying again. After dying once with your Twisted Bow in a gravestone, dying again moves the first gravestone's items to Death's Office. While still recoverable, you now pay a fee. Stay calm and don't rush into danger. 
Logging out to pause the timer, then forgetting. The gravestone timer pauses when you log out.

Not realizing a death was PvP (not PvM) in Wilderness. In the Wilderness, it's easy to be killed by a PKer while fighting a monster.

ot PvM) in Wilderness. In the Wilderness, it's easy to be killed by a PKer while fighting a monster. If a player dealt the final blow (or any damage recently), it counts as a PvP death — meaning items drop instead of going to a gravestone. This distinction is critical. 
Assuming Protect Item always works. Protect Item protects 1 extra item on death, but it can be disabled by Smite (drains prayer) or if you get skulled (Protect Item only works if you're unskulled).

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: Can I insure my Twisted Bow like in other MMOs? 
A: No, OSRS does not currently have an item insurance system. There are no mechanics for paying an upfront fee to guarantee item recovery.

Jagex has discussed potential item insurance or "soul-bound" mechanics in the past but nothing has been implemented as of 2026.

Q: What if the server crashes while I have my Twisted Bow — do I lose it? 
A: It depends on when the server crashes. If the server crashes during a PvM fight, your character is frozen in place. When the server comes back online, you'll either be alive (if the boss died during the crash or you logged back in safely) or dead with a gravestone at your death location.

Q: How long do items stay in Death's Office? 
A: Indefinitely. Items in Death's Office never expire.

Q: How long do items stay in Death's Office? 
A: Indefinitely. Items in Death's Office never expire. You can leave items there for days, weeks, or months and they will be waiting when you return. The retrieval fee remains the same regardless of how long the items have been there. However, you cannot use items stored at Death's Office — they're in storage, not your bank. 

Q: Does insurance in other games apply to OSRS?

OSRS uses the gravestone + Death's Office system described in this guide. There is no way to pre-pay or guarantee item safety beyond the standard death mechanics.

The OSRS death system is designed to be forgiving for PvM — your Twisted Bow is almost always recoverable from boss deaths with a short run back or a modest fee. The only truly dangerous scenario is Wilderness PvP while skulled, and that's entirely avoidable with one simple rule: never bring your Twisted Bow into the Wilderness.

title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

text:
Q: How do I reclaim my Twisted Bow if I die in PvM?
A: When you die in most PvM areas (non-Wilderness), a Gravestone appears at your death location with a 15-minute timer. Run back to it and right-click 'Loot' to reclaim all items for free, including your Twisted Bow. If you don't reach it in 15 minutes, items move to Death's Office. Speak to Death via the Lumbridge Graveyard portal and pay a retrieval fee — for a Twisted Bow (~900M GP), the fee is approximately 450K-900K GP.
title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

text:
Q: What happens if I lose my Twisted Bow in the Wilderness?
A: If you die in PvP in the Wilderness while skulled, ALL items are dropped and can be picked up by the PKer. Your Twisted Bow is permanently lost — there is no retrieval from Death's Office or Gravestone for Wilderness PvP deaths. If you die unskulled, you keep 3 items (Protect Item makes it 4). If Twisted Bow is one of your protected items, you keep it. Otherwise, it drops. Always check 'Items Kept on Death' before entering Wilderness with valuable items.
title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

text:
Q: How much does it cost to reclaim a Twisted Bow from Death's Office?
A: For items worth 10M+, Death's retrieval fee is approximately 0.05%-0.1% of the item's Grand Exchange value, capped at 500K per item. For a Twisted Bow (valued at ~900M GP), expect a retrieval fee of roughly 450K-500K GP. This fee can be reduced by sacrificing lower-value items from your gravestone to offset the cost. Items stay in Death's Office indefinitely until reclaimed — they never disappear.
title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

text:
Q: How can I protect my Twisted Bow from being lost?
A: Key prevention tips: (1) Never bring Twisted Bow into Wilderness above level 20. (2) Always check 'Items Kept on Death' screen (equipment tab shield icon) before any bossing trip. (3) Enable the RuneLite 'Death Indicators' plugin to show gravestone timer. (4) At risky bosses, have a teammate bless your gravestone (extends timer to 1 hour). (5) Consider insuring the Twisted Bow if insurance mechanics are ever added (currently not available). (6) Use a Ring of Life if doing content where you might disconnect.
title: OSRS How to Reclaim Twisted Bow When Lost 2026 — Death Mechanics & Item Recovery

1. How Flax Spinning Works 
Take flax to any spinning wheel, click the wheel, and select "Spin Flax." Each flax becomes one bowstring, which gives 15 Crafting XP. Bowstrings are a core component for stringing bows (yew longbows, magic longbows) — Fletchers always need them, creating permanent demand.

5. Alternative Spinning Methods 
Spinning Wool into Balls of Wool 
Wool sells for 30-50 GP per ball. Shearing sheep (Lumbridge or Falador farm) provides free wool. This is more of a F2P method since profit is much lower than bowstrings. Each ball gives 2.5 Crafting XP. 
Sinew into Crossbow Strings (Level 10) 
Sinew from cooked meat can be spun into crossbow strings at level 10 Crafting.

Magic Roots into Magic Strings (Level 19) 
Magic roots (from chopping magic trees) can be spun into magic strings. These sell for more than bowstrings but the supply of magic roots is extremely limited, making this impractical for volume profit.

Buy flax in massive bulk. Place a buy order for 100K-200K flax at a time. You'll get a slightly better price on bulk and won't have to stop your spinning session to buy more.

Q: Is spinning flax good for F2P money making? 
A: Yes, it's one of the best F2P zero-requirement methods. At 96K-115K GP/hr using the Lumbridge Castle spinning wheel, it outperforms most F2P skilling methods at low levels. The only better F2P methods require higher combat stats (Ogress Warriors, Moss Giants) or member skills.

text:
Q: How much profit can you make spinning flax into bowstrings?
A: At current 2026 prices, spinning flax into bowstrings yields approximately 144,000 GP per hour when buying flax (3-5 GP each) and selling bowstrings (80-120 GP each). At 1,500 bowstrings per hour, revenue is ~150K GP with ~6K GP in flax costs. If you pick your own flax at Seers' Village, profit increases to the full 150K GP/hr, but the overall rate drops because picking flax takes additional time.
title: OSRS Rune Spinning Profit Guide 2026 — Flax to Bowstrings, GP/hr & Best Locations

text:
Q: Where is the best place to spin flax in OSRS?
A: Seers' Village is the best spinning location. The spinning wheel is only a few tiles from the bank, making it the fastest flax-to-bowstring loop in the game. Neitiznot (after Fremennik Isles) is equally close but requires quest completion. For F2P players, Lumbridge Castle (2nd floor) is the best option — the spinning wheel is upstairs from the bank chest.
title: OSRS Rune Spinning Profit Guide 2026 — Flax to Bowstrings, GP/hr & Best Locations

text:
Q: Is spinning flax a good money maker for new OSRS players?
A: Yes, it's one of the best zero-requirement money makers for brand new accounts. Spinning flax requires level 1 Crafting and no quests. With just 10-20K GP starting capital to buy flax, you can begin earning 100K+ GP/hr immediately. It's especially good for F2P players who have fewer money-making options available.
title: OSRS Rune Spinning Profit Guide 2026 — Flax to Bowstrings, GP/hr & Best Locations

text:
Q: Can you spin flax for profit on an ironman account?
A: Yes, but you must pick the flax yourself. Ironmen can pick flax at Seers' Village (28 flax per inventory, respawns quickly), spin it at the nearby spinning wheel, and sell the bowstrings to a general store for ~50-60 GP each or use them for Fletching. Combined with Fletching, bowstrings turn into strung bows which can be alched for profit — this is a classic ironman crafting/money-making loop.

Boss Guide — Updated June 2026 Soloing God Wars Dungeon bosses is a rite of passage for OSRS PvMers. This guide walks you through soloing General Graardor (Bandos) and Kree'arra (Armadyl) as a beginner, covering gear setups, kill count strategies, trip planning, and profit comparisons for 2026.

1. God Wars Dungeon — The Basics 
God Wars Dungeon is located north of Trollheim. To enter, you need either 60 Strength or 60 Agility to move the boulder at the entrance, plus a rope on your first visit (the rope stays permanently). Inside, four factions are locked in eternal combat: Bandos, Armadyl, Zamorak, and Saradomin.

Each boss room requires 40 kill count (KC) of that faction's followers to enter. KC is earned by killing creatures in the main dungeon area. Once inside the boss room, the boss spawns every 90 seconds along with three minions. KC resets when you leave the dungeon , so plan your trips to maximize kills per entry.

8. Common Mistakes at GWD 
Forgetting protection prayer on entry. Bandos can hit 30+ through melee instantly. If you enter without Protect from Melee, you risk a KO before you can react. 
Not bringing a teleport out. GWD is deep in the dungeon.

text:
Melee, you risk a KO before you can react. 
Not bringing a teleport out. GWD is deep in the dungeon. Without a teleport tab, you must run through aggressive creatures to escape. Always bring a one-click teleport. 
Killing the wrong minion first. At Bandos, the mage minion (Steelwill) hits through prayer. Killing him first saves more HP than any other single action. 
Standing in melee range of minions. The minions stack up.

Standing in melee range of minions. The minions stack up. If all three attack you simultaneously with Graardor, you can get comboed. Position yourself in a corner to limit how many can reach you. 
Not healing between kills. The 90-second respawn window is your healing opportunity. Don't just stand there — use Guthan's or Blood Barrage to top off. 
Forgetting KC when world-hopping. If you hop worlds, your KC resets.

Forgetting KC when world-hopping. If you hop worlds, your KC resets. Only hop inside the boss room if you can survive the boss while waiting for a new world. 

9. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: What stats do I need to solo Bandos? 
A: Minimum recommended stats for solo Bandos: 80+ Attack, 85+ Strength, 80+ Defence, 70+ Prayer (for Piety), and 80+ Hitpoints. You can solo with lower stats but will get fewer kills per trip.

Q: How do I extend my trips at GWD?

text:
Q: How do I get kill count for God Wars Dungeon?
A: You need 40 kill count (KC) of the appropriate faction to enter each boss room. For Bandos, kill goblins (level 12-15), hobgoblins, or ogres in the main GWD area. For Armadyl, kill aviansies or spiritual rangers/mages. The fastest method is killing low-level creatures near the entrance — goblins for Bandos, spiritual creatures for Armadyl. KC resets when you leave the dungeon.

Start with Bandos, learn the GWD rhythm, then branch out to other bosses.

3. The Ectofuntus — Step by Step 
The Ectofuntus is a large temple north of Port Phasmatys. It requires significant running between stations, but the 4× XP multiplier makes it worth the effort. 
Travel to Port Phasmatys: Walk through Morytania (requires Priest in Peril). Alternatively, use an Ectophial (obtained from completing Ghosts Ahoy).

Enter the Ectofuntus dungeon: Go down the trapdoor just outside the temple. 
Grind bones into bonemeal: Use your bones on the Bone Grinder in the basement. You'll need to click each bone individually — this is the slowest step. 
Collect slime: Use an empty bucket on the green slime pool (also in the basement). 
Go upstairs: Climb the stairs to the Ectofuntus altar on the ground floor.

text:
Q: What is the cheapest way to train Prayer in F2P OSRS?
A: The cheapest method is killing Hill Giants in Edgeville Dungeon for their Big Bones (each gives 15 base XP) and using them at the Ectofuntus for 4x XP (60 XP per bone). Hill Giants are free to kill, drop Big Bones 100% of the time, and also drop limpwurt roots which can be sold for additional GP. Alternatively, buying Big Bones from the Grand Exchange (~180 GP each) and using them at the Ectofuntus costs approximately 39M GP to reach level 99 — dramatically cheaper than burying them directly (~156M GP).
title: OSRS How to Train Prayer Cheap F2P 2026 — Big Bones, Ectofuntus & Cost Analysis

text:
Q: How does the Ectofuntus work for Prayer training?
A: The Ectofuntus is located north of Port Phasmatys and gives 4x the base XP of bones. To use it: (1) Grind bones into bonemeal using the Bone Grinder in the basement, (2) Fill a bucket with slime from the green pool in the same area, (3) Offer the bonemeal and slime bucket at the Ectofuntus altar upstairs. Each offering grants 4x the normal burying XP. Big Bones give 60 XP per offering instead of 15. The process is slower than burying but 4x more efficient per bone.
title: OSRS How to Train Prayer Cheap F2P 2026 — Big Bones, Ectofuntus & Cost Analysis

text:
Q: What is the best bone type for F2P Prayer training?
A: Big Bones are the best value for F2P players. They provide 15 base XP, cost ~180 GP each on the GE, and have the best XP/GP ratio (0.33 XP/GP at Ectofuntus). Regular bones give 4.5 XP but only cost ~100 GP, making them slightly worse value (0.18 XP/GP). Baby Dragon Bones give 30 XP but cost ~700 GP each (0.17 XP/GP) — worse value than Big Bones. For pure F2P, stick with Big Bones.
title: OSRS How to Train Prayer Cheap F2P 2026 — Big Bones, Ectofuntus & Cost Analysis

text:
Q: How much does 99 Prayer cost in F2P?
A: Reaching level 99 Prayer requires 13,034,431 XP. Using Big Bones at the Ectofuntus (60 XP each): you need ~217,241 bones, costing ~39M GP at current prices. Burying Big Bones directly (15 XP each): you need ~868,962 bones, costing ~156M GP. Using Dragon Bones at a Gilded Altar (members only): you need ~51,724 bones, costing ~134M GP. The Ectofuntus with Big Bones is by far the cheapest path to 99 for F2P.

🦕 Fossil Island — Updated June 2026 Fossil Island is the centerpiece of OSRS Hunter training. From passive XP birdhouses to the best active training method in the game (Herbiboar), everything high-level about Hunter lives on this island. This guide walks you through unlocking Fossil Island, navigating its prehistoric ecosystem, and setting up your Hunter training for maximum efficiency in 2026.

3. Birdhouses — Your Passive XP Engine 
Birdhouses are the single best reason to unlock Fossil Island early. At level 5 Hunter you can start running birdhouses every 50 minutes for massive passive XP. By the time you reach 99 Hunter, birdhouses alone will have provided 3-5 million XP — for free, while you do other things.

text:
99 Hunter, birdhouses alone will have provided 3-5 million XP — for free, while you do other things. 
Birdhouse Run Setup 
Inventory: 4 logs (appropriate tier for your level), 4 seeds (matching tier), 1 hammer, 1 chisel, Digsite Pendant 
Route: Teleport to Fossil Island → Verdant Valley birdhouse (northeast) → Mushroom Forest birdhouse #1 (center) → Mushroom Forest birdhouse #2 (south) → Tar Swamp birdhouse (south) → teleport out

text:
se #1 (center) → Mushroom Forest birdhouse #2 (south) → Tar Swamp birdhouse (south) → teleport out 
Process: At each spot, dismantle the old birdhouse (empty it for XP + nests), use a log on the birdhouse space, add seeds, and move to the next spot. Takes 20-30 seconds per birdhouse. 
Collection Rewards: Bird nests containing rings (up to 500K GP for a dragonstone ring), tree seeds (ranarr through magic), and always a raw bird meat (negligible).

4. Herbiboar — The Crown Jewel of Fossil Island 
Herbiboar is unlocked automatically when you reach level 80 Hunter and 31 Herblore. You track a massive boar through the Mushroom Forest by inspecting disturbed objects along its path. Each tracking sequence involves 6-10 object inspections before you catch the boar.

Bush Forest edges/shrubs 2,150

Each completed trail grants ~18K-22K Hunter XP total and 2-4 grimy herbs (type determined by your Herblore level).

While training on Fossil Island, you'll occasionally find unidentified fossils (small/medium/large/rare) from activities like chopping sulliuscep mushrooms, mining volcanic ash, or killing wyverns.

Completing the entire fossil display (all exhibits) rewards roughly 120K total XP in lamps — a nice bonus on the road to maxing.

7. Common Mistakes on Fossil Island 
Not bringing a Digsite Pendant. The pendant is the only fast teleport to Fossil Island. Without it, you walk from the Digsite every time — a 2-minute run each way. 
Forgetting seeds for birdhouses. You need specific seed types for each birdhouse tier (barley for regular, hammerstone for oak, etc.). Always check before teleporting. 
Ignoring Herbiboar until 99 Hunter. Start Herbiboar exactly at level 80.

8. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: What is the minimum level to start Fossil Island content? 
A: Level 5 Hunter (birdhouse runs). You can start birdhouses immediately after Bone Voyage.

ontent?

Q: Can Ironmen benefit from Fossil Island? 
A: Absolutely — Fossil Island is essential for Ironmen. Bird nests (from birdhouses) are the primary source of tree seeds for Farming.

The island is one of the most Ironman-friendly pieces of content in OSRS. 

Q: How do I get Digsite Pendants? 
A: Enchant a ruby necklace using Lvl-3 Enchant (level 49 Magic).

Q: How do I get Digsite Pendants? 
A: Enchant a ruby necklace using Lvl-3 Enchant (level 49 Magic). The spell requires 1 ruby necklace and 1 cosmic rune. Digsite pendants have 5 charges each and can be recharged by using them on the barge's navigation wheel. You can also use the POH Jewellery Box (Con 81) for infinite teleports.

text:
Q: How do I unlock Fossil Island in OSRS?
A: Complete the Bone Voyage quest. Requirements: 100 kudos from the Varrock Museum (earned via the Natural History Quiz, cleaning finds, and completing quests like The Digsite). After earning 100 kudos, speak to the barge guard north of the Digsite to start Bone Voyage. The quest takes ~15 minutes and unlocks the entire island.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Dinosaur Hunting 2026 — Fossil Island, Herbiboar & Hunter Guide

text:
Q: What Hunter creatures are on Fossil Island?
A: Fossil Island's Hunter ecosystem includes: birdhouses (level 5+, passive XP via runs), Herbiboar (level 80+, best XP in the game at 140K-180K/hr), Drift Net Fishing (level 44+, combines Fishing + Hunter), and salamanders in the swamp. While not technically dinosaur hunting, these prehistoric-themed activities form the core of high-level Hunter training.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Dinosaur Hunting 2026 — Fossil Island, Herbiboar & Hunter Guide

text:
Q: What is the fastest way to get 100 kudos for Bone Voyage?
A: Do the Natural History Quiz in the Varrock Museum basement (28 kudos, 5 minutes). Complete The Digsite quest (30 kudos, required anyway). Complete specific quests for their kudos rewards: Demon Slayer (5), Rune Mysteries (5), Shield of Arrav (5), etc. Clean 5-10 finds from the Digsite specimen rocks for the remaining kudos. Total time: 1-2 hours.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Dinosaur Hunting 2026 — Fossil Island, Herbiboar & Hunter Guide

text:
Q: Can I hunt actual dinosaurs in OSRS?
A: Not as a traditional Hunter activity. While Fossil Island has prehistoric themes and you can discover rare fossils that assemble into skeletal displays in the Varrock Museum, there is no 'catch dinosaurs with box traps' activity. The term 'dinosaur hunting' in the OSRS community generally refers to the full Fossil Island Hunter ecosystem: birdhouses, salamanders in the tar swamp, and Herbiboar tracking.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Dinosaur Hunting 2026 — Fossil Island, Herbiboar & Hunter Guide

1. Why Fairy Rings Are Essential 
Fairy rings provide instant, free teleportation to over 50 locations across the entire OSRS map.

2. Step 1: Complete Lost City (Partial Access) 
Requirement Detail

Lost City Walkthrough 
Start: Talk to the warrior near Lumbridge Swamp (east side, near Draynor Village).
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
City Walkthrough 
Start: Talk to the warrior near Lumbridge Swamp (east side, near Draynor Village). He tells you about Zanaris and the Dramen Tree. 
Find the Leprechaun: Head into Lumbridge Swamp. Find the tree stump in the center of the swamp (near where the dungeon entrance is). Search it to find a leprechaun. Ask about Zanaris. 
Cut the Dramen Tree: The leprechaun tells you to cut a specific tree nearby WITHOUT any weapon or shield equipped. Unequip everything (only bare hands/fists).
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
cific tree nearby WITHOUT any weapon or shield equipped. Unequip everything (only bare hands/fists). Right-click the tree and select "Chop." A level 101 Tree Spirit attacks. 
Fight the Tree Spirit: Re-equip your weapon and armor. The Tree Spirit hits up to 12 damage and has roughly 85 HP. Protect from Melee prayer works. Kill it with melee (it's immune to ranged and magic). It drops the Dramen Branch. 
Craft the Dramen Staff: Take the branch to Lumbridge Castle bank.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
gic). It drops the Dramen Branch. 
Craft the Dramen Staff: Take the branch to Lumbridge Castle bank. Use a knife on the branch (requires 31 Crafting) to create a Dramen Staff. 
Enter Zanaris: Bank all weapons and armor (Entrana's restrictions apply). Equip ONLY the Dramen Staff. Travel to Port Sarim, take the boat to Entrana. On Entrana, walk to the northwest corner and find the shed with the trapdoor. Enter it to reach Zanaris. Quest complete.

4. Step 3: Start A Fairy Tale Part II — Cure a Queen (Don't Finish!) 
This is the key trick every OSRS veteran knows: you do NOT need to complete Fairy Tale Part II to get the full fairy ring benefit. Simply STARTING the quest is enough. 
How to Start Fairy Tale Part II: 
Complete A Fairy Tale Part I. 
Talk to the Fairy Godfather in Zanaris (he's in the main fairy city area).
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
ete A Fairy Tale Part I. 
Talk to the Fairy Godfather in Zanaris (he's in the main fairy city area). Select the dialogue option to begin Part II. 
He assigns you the quest and gives you items. That's it — the quest is now started. 
From this moment forward, you can use fairy rings WITHOUT equipping the Dramen Staff. The staff becomes optional. 
Why this works: The quest's storyline involves the Fairy Queen granting you permanent access to the fairy ring network.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

quest's storyline involves the Fairy Queen granting you permanent access to the fairy ring network. The game engine checks whether you've started Part II (not whether you've finished it). This is one of the most valuable early-game unlocks — freeing up your weapon slot while still using fairy rings. 
Do I Ever Need to Finish Part II? 
Only if you want the quest cape, the XP lamp reward, or access to the Fairy Resistance Hideout (used in A Kingdom Divided quest).

Q: Can I use fairy rings on F2P worlds? 
A: No. Fairy rings are a members-only feature. Lost City, Fairy Tale quests, and the fairy ring network are all members content. However, once unlocked on a members account, fairy rings remain functional if you go F2P (you retain your membership benefits until your subscription expires). F2P-only accounts cannot access fairy rings at all.

text:
Q: How do I unlock Fairy Rings in OSRS?
A: Two levels of access: (1) Partial Access — Complete Lost City quest to obtain a Dramen Staff. Equip the staff and click any fairy ring for full teleport access. (2) Full Access — Complete A Fairy Tale Part I, then START A Fairy Tale Part II (no need to finish it). After starting Part II, you can use fairy rings without equipping the Dramen staff, freeing up your weapon slot. This takes roughly 2-3 hours total from a fresh account.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
Q: Do I need to complete Fairy Tale Part II for staff-free fairy rings?
A: No! You only need to START Fairy Tale Part II — not complete it. After finishing Part I, talk to the Fairy Godfather in Zanaris to begin Part II. Once the quest is started, your character will be able to use fairy rings without equipping the Dramen staff. This is the most common OSRS shortcut — many players never finish Part II but benefit from staff-free fairy rings permanently.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

text:
Q: What are the requirements for Lost City?
A: Lost City requires 31 Crafting and 36 Woodcutting. No quest prerequisites. You must be able to defeat a level 101 Tree Spirit (no prayer, no special attacks — just melee it down). The spirit hits up to 12 damage. Recommended: 40+ combat stats and some food. The quest rewards the Dramen Staff (essential for fairy rings) and access to Zanaris.
title: OSRS How to Unlock Fairy Rings 2026 — Lost City, Fairy Tale I & II Quick Guide

There are 50+ total destinations.

The Abyss is a dangerous but incredibly useful area that provides fast access to every Runecrafting altar in OSRS. This guide covers how to unlock the Abyss through the Mage of Zamorak miniquest, how to use it effectively for Runecrafting, the risks involved, and the abyssal creatures you'll encounter in 2026.

What is the Abyss? 
The Abyss is a mysterious dimension accessible through the Wilderness. It contains rifts to every Runecrafting altar in the game, making it the fastest way to craft runes.
title: OSRS How to Unlock the Abyss Guide 2026

text:
It contains rifts to every Runecrafting altar in the game, making it the fastest way to craft runes. Instead of running to each altar individually, you enter the Abyss and click the corresponding rift to teleport directly to the altar. 
The catch? The Abyss is located in the Wilderness (level 5–6), meaning you can be attacked by other players. Additionally, the Abyss drains your prayer points upon entry and contains aggressive creatures.

You appear in the outer ring of the Abyss

Risk vs Reward Analysis 
The Abyss is in the Wilderness, which means PKers can attack you. Here's a realistic risk assessment:

PKer attacks — players may attack you while running to the Mage of Zamorak or inside the Abyss

Keep pure essence noted or in small stacks

Despite the name, abyssal demons are NOT found in the Abyss. They're found in the Slayer Tower and require 85 Slayer.

PKers gain nothing from killing you except chinchompas — and if you bank frequently, even those are safe. The only thing a PKer can take is the chinchompas in your inventory.

ntly, even those are safe. The only thing a PKer can take is the chinchompas in your inventory. 

7. Advanced Hunter Money Making Tips 
Prioritize 80 Hunter above all else. The 5th trap (or 6th in Wilderness) is the single biggest productivity increase in Hunter. Every level from 1-80 should be aimed at unlocking the next trap tier. 
Complete Western Provinces Hard Diary. The private red chinchompa hunting ground eliminates player competition entirely.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
vinces Hard Diary. The private red chinchompa hunting ground eliminates player competition entirely. Your catch rate increases by 20-30% simply because no one else is stealing your traps or scaring chinchompas. 
Sell chinchompas in batches, not all at once. Dumping 5,000 red chinchompas on the GE simultaneously crashes the price for your own sale. Sell 500-1,000 at a time. 
Chinchompa prices fluctuate with PvM meta. When new ranged-weak bosses release, chinchompa demand spikes.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
hompa prices fluctuate with PvM meta. When new ranged-weak bosses release, chinchompa demand spikes. Monitor OSRS news and stockpile during low-demand periods to sell during peaks. 
Use the Hunter skillcape perk. At 99 Hunter, the skillcape summons a falcon that passively helps catch implings. This makes magpie/ninja impling hunting significantly more profitable post-99. 

8. Common Mistakes 
Bringing valuables to black chin hunting. This is the #1 mistake. PKers at black chins are common.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
Bringing valuables to black chin hunting. This is the #1 mistake. PKers at black chins are common. If you bring your graceful outfit, rune pouch, or cash stack, you will lose them eventually. Bring ONLY box traps. 
Not banking frequently at black chins. If you stay for 2 hours and catch 2,000 chinchompas, a single PK death wipes all your profit. Bank every 15-20 minutes. 
Staying at black chins with a white dot visible. If you see ANY player dot on the minimap, teleport or log out.

black chins with a white dot visible. If you see ANY player dot on the minimap, teleport or log out. Don't wait to see if they're friendly — they're not.

Ignoring Herbiboar as an Ironman. Herbiboar provides a steady stream of ranarr, snapdragon, and torstol herbs that are otherwise difficult to farm in bulk.

9. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: How much GP per hour can I make with Hunter? 
A: Black Chinchompas (73+ Hunter): 1M-3M+ GP/hr but with Wilderness PK risk. Red Chinchompas (63+ Hunter): 400K-825K GP/hr safely. Herbiboar (80+ Hunter): 300K-600K GP/hr plus Herblore XP. Dragon Implings (83+ Hunter): 1M-3M+ GP/hr but extremely rare spawns.

This is the maximum possible and is why black chinchompas (Wilderness) are more profitable than red chinchompas despite similar individual catch rates.

text:
Q: How much GP per hour can I make with Hunter in OSRS?
A: Hunter GP rates vary by level and method: Black Chinchompas (73+ Hunter) earn 1M-3M+ GP/hr in the Wilderness but carry PK risk. Red Chinchompas (63+ Hunter) earn 400K-800K GP/hr safely in Feldip Hills. Herbiboar (80+ Hunter) earns 300K-600K GP/hr in herbs plus Herblore XP. Carnivorous Chinchompas (63+ Hunter) earn 350K-600K GP/hr. Impling hunting varies widely from 100K-1M+ GP/hr depending on impling type.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
Q: What Hunter level do I need for 5 traps?
A: You unlock 1 trap at level 1, 2 traps at level 20, 3 traps at level 40, 4 traps at level 60, and 5 traps at level 80. Additionally, being in the Wilderness grants +1 extra trap, allowing 6 traps at level 80+ in the Wilderness. This is the maximum possible and is why black chinchompas (in the Wilderness) are so much more profitable than red chinchompas despite similar catch rates.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
Q: Is hunting black chinchompas worth the PK risk?
A: For most players, yes — with proper anti-PK precautions. Black chinchompas earn 2-3× more GP/hr than red chinchompas. To minimize risk: bring only box traps (no valuable gear), use a one-click teleport (Ring of Dueling or Ardougne Cloak) that works below level 30 Wilderness, log out immediately when you see a white dot on the minimap, and avoid peak PK hours (evenings and weekends). If you die, you lose only the chinchompas caught since your last bank trip — box traps are cheap to replace.
title: OSRS Hunter Money Making Guide 2026 — Black Chins, Red Chins & Herbiboar

text:
Q: What is the best Hunter method for Ironmen?
A: Herbiboar (80+ Hunter) is the best Hunter method for Ironmen. It provides ~100-140 grimy herbs per hour, which is invaluable for Herblore training. The herbs include ranarr, snapdragon, torstol, and other high-tier herbs that are otherwise difficult to obtain in bulk. Herbiboar also gives ~50K-60K Hunter XP per hour, making it efficient for both skilling and resource gathering. Red chinchompas are the second-best option for Ironmen who need ranged training ammunition.

73 → 99 Black Chinchompas (Wildy) OR Hunter's Rumours 145K–265K Chinchompas = fastest + profit;Rumours = safe 
80 → 99 Herbiboar (AFK alternative) 137K–171K Very low effort + herb income 
⏱️ Time to 99 Estimates 
Fastest path (Black Chinchompas 73+): ~75 hours total 
Safe path (Hunter's Rumours 72+): ~80–90 hours total

Frequently Asked Questions 
What is the fastest way to train Hunter in OSRS? 
Black Chinchompas in the Wilderness (level 73+) offer the highest XP rates: up to 265,000 XP/hr at level 99 with a scout account.

Are Birdhouse runs worth doing? 
Absolutely. Each run takes ~90 seconds of actual playtime every 50 minutes. At high levels using Magic/Redwood birdhouses, this gives 172,800–216,000 effective XP/hr for almost zero effort.

OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner Home / Guides / OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 
OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 
The OSRS game screen looks intimidating at first — 20+ UI elements, chat boxes, menus everywhere. This guide breaks down EVERY interface element and control you need to know, explained in plain English for total newbies.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
EVERY interface element and control you need to know, explained in plain English for total newbies. 
📅 Part of our Weekly Beginner Series — New articles every week to fast-track your progress 

Table of Contents 
Your First Look at the Game Screen 
Main Game Interface Elements 
Camera Controls — Move Your View 
Mouse & Click Controls 
Essential Keyboard Shortcuts 
Inventory & Equipment Tabs 
Minimap & World Map 
Chat Box & Communication 
Settings You Should Change Immediately 
What to Do Next

map & World Map

1. Your First Look at the Game Screen 
When you finish Tutorial Island and see the full game screen for the first time, it's normal to feel overwhelmed. There are numbers, icons, a chat box, a minimap, and menus everywhere. 
Don't panic. You only need to understand about 8 core UI elements to play the game comfortably. Everything else is secondary and you'll learn it naturally over time.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
to play the game comfortably. Everything else is secondary and you'll learn it naturally over time. 
🎮 The Good News 
If you install RuneLite (the free community client), the interface becomes MUCH cleaner and more customizable. We cover RuneLite setup in detail in our RuneLite guide . 

The 8 UI Elements Every Beginner Needs 
Element Location What It Does 
Chat Box Bottom-left Shows game messages, lets you type to other players 
Minimap Top-right Shows nearby terrain, NPC dots, your position
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

s, lets you type to other players 
Minimap Top-right Shows nearby terrain, NPC dots, your position

Equipment Next to Inventory tab Shows what you're wearing/wielding 
Skill Icons Left side (vertical) Click to see XP, click again to open skill guide 
Prayer Book Tab near Inventory Activate prayers (unlocked later) 
Spellbook Tab near Inventory Magic spells (unlocked after certain quests)
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
rayers (unlocked later) 
Spellbook Tab near Inventory Magic spells (unlocked after certain quests) 
Map Icon Minimap top-right corner Opens full world map 

2. Main Game Interface Elements — Explained 
🗨️ The Chat Box (Bottom-Left) 
The chat box is where the game talks to you. It has 5 tabs: 
All — Every message (game, public, private, clan) 
Game — Only game messages (loot, XP, warnings) 
Public — Messages from players near you 
Private — PMs from other players 
Clan — Your clan chat messages
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
— Messages from players near you 
Private — PMs from other players 
Clan — Your clan chat messages 
💡 Chat Tip: Turn Off Public Chat Early 
Public chat can be spammy and sometimes toxic. Right-click the chat box → "Filter" → "Friends" to only see messages from friends. You can always turn it back on later. 

📦 The Inventory (28 Slots) 
Your inventory holds everything you're carrying. 28 slots total. When it's full, you'll need to bank or drop items.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
holds everything you're carrying. 28 slots total. When it's full, you'll need to bank or drop items. 
Stackable items (coins, runes, bolts): multiple stack into 1 slot 
Non-stackable items (food, weapons, armor): each takes 1 slot 
Wearing items : click an item → "Wield" or "Wear" → it moves to Equipment slot 
⚔️ Combat Style Tab (Bottom-Right) 
This is CRITICAL and many beginners miss it. When you have a weapon equipped, a "Combat Options" tab appears.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
RITICAL and many beginners miss it. When you have a weapon equipped, a "Combat Options" tab appears. Here you choose your Attack style : 
Accurate → trains Attack (hit more often) 
Aggressive → trains Strength (hit harder) 
Defensive → trains Defence (get hit less) 
Controlled → trains all three evenly (slowest path) 
⚠️ Common Beginner Mistake 
Many new players never change their combat style and wonder why their Attack stays at level 1 while they have 20 Strength.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
change their combat style and wonder why their Attack stays at level 1 while they have 20 Strength. Check this tab every time you train combat! 

3. Camera Controls — Move Your View 
You need to control the camera to see where you're going, target NPCs, and admire the scenery. Here's how: 
Control Action Notes 
Arrow Keys Pan camera up/down/left/right Easiest method for beginners 
Mouse Wheel Click + Drag Rotate camera freely Fastest method once you get used to it
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
or beginners 
Mouse Wheel Click + Drag Rotate camera freely Fastest method once you get used to it 
Page Up / Page Down Zoom in / Zoom out Helpful for ranged/magic 
Home Key Reset camera behind character Very useful when you get "lost" 
Minimap Click Rotate camera toward that direction Quick way to look somewhere 
📷 Camera Settings You Should Change 
Go to Settings (wrench icon) → Display and enable: 
"Camera zoom adjustment" — lets you zoom much further out (game-changer for bossing)
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
and enable: 
"Camera zoom adjustment" — lets you zoom much further out (game-changer for bossing) 
"Rotate minimap" — minimap stays oriented to your camera (or disable for north-always-up) 

4. Mouse & Click Controls 
OSRS is a point-and-click game. Understanding what different clicks do will save you hundreds of misclicks.

text:
a point-and-click game. Understanding what different clicks do will save you hundreds of misclicks. 
Left Click vs Right Click 
Action Left Click Right Click (Options Menu) 
NPC (e.g., Cow) Attack (if combat ready) Examine, Talk-to, Follow, Attack 
Item on ground Take it Examine, Take, (if shift-click: Drop)

Important Mouse Settings 
⚙️ "Shift-Click Drop" Is a Must-Enable Setting 
Go to Settings → Gameplay → Controls and enable "Shift-click to drop items" . This lets you quickly drop items by Shift+clicking them instead of right-click → Drop. Saves hours of inventory management time.

5. Essential Keyboard Shortcuts (Memorize These) 
You CAN play OSRS with just mouse clicks. But these keyboard shortcuts will make your life 10x easier. Memorize the top 5 first. 
Key Action Priority 
Esc Close any open interface 🔴 Essential 
Spacebar Continue dialogue / Open settings 🔴 Essential

6. Inventory & Equipment Tabs — Manage Your Gear 
Equipment Slots (10 Total) 
When you click the Equipment tab (helmet icon), you'll see 10 slots.

How to Check Your Stats (Gear Bonuses) 
Click the Equipment tab → "Show Equipment Stats" button .

How to Check Your Stats (Gear Bonuses) 
Click the Equipment tab → "Show Equipment Stats" button . This shows your: 
Attack bonuses (stab, slash, crush, magic, ranged) 
Defence bonuses (all styles) 
Other bonuses (strength bonus, prayer bonus, melee accuracy) 
As a beginner, the most important number is your Max Hit in the Melee section — it tells you the highest damage you can deal in one hit.

7. Minimap & World Map — Don't Get Lost 
The Minimap (Top-Right Corner) 
The minimap shows what's immediately around you. Here's how to read it: 
Yellow dot = You (always in center) 
Red dots = NPCs / Monsters (potential enemies) 
Blue dots = Other players 
Green dots = Fifeplaces, anvils, etc.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
PCs / Monsters (potential enemies) 
Blue dots = Other players 
Green dots = Fifeplaces, anvils, etc. (interactive objects) 
Door icon = Building entrance 
Minimap icon (top-right of minimap) = Click to open full World Map 
The World Map (Hotkey: Accented character / click minimap icon) 
The full world map is VAST. In F2P it shows Misthalin (the starting continent). In Members it shows the entire world of Gielinor.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
n F2P it shows Misthalin (the starting continent). In Members it shows the entire world of Gielinor. 
🗺️ How to Use the World Map Efficiently 
Open the map (click the minimap icon or press the accented char key) 
Click on a location name on the left panel to center the map there 
Right-click a location → "Add marker" to create a custom waypoint 
Close the map — a small arrow appears on your screen pointing toward the marked location 
Follow the arrow until you arrive!

arrow appears on your screen pointing toward the marked location 
Follow the arrow until you arrive! 

💡 Pro Tip: Mark Your Bank 
As soon as you visit a bank for the first time (Lumbridge castle has a bank chest on the second floor), mark it on your world map. You'll be going back there constantly in your first weeks. 

8. Chat Box & Communication — Stay Safe 
How to Talk to Other Players 
Press Enter to open the chat box, type your message, press Enter again to send.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
lk to Other Players 
Press Enter to open the chat box, type your message, press Enter again to send. Your message appears in the Public tab (if enabled) and other nearby players can see it. 
⚠️ CRITICAL: Chat Safety Rules 
OSRS has scammers, just like anywhere online. NEVER click a link sent in chat. NEVER share your password. NEVER give your items to someone promising to "double them" or "bless them." These are ALL scams. Real players will never ask for your password.

(In some worlds) Teleports you to a bank (not in standard worlds)

Send a private message 
Report a player for rule violations 
Note: Most chat commands aren't needed as a beginner. Focus on playing the game. You'll learn these naturally if you join a clan later.

FAQ — Game Interface & Controls 
How do I rotate the camera? Click and hold the middle mouse button (or press and hold the arrow keys ) to rotate the camera around your character. You can also hold Right-click + Left-click simultaneously on mobile or laptop touchpads. The camera angle affects what you can see in the game world.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
aneously on mobile or laptop touchpads. The camera angle affects what you can see in the game world. 
What's the difference between Fixed and Resizable mode? Fixed mode is the classic OSRS layout (small game window, fixed interface panels). Resizable mode lets you resize the game window to fill your screen — highly recommended for modern monitors. Resizable (modern) mode also has more keybind options and smoother camera control.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
modern monitors. Resizable (modern) mode also has more keybind options and smoother camera control. 
How do I set up keybinds for quick access? Open Settings → Controls → Keybinds . The most important keybinds to set: F1 (inventory), F2 (equipment), F3 (prayer), F4 (magic), F5 (combat options), Escape (log out). This lets you switch panels instantly without clicking tabs. 
What are chat commands and how do I use them? Type :: followed by a command in the chat box.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
tabs. 
What are chat commands and how do I use them? Type :: followed by a command in the chat box. Useful commands: ::stats (open skill stats), ::bank (open bank if near a bank chest), ::toggle (toggle items on/off), ::level (check your level). These are client commands, not cheats — they're built into the official client. 
How do I use the Quest Journal efficiently? Click the shield icon in the bottom-right tab (or press Alt+Q ).
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
use the Quest Journal efficiently? Click the shield icon in the bottom-right tab (or press Alt+Q ). The journal shows all quests with colored dots: Red = not started, Yellow = in progress, Green = completed. Right-click a quest name for a quick summary. Completed quests show a completion time and date. 
What does the "Wilderness Level" mean on the minimap? When you enter the Wilderness (north of Varrock), your minimap shows a Wilderness Level (1–53). This indicates how deep you are.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
s (north of Varrock), your minimap shows a Wilderness Level (1–53). This indicates how deep you are. At Wilderness Level 1, players within 3 combat levels of you can attack you. At Level 50, ANYONE can attack you. The deeper you go, the more dangerous. Always check before entering. 
How do I use the Grouping System? The Grouping icon (two people icon, bottom-right) lets you form groups with other players. Groups are needed for some activities (Raids, Chambers of Xeric, Theater of Blood).
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
h other players. Groups are needed for some activities (Raids, Chambers of Xeric, Theater of Blood). For solo play, you don't need it. But joining a clan (via the Clan Chat tab) is highly recommended for getting help and finding groups. 
What is RuneLite and should I use it? RuneLite is a third-party client with hundreds of plugins that enhance the game.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
nd should I use it? RuneLite is a third-party client with hundreds of plugins that enhance the game. Popular plugins: GE Price Checker (shows item values), Inventory Setups (switch gear sets with one click), XP Tracker (track your skill XP rates), Nightmare Zone (auto-absorption tracking). Recommended for all players. Download from runelite.net. 
How do I move the minimap or resize panels? In Resizable mode , you can click and drag the edges of interface panels to resize them.
title: OSRS Game Interface & Controls Guide 2026 — Master the OSRS UI as a Beginner

text:
ize panels? In Resizable mode , you can click and drag the edges of interface panels to resize them. Right-click a panel tab and select "Detatch" to move it freely. To reset everything to default: Settings → Reset → Interface. Note: Some panel positions save automatically between sessions.

OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026 
Ironman Smithing Skill Training Updated 2026 
Smithing is one of the most useful skills for an Ironman account in OSRS — it lets you craft your own armour and weapons, and the Blast Furnace is the fastest XP method available.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
ets you craft your own armour and weapons, and the Blast Furnace is the fastest XP method available. This guide covers every stage from level 1 to 99 specifically for Ironmen, including how to obtain ores, the best Blast Furnace strategy, and gold bar smiting for the final push to 99. 

Ironman vs Regular Smithing — Key Differences 
For regular players, Smithing typically involves buying ores on the Grand Exchange and processing them.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
regular players, Smithing typically involves buying ores on the Grand Exchange and processing them. Ironmen cannot do this — every ore must be obtained through Mining, monster drops, or other in-game sources. This makes planning your Smithing training route critically important for resource management. 
Mine ores yourself (time-intensive but sustainable). 
Get bars from monster drops (limited supply). 
Blast Furnace still works for Ironmen — you just need to bring your own ores.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
ops (limited supply). 
Blast Furnace still works for Ironmen — you just need to bring your own ores. 
Gold bars are the fastest XP method at high levels — gold ore is abundant via mining. 
Completing Elemental Workshop quests gives Smithing XP without consuming materials. 

Early Levels: 1–40 
The early levels of Smithing can be knocked out quickly with a combination of quest rewards and basic smelting.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
evels of Smithing can be knocked out quickly with a combination of quest rewards and basic smelting. Here is the recommended approach: 
Quest XP Rewards for Early Smithing 
Doric's Quest: 1,300 Smithing XP — unlocks at level 1, requires just some ores. Get to level 10 instantly. 
The Knight's Sword: 12,725 Smithing XP — gets you from level 1 to approximately level 29 in one quest.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
e Knight's Sword: 12,725 Smithing XP — gets you from level 1 to approximately level 29 in one quest. 
Elemental Workshop I: 5,000 Smithing XP 
Elemental Workshop II: 7,500 Smithing XP 

By completing The Knight's Sword and Doric's Quest, you skip to approximately level 30 without smelting a single ore. This is the single most time-efficient start to Ironman Smithing. 
Level 1–15 (or after quests): Smelt bronze or iron bars. Mine copper and tin from Lumbridge mines.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
Level 1–15 (or after quests): Smelt bronze or iron bars. Mine copper and tin from Lumbridge mines. 
Level 15–30: Smelt iron bars. Iron ore is abundant and iron bars can be used to make useful items. 
Level 30–40: Smelt steel bars (requires 1 iron ore + 2 coal). Steel equipment is useful for mid-game combat. 
Level 40+: Begin considering Blast Furnace for mithril, adamant, and rune bars.

or mid-game combat. 
Level 40+: Begin considering Blast Furnace for mithril, adamant, and rune bars.

Blast Furnace — Best Ironman XP Method 
The Blast Furnace in Keldagrim is the best Smithing XP method available in OSRS. It halves the coal required to smelt bars and processes bars faster than a regular furnace. For Ironmen, this means significantly less coal needs to be mined compared to using a standard furnace.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
Ironmen, this means significantly less coal needs to be mined compared to using a standard furnace. 
Blast Furnace Requirements 
30 Smithing (or higher for better bars) 
Starting Keldagrim quest complete (The Giant Dwarf, or simply enter Keldagrim by boat) 
Ore stockpiled from Mining 
1,000–2,500 GP for the hourly Blast Furnace fee (negligible for Ironmen) 
Recommended: Ice Gloves or a bucket of water (to handle hot bars efficiently)

Gold Bars — The Fastest XP Method 
Smelting gold bars at the Blast Furnace with Goldsmith Gauntlets equipped (obtained from Family Crest quest) gives 56.2 XP per bar instead of the normal 22.5 XP. This results in XP rates of 300,000+ per hour — far higher than any other Smithing method.

Where Ironmen Get Gold Ore 
Gold ore is the main bottleneck for Ironmen using this method. Here are the best sources: 
Arzinian Mine (Between a Rock quest required) — After completing the quest, you can mine from 180 gold ore rocks simultaneously. This is by far the best gold ore source for Ironmen. 
Crafting Guild — Requires level 40 Crafting and a brown apron. Contains several gold rocks.

text:
ronmen. 
Crafting Guild — Requires level 40 Crafting and a brown apron. Contains several gold rocks. 
Brimhaven Resource Area — Accessible with a fee (GP or ticket). Contains several gold rocks. 
Shilo Village Mine — Good density of gold rocks, requires Shilo Village quest. 

Anvil Methods — Alternative Smithing XP 
While the Blast Furnace is generally superior, anvil methods are useful for Ironmen who want to create useful equipment rather than just bars.

ior, anvil methods are useful for Ironmen who want to create useful equipment rather than just bars. Smithing items at an anvil gives additional XP on top of the smelting XP.

Cannonballs deserve special mention — they are extremely useful for Ironmen who use the dwarf multicannon for Slayer tasks. Smithing cannonballs uses steel bars (2 bars = 4 cannonballs) and gives modest XP while supplying cannon ammunition you would otherwise need to buy.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

cannonballs) and gives modest XP while supplying cannon ammunition you would otherwise need to buy. 

Ironman Smithing Tips 
Complete The Knight's Sword quest first — it saves hours of early Smithing training. 
Always use Goldsmith Gauntlets at the Blast Furnace for gold bars — the XP difference is enormous. 
The Arzinian Mine from Between a Rock quest is the best gold ore source in the game for Ironmen. 
Smith cannonballs alongside other Smithing training to maintain a supply of cannon ammo.
title: OSRS Ironman 1-99 Smithing Guide 2026

text:
r Ironmen. 
Smith cannonballs alongside other Smithing training to maintain a supply of cannon ammo. 
Use the Varrock Armour (Hard diary reward) to occasionally smelt two bars at once — this is a passive XP boost. 
The Smithing cape perk allows you to smelt runite ore without needing any coal — excellent for late game. 
If stuck on ores, consider fighting monsters that drop bars directly — Fire Giants, Dark Beasts, and others drop various metal bars.
